sa_verse: वारमुख्यास्तु तच्छ्रुत्वा वनं प्रविविशुर्महत्। आश्रमस्याविदूरेऽस्मिन् यत्नं कुर्वन्ति दर्शने॥ ऋषेः पुत्रस्य धीरस्य नित्यमाश्रमवासिनः। पितुः स नित्यसंतुष्टो नातिचक्राम चाश्रमात्॥
en: In accordance with the instructions, the courtezans entered that great forest; and remaining at some distance from the hermitage, endeavoured to meet with the sober son of the saint ever dwelling in the woods. And satisfied with serving his sire, he never strayed from the hermitage.

sa_verse: न तेन जन्मप्रभृति दृष्टपूर्वं तपस्विना। स्त्री वा पुमान् वा यच्चान्यत् सत्त्वं नगरराष्ट्रजम्॥
en: Since his birth he had never before seen men and women, or any other creatures living in cities and towns.

sa_verse: ततः कदाचित् तं देशमाजगाम यदृच्छया। विभाण्डकसुतस्तत्र ताश्चपश्यद् वराङ्गनाः॥
en: It came to pass that on one occasion, walking about at will, Vibhandaka's son came to that spot and beheld the courtezans.

sa_verse: ताश्चित्रवेषाः प्रमदा गायन्त्यो मधुरस्वरम्। ऋषिपुत्रमुपागम्य सर्वा वचनमब्रुवन्॥
en: Colourfully attired, and singing sweet songs, the women came to the saint's son and said.

sa_verse: कस्त्वं किं वर्तसे ब्रह्मज्ञातुमिच्छामहे वयम्। एकस्त्वं विजने दूरे वने चरसि शंस नः॥
en: Who are you? And where are you going, O Brahman? We wish to learn all this. And why is it that you range alone this far-off forest?

sa_verse: अदृष्टरूपास्तास्तेन काम्यरूपा वने स्त्रियः। हार्दात्तस्य मतिर्जाता आख्यातुं पितरं स्वकम्॥
en: Beholding these beautiful damsels never seen before, he from delight, hastened to inform them of his lineage.

sa_verse: पिता विभाण्डकोऽस्माकं तस्याहं सुत औरसः। ऋष्यशृङ्ग इति ख्यातं नाम कर्म च मे भुवि॥
en: My father is Vibhăndaka; and I am his son. My name is Rşyasſiga and my occupation is known the world over.

sa_verse: इहाश्रमपदोऽस्माकं समीपे शुभदर्शनाः। करिष्ये वोऽत्र पूजां वै सर्वेषां विधिपूर्वकम्॥
en: This auspicious hermitage hard by belongs to us; and there I shall receive you all in due form O' lovely ones! .

sa_verse: ऋषिपुत्रवचः श्रुत्वा सर्वासां मतिरास वै। तदाश्रमपदं द्रष्टुं जग्मुः सर्वास्ततोऽङ्गनाः॥
en: Hearing the words of the saint's son, they all consented, and the women went to behold that asylum.

sa_verse: गतानां तु ततः पूजामृषिपुत्रश्चकार ह। इदमर्ध्यमिदं पाद्यमिदं मूलं फलं च नः॥
en: When they had come there, the saint's son received them hospitably, saying, 'Here is Arghya,' 'Here is water for washing the feet,' 'Here are fruits and roots.'

sa_verse: प्रतिगृह्य तु तां पूजां सर्वा एव समुत्सुकाः। ऋषेीताश्च शीघ्रं तु गमनाय मतिं दधुः॥
en: And thereupon they readily received his hospitality. And actuated by the fear of the saint, Vibhāndaka, they bent their minds upon departing soon.

sa_verse: अस्माकमपि मुख्यानि फलानीमानि हे द्विज । गृहाण विप्र भद्रं ते भक्षयस्व च मा चिरम्॥
en: They said, Do you also, O twice-born one, receive from us these excellent fruits! O Vipra! good betide you, eat them without delay.

sa_verse: ततस्तास्तं समालिङ्गय सर्वा हर्षसमन्विताः। मोदकान् प्रददुस्तस्मै भक्ष्यांश्च विविधाञ्शुमान्॥
en: Thereupon, embracing him joyfully, they gave him sweetmeats and various kinds of savoury viands.

sa_verse: तानि चास्वाद्य तेजस्वी फलानीति स्म मन्यते। अनास्वादितपूर्वाणि वने नित्यनिवासिनाम्॥
en: And tasting those things, the glorious one took them for fruits, never tasted before by the dwellers of the forest.

sa_verse: आपृच्छय च तदा विप्रं व्रतचर्यां निवेद्य च। गच्छन्ति स्मापदेशात्ता भीतास्तस्य पितुः स्त्रियः॥
en: Then, having accosted him the women, feigning the observance of some vow, went away, inspired with the fear of his father.

sa_verse: गतासु तासु सर्वासु काश्यपस्यात्मजो द्विजः। अस्वस्थहृदयश्चासीद् दुःखाच्च परिवर्तते॥
en: And when they had gone, that twice-born one, Kasyapa's son, became sad, and suffered from grief of heart and became restless due to sadness.

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वैव च ततो विप्रमायान्तं हृष्टमानसाः॥ उपसृत्य ततः सर्वास्तास्तमूचुरिदं वचः। एह्याश्रमपदं सौम्य अस्माकमिति चाब्रुवन् ॥
en: As soon as they observed him coming, they came forward, and said, Do you, O Brāhmaṇa, come into our hermitage.

sa_verse: चित्राण्यत्र बहूनि स्युर्मूलानि च फलानि च। तत्राप्येष विशेषेण विधिर्हि भविता ध्रुवम्॥
en: There are in that asylum diverse kinds of fruits and roots; and there you will surely feed your fill.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा तु वचनं तासां सर्वासां हृदयङ्गमम्। गमनाय मतिं चक्रे तं च निन्युस्तथा स्त्रियः॥
en: Thereupon hearing those words of theirs capable of influencing the heart, he became bent upon going, and the women brought him away.

sa_verse: तत्र चानीयमाने तु विप्रे तस्मिन् महात्मनि । ववर्ष सहसा देवो जगत् प्रह्लादयंस्तदा॥
en: When that great-souled Vipra had been brought over, the god, Indra, suddenly poured forth plenteous showers, enlivening the spirits of men.

sa_verse: वर्षेणैवागतं विप्रं तापसं स नराधिपः। प्रत्युद्गम्य मुनिं प्रह्वः शिरसा च महीं गतः॥
en: When the ascetic had arrived, with showers, the king approached him in bumble guise, bending his head to the ground.

sa_verse: अर्घ्यं च प्रददौ तस्मै न्यायतः सुसमाहितः। वने प्रसादं विप्रेन्द्रान्मा विप्रं मन्युराविशेत् ॥
en: He offered him Arghya in due form, and with a collected mind; and asked for his favour, so that wrath could not influence the Vipra.

sa_verse: अन्तःपुरं प्रवेश्यास्मै कन्यां दत्त्वा यथाविधि। शान्तां शान्तेन मनसा राजा हर्षमवाप सः॥
en: And taking him into the inner apartments, and in due form conferring upon him in sober mood his daughter śāntā, the king became happy.

sa_verse: एवं स न्यवसत् तत्र सर्वकामैः सुपूजितः। ऋष्यशृङ्गो महातेजाः शान्तया सह भार्यया॥
en: Thus the highly powerful Rşyaśřnga together with his wife śāntä, began to live there, respectfully ministered to in regard to every desire.

sa_verse: भूय एव हि राजेन्द्र शृणु मे वचनं हितम्। यथा स देवप्रवरः कथयामास बुद्धिमान्॥
en: And he said again, O foremost of monarchs, do you listen to me as I relate how that intelligent Sanatkumāra, best of deities, spoke.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकूणां कुले जातो भविष्यति सुधार्मिकः। नाम्ना दशरथो राजा श्रीमान् सत्यप्रतिश्रवः॥
en: In the line of Ikşvāku will be born a righteous king, named Dasaratha, fair of form, and firm in promise.

sa_verse: अङ्गराजेन सख्यं च तस्य राज्ञो भविष्यति। कन्या चास्य महाभागा शान्ता नाम भविष्यति॥ पुत्रस्त्वङ्गस्य. राज्ञस्तु रोमपाद इति श्रुतः। तं स राजा दशरथो गमिष्यति महायशाः।४।। अनपत्योऽस्मि धर्मात्मन्शान्ताभर्ता मम क्रतुम्। आहरेत त्वयाऽऽज्ञप्त: संतानार्थे कुलस्य च ॥
en: That king will contract friendship with the ruler of the Angas. And the latter will have a highly pious daughter, śāntā by name. And the (old) king of the Angas will have a son, named Romapāda. And repairing to him, the highly famous king Dasaratha will speak to Romapada, O righteous-souled one! I am without issue. Let śāntā's husband, desired by you, take charge of this sacrifice of mine, to be celebrated with the object of my obtaining a son to perpetuate the race.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा राज्ञोऽथ तद्वाक्यं मनसा स विचिन्त्य च। प्रदास्यते पुत्रवन्तं शान्ताभर्तारमात्मवान्॥
en: Hearing these words of the king, and having pondered well, he will make over to him Rsyasînga of subdued senses, together with Santa and his children.

sa_verse: प्रतिगृह्य च तं विप्रं स राजा विगतज्वरः। आहरिष्यति तं यज्ञं प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना ॥
en: And taking that Vipra, that king, his mind free from anxiety, with a glad heart, will prepare for that sacrifice.

sa_verse: तं च राजा दशरथो यशस्कामः कृताञ्जलिः। ऋष्यशृङ्ग द्विजश्रेष्ठं वरयिष्यति धर्मवित्॥ यज्ञार्थं प्रसवार्थं च स्वर्गार्थं च नरेश्वरः। लभते च स तं कामं द्विजमुख्याद् विशांपतिः॥
en: And king Dasaratha, knowing duty and desirous of fame, with the intention of obtaining offspring and heaven, with folded hands, will appoint that best of Brahmanas, Rsyasriga, to conduct the ceremony. And that bringer of good will attain his object at the hands of that foremost of Brāhmaṇas.

sa_verse: पुत्राश्चास्य भविष्यन्ति चत्वारोऽमितविक्रमाः। वंशप्रतिष्ठानकराः सर्वभूतेषु विश्रुताः॥ एवं स देवप्रवरः पूर्वं कथितवान् कथाम्। सनत्कुमारो भगवान् पुरा देवयुगे प्रभुः॥
en: And four sons will be born to him of immeasurable prowess, bringing fame to the family, and known by all.' Thus spoke formerly in the divine age, that worshipful and foremost of deities, Sanatkumāra.

sa_verse: स त्वं पुरुषशार्दूल समानय सुसत्कृतम्। स्वयमेव महाराज गत्वा सबलवाहनः॥
en: Therefore, do you, O tiger among men, repairing there, accompanied with your forces and equipage, yourself, O mighty king, bring Rşyasſrga over with due honours.

sa_verse: सुमन्तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा हृष्टो दशरथोऽभवत्। अनुमान्य वसिष्ठं च सूतवाक्यं निशाम्य च॥ सान्तःपुरः सहामात्यः प्रययौ यत्र स द्विजः।
en: Hearing Sumantra's words, Dasaratha was exceedingly delighted. And hearing these words, and permitted by Vasistha, he, accompanied with the ladies, and his courtiers, set out for the place where that twice-born one was.

sa_verse: वनानि सरितश्चैव व्यतिक्रम्य शनैः शनैः॥ भचक्राम तं देशं यत्र वै मुनिपुङ्गवः।
en: And gradually passing by woods and fells, he arrived at the place where that foremost of ascetics was.

sa_verse: आसाद्य तं द्विजश्रेष्ठं रोमपादसमीपगम्॥ ऋषिपुत्रं ददर्शाथो दीप्यमानमिवानलम्।
en: And coming before that best of regenerate ones, he saw that sage's son near Romapāda, like that of a flaming fire.

sa_verse: ततो राजा यथायोग्यं पूजां चक्रे विशेषतः॥ सखित्वात् तस्य वै राज्ञः प्रहष्टेनान्तरात्मना। रोमपादेन चाख्यातमृषिपुत्राय धीमते॥ सख्यं सम्बन्धकं चैव तदा तं प्रत्यपूजयत्।
en: Then the king received him respectfully, and with a delighted mind, on account of the friendship he bore him. He communicated to the intelligent son of the saint, the fact of their intimacy, and then the latter paid homage to Dasaratha.

sa_verse: एवं सुसत्कृतस्तेन सहोषित्वा नरर्षभः॥ सप्ताष्टदिवसान् राजा राजानमिदमब्रवीत्। शान्ता तव सुता राजन् सह भर्ना विशाम्पते॥ मदीयं नगरं यातु कार्य हि महदुद्यतम्।
en: Having passed seven or eight days with Romapäda, receiving high honours, that foremost of men, Dasaratha spoke to Romapada,. saying, Let your daughter, O king, together with her husband, O lord of men, repair to my city. I am going to be engaged in a mighty enterprise.

sa_verse: तथेति राजा संश्रुत्य गमनं तस्य धीमतः॥ उवाच वचनं विप्रं गच्छ त्वं सह भार्यया। ऋषिपुत्रः प्रतिश्रुत्य तथेत्याह नृपं तदा॥
en: Hearing this as to the journey of that intelligent one, the king said to that Vipra, Do you repair with your wife! Thereupon the saint's son, promising to go, said to the king, So be it!

sa_verse: स नृपेणाभ्यनुज्ञातः प्रययौ सह भार्यया। तावन्योन्याञ्जलिं कृत्वा स्नेहात्संश्लिष्य चोरसा ॥ ननन्दतुर्दशरथो रोमपादश्च वीर्यवान्। ततः सुहृदमापृच्छ्य प्रस्थितो रघुनन्दनः॥
en: Then with the king's permission, he set out with his wife. Dasaratha and the puissant Romapāda clasping each other by the palm, and embracing each other in affection, attained excess. of joy. Then Raghu's son, bidding farewell to his friend, set out.

sa_verse: पौरेषु प्रेषयामास दूतान् वै शीघ्रगामिनः। क्रियतां नगरं सर्वं क्षिप्रमेव स्वलंकृतम्॥ धूपितं सिक्तसम्मृष्टं पताकाभिरलंकृतम्।
en: He despatched swift messengers to the citizens, saying, Let the entire city be embellished; let it be perfumed with dhupa, and watered and decked with pennons.

sa_verse: ततः प्रहृष्टाः पौरास्ते श्रुत्वा राजानमागतम्॥ तथा चक्रुश्च तत् सर्वं राज्ञा यत् प्रेषितं तदा।
en: Hearing of the king's approach, the citizens joyfully did every thing as per message they had been communicated.

sa_verse: ततः स्वलंकृतं राजा नगरं प्रविवेश ह॥ शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिहादैः पुरस्कृत्वा द्विजर्षभम्।
en: Then the monarch, preceded by the foremost of Brāhmanas before him, entered the decorated city, to the blares of conches and drums.

sa_verse: ततः प्रमुदिताः सर्वे दृष्ट्वा वै नागरा द्विजम् ॥ प्रवेश्यमानं सत्कृत्य नरेन्द्रणेन्द्रकर्मणा। यथा दिवि सुरेन्द्रेण सहस्राक्षेण काश्यपम्॥
en: And beholding that Brāhmaṇa entering the city, duly honoured by the prime of men; subservient to Indra, like to Kaśyapa's son entering the celestial regions honoured of the thousand eyed lord of the celestials, all the citizens rejoiced exceedingly.

sa_verse: अन्तःपुरं प्रवेश्यैनं पूजां कृत्वा च शास्त्रतः। कृतकृत्यं तदात्मानं मेने तस्योपवाहनात्॥
en: Then taking him into the inner apartment, and paying him homage according to the ordinance, the king considered himself as having gained his object, in consequence of the presence of that Brahmana.

sa_verse: अन्त:पुराणि सर्वाणि शान्तां दृष्ट्वा तथागताम्। सह भ; विशालाक्षीं प्रीत्यानन्दमुपागमन्॥
en: And all the inmates of the inner apartment, seeing the large-eyed Săntă thus arrived with her husband, experienced excess of joy.

sa_verse: पूज्यमाना तु ताभिः सा राज्ञा चैव विशेषतः। उवास तत्र सुखिता कञ्चित् कालं सहद्विजा ॥
en: Then honoured by them and the king in especial, she happily spent there some time along with that twice-born one.

sa_verse: ततः काले बहुतिथे कस्मिंश्चित् सुमनोहरे। वसन्ते समनुप्राप्ते राज्ञो यष्टुं मनोऽभवत्॥
en: Then after a long while, when the charming spring had appeared on the earth, the king conceived the desire of celebrating the sacrifice.

sa_verse: ततः प्रणम्य शिरसा तं विप्रं देववर्णिनम्। यज्ञाय वरयामास संतानार्थं कुलस्य च।२।।
en: Then bowing down the head before the Vipra effulgent like a celestial, he appointed him to undertake the ceremony, for the purpose of obtaining offspring to perpetuate his line.

sa_verse: तथेति च स राजानमुवाच वसुधाधिपम्। सम्भाराः सम्भ्रियन्तां ते तुरगश्च विमुच्यताम्॥ सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे यज्ञभूमिर्विधीयताम्।
en: Then that Brāhmana said to the lord of the earth, the king, So be it! Get order the necessary provisions procured, free the horse set, and prepare a sacrificial ground on the north bank of the Sarayd.

sa_verse: ततोऽब्रवीनृपो वाक्यं ब्राह्मणान् वेदपारगान्॥ सुमन्त्रावाहय क्षिप्रमृत्विजो ब्रह्मवादिनः। सुयज्ञं वामदेवं च जाबालिमथ काश्यपम्॥ पुरोहितं वसिष्ठं च ये चान्ये द्विजसत्तमाः।
en: Then the king spoke to Sumantra, saying, O Sumantra, do you summon speedily Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vedas and priests professing the Vedānta philosophy, Suyajña, and Vāmadeva, and Jabali, and Kasyapa, and the priest Vasistha, together with other excellent twice-born ones.

sa_verse: ततः सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितं गत्वा त्वरितविक्रमः॥ समानयत् स तान् सर्वान् समस्तान् वेदपारगान्।
en: Thereupon Sumantra eridowed with activity, bestirring himself, summoned all those versed in the Vedas.

sa_verse: तान् पूजयित्वा धर्मात्मा राजा दशरथस्तदा॥ धर्मार्थसहितं युक्तं श्लक्ष्णं वचनमब्रवीत्।
en: Then, honouring them duly, the virtuous king Dasaratha spoke to them these amiable words, consistent with duty and interest.

sa_verse: मम तातप्यमानस्य पुत्रार्थं नास्ति वै सुखम्॥ पुत्रार्थं हयमेधेन यक्ष्यामीति मतिर्मम।
en: Aggrieved on account of a son, I have no happiness on earth, and, therefore, I have intended to celebrate a horse-sacrifice.

sa_verse: तदहं यष्टुमिच्छामि हयमेधेन कर्मणा॥ ऋषिपुत्रप्रभावेण कामान् प्राप्स्यामि चाप्यहम्।
en: So, I would like to perform a horse-sacrifice. And by the grace of the saint's son, I shall obtain my desire.

sa_verse: ततः साध्विति तद्वाक्यं ब्राह्मणाः प्रत्यपूजयन्॥ वसिष्ठप्रमुखाः सर्वे पार्थिवस्य मुखाच्च्युतम्।
en: Thereupon, the Brāhmaṇas with Vasiştha at their head honoured the words that fell from the king's lips, saying, Well!

sa_verse: ऋष्यशृङ्गपुरोगाश्च प्रत्यूचुर्नृपतिं तदा॥ सम्भाराः सम्भ्रियन्तां ते तुरगश्च विमुच्यताम्। सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे यज्ञभूमिर्विधीयताम्॥
en: The Brāhmaṇas headed by Rsyaśộnga addressed the king, saying, Do you arrange for the provisions, loose the horse, and prepare the sacrificial ground on the north bank of the Sarayū river!

sa_verse: सर्वथा प्राप्स्यसे पुत्रांश्चतुरोऽमितविक्रमान्। यस्य ते धार्मिकी बुद्धिरियं पुत्रार्थमागता॥
en: Since you purpose so virtuously for obtaining offspring, you shall obtain four sons of immeasurable prowess.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतोऽभवद् राजा श्रुत्वा तु द्विजभाषितम्। अमात्यानब्रवीद् राजा हर्षेणेदं शुभाक्षरम्॥
en: Hearing those words of the regenerate ones, the king was exceedingly delighted. And cheerfully he spoke these auspicious words to his courtiers.

sa_verse: गुरूणां वचनाच्छीघ्रं सम्भाराः सम्भ्रियन्तु मे। समर्थाधिष्ठितश्चाश्वः सोपाध्यायो विमुच्यताम्॥
en: In accordance with the directions of my spiritual guides, do you speedily procure these provisions, loose the horse well protected, and followed by a priest.

sa_verse: सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे यज्ञभूमिर्विधीयताम्। शान्तयश्चाभिवर्धन्तां यथाकल्पं यथाविधि ॥
en: And prepare the sacrificial ground on the north bank of the Sarayu. And perform the ceremonies capable of securing the rites from disturbance.

sa_verse: शक्यः कर्तुमयं यज्ञः सर्वेणापि महीक्षिता। नापराधो भवेत् कष्टो यद्यस्मिन् क्रतुसत्तमे॥
en: Surely every king is competent to perform this sacrifice yet care must be taken that no default occurs in it.

sa_verse: छिद्रं हि मृगयन्त्येते विद्वांसो ब्रह्मराक्षसाः। विधिहीनस्य यक्षस्य सद्यः कर्ता विनश्यति ॥
en: For flaws in this foremost of sacrifices are watched by learned Brahma-Rākşasas. And should it come to be celebrated in violation of the ordinance, ihe performer thereof shall meet with instant destruction.

sa_verse: तद् यथा विधिपूर्वं मे क्रतुरेष समाप्यते। तथा विधानं क्रियतां समर्थाः करणेष्विह॥
en: And do you so order that this sacrifice of mine may be completed according to the prescribed ritual.

sa_verse: तथेति च ततः सर्वे मन्त्रिणः प्रत्यपूजयन्। पार्थिवेन्द्रस्य तद्वाक्यं यथाज्ञप्तमकुर्वत ॥
en: Thereupon honouring those words of the king, the ministers did as ordered.

sa_verse: ततो द्विजास्ते धर्मज्ञमस्तुवन् पार्थिवर्षभम्। अनुज्ञातास्ततः सर्वे पुनर्जग्मुर्यथागतम्॥
en: And having eulogised the king knowing duty, the twice-born ones, with the Monarch's leave, departed for their respective quarters.

sa_verse: गतानां तेषु विप्रेषु मन्त्रिणस्तान् नराधिपः। विसर्जयित्वा स्वं वेश्म प्रविवेश महामतिः॥
en: When the Vipras had gone, the mightyminded lord of men dismissing his counsellors, entered his place. When the Vipras had gone, the mightyminded lord of men dismissing his counsellors, entered his place.

sa_verse: पुनः प्राप्ते वसन्ते तु पूर्णः संवत्सरोऽभवत्। प्रसवार्थं गतो यष्टुं हयमेधेन वीर्यवान्॥
en: When after a full one year, spring had again appeared on the face of the earth, the puissant king, intent upon getting offspring through the horse-sacrifice, sough Vasistha's side.

sa_verse: अभिवाद्य वसिष्ठं च न्यायतः प्रतिपूज्य च। अब्रवीत् प्रश्रितं वाक्यं प्रसवार्थं द्विजोत्तमम्॥
en: Having saluted Vasiştha and duly paid him homage, he humbly spoke to that best of twiceborn ones, with the intention of having offspring.

sa_verse: यज्ञो मे क्रियतां ब्रह्मन् यथोक्तं मुनिपुङ्गव। यथा न विघ्नाः क्रियन्ते यज्ञाङ्गेषु विधीयताम्॥
en: O Brahman, undertake to perform this sacrifice of mine, according to the ordinance, O foremost of ascetics! do you order so that no impediment may happen to the sacrifice!.

sa_verse: भवान् स्निग्धः सुहृन्मह्यं गुरुश्च परमो महान्। वोढव्यो भवता चैव भारो यज्ञस्य चोद्यतः॥
en: You are my kind friend, and prime and mighty spiritual guide. Engaged in it, you will have to bear the entire burden of the ceremony.

sa_verse: तथेति च स राजानमब्रवीद् द्विजसत्तमः। करिष्ये सर्वमेवैतद् भवता यत् समर्थितम्॥
en: Thereupon that best of Brāhmaṇas said to the king, 'So be it! I will do all that you asked.

sa_verse: ततोऽब्रवीद् द्विजान् वृद्धान् यज्ञकर्मसु निष्ठितान्। स्थापत्ये निष्ठितांश्चैव वृद्धान् परमधार्मिकान्॥ कर्मान्तिकाशिल्पकारान् वर्धकीन् खनकानपि। गणकाशिल्पिनश्चैव तथैव नटनर्तकान्॥ तथा शुचीशास्त्रविदः पुरुषान् सुबहुश्रुतान्। यज्ञकर्म समीहन्तां भवन्तो राजशासनात्॥
en: He then said to the old Brāhmaṇas well-up in sacrificial affairs, and experienced car-makers, and highly pious aged people, and servants, carrying on the ceremonial operations till the end, and artists, and carpenters, and diggers, and astrologers, and artizans, and dancers, and conductors of theatres, and pure and learned persons variously versed in knowledge, Do you, in obedience to the royal mandate, engage in the sacrificial work!

sa_verse: इष्टका बहुसाहस्री शीघ्रमानीयतामिति। उपकार्याः क्रियन्तां च राज्ञो बहुगुणान्विताः॥
en: And fetch bricks by thousands. And do raise structures for the kings, commanding every convenience.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणावसथाश्चैव कर्तव्याः शतशः शुभाः। भक्ष्यान्नपानैर्बहुभिः समुपेताः सुनिष्ठिताः॥
en: Do you rear goodly and comfortable buildings by hundreds for the Brāhmaṇas, replenished with various meats and drinks.

sa_verse: तथा पौरजनस्यापि कर्तव्याश्च सुविस्तराः। आगतानां सुदूराच्च पार्थिवानां पृथक् पृथक् ॥ वाजिवारणशालाश्च तथा शय्यागृहाणि च। भटानां महदावासा वैदेशिकनिवासिनाम्॥
en: You should provide spacious apartments for the citizens and the dwellers, of provinces, and separate quarters for the princes, coming from foreign parts. And stables and sleeping apartments be raised for warriors coming from foreign countries.

sa_verse: आवासा बहुभक्ष्या वै सर्वकामैरुपस्थिताः। तथा पौरजनस्यापि जनस्य बहुशोभनम्॥ दातव्यमनं विधिवत् सत्कृत्य न तु लीलया। सर्वे वर्णा यथा पूजां प्राप्नुवन्ति सुसत्कृताः॥ न चावज्ञा प्रयोक्तव्या कामक्रोधवशादपि।
en: And dwellings filled with diverse kinds of viands, and commanding everything desirable, and mansions for the lower orders of the citizens, exceedingly beautiful to behold. And meats should be duly dispensed with all hospitality, and not in the indifference of festive occasions, so that all may regard themselves as honourably entertained. And none should be disregarded out of lust or passion.

sa_verse: यज्ञकर्मसु ये व्यग्राः पुरुषाः शिल्पिनस्तथा॥ तेषामपि विशेषेण पूजा कार्या यथाक्रमम्।
en: Those persons, and artizans, that will labour eagerly in the sacrifice, should by turns, be especially entertained.

sa_verse: ये स्युः सम्पूजिताः सर्वे वसुभिर्भोजनेन च॥ यथा सर्वं सुविहितं न किंचित् परिहीयते। तथा भवन्तः कुर्वन्तु प्रीतियुक्तेन चेतसा ।१७।।
en: And servants, who being entertained with gifts, do everything completely, and omit nothing. And do you, with hearts mollified by love, act so, that all our friends be well pleased with us.

sa_verse: ततः सर्वे समागम्य वसिष्ठमिदमब्रुवन्। यथेष्ठं तत् सुविहितं न किंचित् परिहीयते॥ यथोक्तं तत् करिष्यामो न किंचित् परिहास्यते। ततः सुमन्त्रमाहूय वसिष्ठो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥ निमन्त्रयस्व नृपतीन् पृथिव्यां ये च धार्मिकाः। ब्राह्मणान् क्षत्रियान् वैश्याशूद्रांश्चैव सहस्रशः॥
en: Then they approached Vasistha, saying, Everything has been performed properly, without anything being left out. And what you say shall be performed, and nothing words, Do you invite all those kings that are pious, and Brāhmaṇas, and Kșatriyas and Sūdras, by thousands. And do you with due honours bring people from all countries.

sa_verse: समानयस्व सत्कृत्य सर्वदेशेषु मानवान्। मिथिलाधिपतिं शूरं जनकं सत्यवादिनम्॥ तमानय महाभागं स्वयमेव सुसत्कृतम्। पूर्वं सम्बन्धिनं ज्ञात्वा ततः पूर्वं ब्रवीमि ते॥
en: With proper honour yourself bring the righteous, truthful, and heroic Janaka, lord of Mithilā. And it is because he is our old friend that I first mention him.

sa_verse: तथा काशिपति स्निग्धं सततं प्रियवादिनम्। सद्वृत्तं देवसंकाशं स्वयमेवानयस्व ह ॥
en: Then do yourself bring the amiable and ever fair-spoken lord of Kāśī, of excellent character, resembling a celestial.

sa_verse: तथा केकयराजानं वृद्धं परमधार्मिकम्। श्वशुरं राजसिंहस्य सपुत्रं तमिहानय॥
en: Then do you bring here along with his son, the highly-pious, old king of Kekaya, who is the father-in-law of this tiger of a monarch.

sa_verse: अङ्गेश्वरं महेष्वासं रोमपादं सुसत्कृतम्। वयस्यं राजसिंहस्य सपुत्रं तमिहानय॥
en: Then also bring with due honours the king of Anga, and that mighty archer, the illustrious Romapada, the friend of that lion among the kings, along with his son.

sa_verse: तथा कोसलराजानं भानुमन्तं सुसत्कृतम्। मगधाधिपति शूरं सर्वशास्त्रविशारदम्॥ प्राप्तिज्ञं परमोदारं सत्कृतं पुरुषर्षभम्। राज्ञः शासनमादाय चोदयस्व नृपर्षभान्। प्राचीनान् सिन्धुसौवीरान्सौराष्ट्रेयांश्च पार्थिवान्॥ दाक्षिणात्यान् नरेन्द्रांश्च समस्तानानयस्व ह। सन्ति स्निग्धाश्च ये चान्ये राजानः पृथिवीतले ॥ तानानय यथा क्षिप्रं सानुगान् सहबान्धवान्। एतान् दूतैर्महाभागैरानयस्व नृपाज्ञया॥
en: Also bring with due respect the puissant king of Kosala. The heroic, and highly generous lord of Magadha, versed in all branches of learning. And in accordance with the mandate of the king, do invite the foremost monarchs! And summon the king, of the East, of the Sindhu and Sauvīra countries, and of Saurastra, and of the South! And speedily bring those monarchs that are attached to us, together with their friends and followers, the mandate of the monarch, bring over these, despatching dignified emissaries.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठवाक्यं तच्छ्रुत्वा सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितं तदा। व्यादिशत् पुरुषांस्तत्र राज्ञामानयने शुभान्॥
en: Having heard those words of Vasistha, Sumantra speedily ordered the emissaries to bring the kings.

sa_verse: स्वयमेव हि धर्मात्मा प्रयातो मुनिशासनात्। सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितो भूत्वा समानेतुं महामतिः॥
en: The virtuous Sumantra, in accordance with the injunction of the ascetic, himself speedily set out for the purpose of bringing the monarchs.

sa_verse: ते च कर्मान्तिकाः सर्वे वसिष्ठाय महर्षये। सर्वं निवेदयन्ति स्म यज्ञे यदुपकल्पितम्॥
en: Then the servants came and informed the intelligent Vasiştha as to the articles that had been got ready for the sacrifice.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतो द्विजश्रेष्ठस्तान् सर्वान् मुनिरब्रवीत्। अवज्ञया न दातव्यं कस्याचिल्लीलयापि वा॥ अवज्ञया कृतं हन्याद् दातारं नात्र संशयः।
en: Then well pleased that best of twice-born ones, the ascetic Vasistha, said to them, Do not give away disrespectfully or lightly. A gift bestowed with disrespect, indubitably destroy the giver.

sa_verse: ततः कैश्चिदहोरात्रैरुपयाता महीक्षितः॥ बहूनि रत्नान्यादाय राज्ञो दशरथस्य ह।
en: Then for several days, kings began to pour into Dasaratha's city daily and nightly, bringing with them various kinds of gems.

sa_verse: ततो वसिष्ठः सुप्रीतो राजानमिदमब्रवीत्॥ उपयाता नरव्याघ्र राजानस्तव शासनात्। मयापि सत्कृताः सर्वे यथार्ह राजसत्तम॥
en: Thereupon Vasiştha well-pleased said to the king, O tiger among men, obeying your mandate the kings have come here; and I too according to merit, have received those excellent kings with respect.

sa_verse: यज्ञियं च कृतं सर्वं पुरुषैः सुसमाहितैः। निर्यातु च भवान् यष्टुं यज्ञायतनमन्तिकात्॥
en: Everything has been carefully made ready for the sacrifice by the persons concerned. Do you, therefore, repair to the sacrificial ground, for performing the sacrifice.

sa_verse: सर्वकामैरुपहतैरुपेतं वै समन्ततः। द्रष्टुमर्हसि राजेन्द्र मनसेव विनिर्मितम्॥
en: O foremost of monarchs, it behove you to view the place, filled with all desirable objects, and looking as if prepared by imagination herself.

sa_verse: तथा वसिष्ठवचनादृष्यशृङ्गस्य चोभयोः। दिवसे शुभनक्षत्रे निर्यातो जगतीपतिः॥
en: Then in accordance with the injunctions of both Vasiştha and Rşyasſga, the king came to the sacrificial spot on a day presided over by an auspicious star.

sa_verse: ततो वसिष्ठप्रमुखाः सर्व एव द्विजोत्तमाः। ऋष्यशृङ्गं पुरस्कृत्य यज्ञकर्मारभंस्तदा॥ यज्ञवाटं गताः सर्वे यथाशास्त्रं यथाविधि। श्रीमांश्च सह पत्नीभी राजा दीक्षामुपाविशत्॥
en: Then, with, Rsyasrriga at their head, Vasistha and the other principal Brāhmaṇas wending to the sacrificial ground, began the ceremony, according to the ordinance; and in due form. And the auspicious king, in company with his wives, was initiated into the ceremony.

sa_verse: अथ संवत्सरे पूर्णे तस्मिन् प्राप्ते तुरङ्गमे। सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे राज्ञो यज्ञोऽभ्यवर्तत ॥
en: After the expiry of full one year, when the sacrificial horse had returned, the sacrifice, of the king commenced on the north bank of the Sarayu.

sa_verse: ऋष्यशृङ्गं पुरस्कृत्य कर्म चकुर्द्विजर्षभाः। अश्वमेधे महायज्ञे राज्ञोऽस्य सुमहात्मनः॥
en: And with Rşyasſága at their head, the principal twice-born ones began the proceedings in that mighty horse-sacrifice of that high-souled monarch.

sa_verse: कर्म कुर्वन्ति विधिवद् याजका वेदपारगाः। यथाविधि यथान्यायं परिक्रामन्ति शास्त्रतः॥
en: And the priests, each duly and according to the ordinance performing his proper part, engaged in the ceremony in consonance with the scriptures.

sa_verse: प्रवर्यं शास्त्रतः कृत्वा तथैवोपसदं द्विजाः। चक्रुश्च विधिवत् सर्वमधिकं कर्म शास्त्रतः॥ अभिपूज्य तदा हृष्टाः सर्वे चक्रुर्यथाविधि। प्रातः सवनपूर्वाणि कर्माणि मुनिपुङ्गवाः॥
en: And the regenerate ones, having performed the pravargya as well as the upasada according to the ordinance, duly completed the additional ceremonies. Then, worshipping the deities with glad hearts, those foremost of ascetics duly performed the morning ablutions and the other prescribed rites.

sa_verse: ऐन्द्रश्च विधिवद् दत्तो राजा चाभिषुतोऽनघः। मध्यंदिनं च सवनं प्रावर्तत यथाक्रमम्॥
en: The oblations of clarified butter first having been offered to Indra, according to the ritual, the king with a purified heart performed his ablutions. And then the mid-day ablutions took place in proper sequence.

sa_verse: तृतीयसवनं चैव राज्ञोऽस्य सुमहात्मनः। चकुस्ते शास्त्रतो दृष्ट्वा यथा ब्राह्मणपुङ्गवाः ॥
en: Those foremost of Brahmanas, in due form, and according to the ordinance, officiated at the third bath of that high-souled monarch.

sa_verse: आह्वयाञ्चक्रिरे तत्र शक्रादीन् विबुधोत्तमान्। ऋष्यशृङ्गादयो मन्त्रैः शिक्षाक्षरसमन्वितैः॥
en: The priests presided over by Rşyasſiga, invoked Śakra and the other deities, reciting measured mantras.

sa_verse: न चाहुतमभूत् तत्र स्खलितं वा न किंचन। दृश्यते ब्रह्मवत् सर्व क्षेमयुक्तं हि चक्रिरे॥
en: And no part of the ceremony was performed improperly, or left out, and every thing was satisfactorily celebrated with mantras.

sa_verse: न तेष्वहःसु श्रान्तो वा क्षुधितो वा न दृश्यते। नाविद्वान् ब्राह्मणः कश्चिन्नाशतानुचरस्तथा॥
en: And on that day no Brāhmaṇa ever felt tired, or hungry and there was none that was not learned, or that was not followed by an hundred persons.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणा भुञ्जते नित्यं नाथवन्तश्च भुञ्जते। तापसा भुञ्जते चापि श्रमणाश्चैव भुञ्जते॥ वृद्धाश्च व्याधिताश्चैव स्त्रीबालाश्च तथैव च। अनिशं भुञ्जमानानां न तृप्तिरुपलभ्यते॥
en: Brāhmaṇas, and Śūdras having among them ascetics, and Sramanas, and the aged, and the infirm, and women, and children, were continually fed. And although they ate their fill, yet they know no repletion.

sa_verse: दीयतां दीयतामन्नं वासांसि विविधानि च। इति संचोदितास्तत्र तथा चक्रनेकशः॥
en: And Give food, and clothes of various kinds (was heard all around) And those employed in the task gave away profusely.

sa_verse: अन्नकूटाश्च दृश्यन्ते बहवः पर्वतोपमाः। दिवसे दिवसे तत्र सिद्धस्य विधिवत् तदा॥
en: And every day food dressed properly in due form was to be seen in countless heaps resembling hills.

sa_verse: नानादेशादनुप्राप्ताः पुरुषाः स्त्रीगणास्तथा। अन्नपानैः सुविहितास्तस्मिन् यज्ञे महात्मनः॥
en: And men and women coming from various countries to the sacrifice of that high-souled one were excellently entertained with meats and drinks.

sa_verse: अन्नं हि विधिवत्स्वादु प्रशंसन्ति द्विजर्षभाः। अहो तृप्ताः स्म भद्रं ते इति शुश्राव राघवः॥
en: The foremost regenerate ones said, The viands have been prepared in the prescribed form, and they taste excellent. We have been gratified. God bless you! All this was heard by the descendant of Raghu.

sa_verse: स्वलंकृताश्च पुरुषा ब्राह्मणान् पर्यवेषयन्। उपासन्ते च तानन्ये सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः॥
en: Persons adorned with ornaments distributed the victuals among the Brāhmaṇas, and they were assisted by others wearing jewelled pendants.

sa_verse: दिवसे दिवसे तत्र संस्तरे कुशला द्विजाः। सर्वकर्माणि चक्रुस्ते यथाशास्त्रं प्रचोदिताः॥
en: Every day in that sacrifice, skilful Brāhmaṇas, engaged in the ceremony, did everything, according to the ritual.

sa_verse: नाषडङ्गविदत्रासीन्नाव्रतो नाबहुश्रुतः। सदस्यास्तस्य वै राज्ञो नावादकुशलो द्विजः॥
en: There was no twice-born one that was not versed in the Vedas and the Vedāngas, or that did not observe vows, or that was not profoundly learned, nor did any assist at the sacrifice that could not argue ably.

sa_verse: प्राप्ते यूपाच्छ्ये तस्मिन् षड्बैल्वाः खादिरास्तथा। तावन्तो बिल्वसहिताः पर्णिनश्च तथा परे॥ श्लेष्मातकमयो दिष्टो देवदारुमयस्तथा। द्वावेव तत्र विहितौ बाहुव्यस्तपरिग्रहो॥
en: And when the time came for planting the Yüpas of bilva, as many of catechu, and as many of Palasa, and one of Sleşmätaka, and two of Devadāru well-made and measuring two outstretched arms.

sa_verse: कारिताः सर्व एवैते शास्त्रज्ञैर्यज्ञकोविदः शोभार्थं तस्य यज्ञस्य काञ्चनालङ्कृता भवन्॥ एकविंशतियूपास्ते एकविंशत्यरत्नयः। वासोभिरेकविंशद्भिरेकैकं समलङ्कृताः॥ विन्यस्ता विधिवत् सर्वे शिल्पिभिः सुकृता दृढाः। अष्टास्रयः सर्व एव श्लक्ष्णरूपसमन्विताः॥
en: Persons versed in the arts and science of sacrifice constructed these Yüpas. And at the time of throwing up the Yüpas for embellishing the sacrifice, these one and twenty Yüpas, each measuring one and twenty Aratnis, having eight angles, and smooth-faced, were decked out in one and twenty pieces of cloth, and were firmly planted with due ceremonies by artisans.

sa_verse: आच्छादितास्ते वासोभिः पुष्पैर्गन्धैश्च पूजिताः। सप्तर्षयो दीप्तिमन्तो विराजन्ते यथा दिवि॥
en: And being wrapped up in cloths, and worshipped with flowers, they looked like the seven Rșis appearing in the welkin.

sa_verse: इष्टकाश्च यथान्यायं कारिताश्च प्रमाणतः। चितोऽग्निर्ब्राह्मणैस्तत्र कुशलैः शिल्पकर्मणि॥
en: And an adequate number of bricks was also duly made (for the ceremony.) And Brāhmaṇas accomplished in the arts constructed the sacrificial fire-place with those bricks.

sa_verse: स चित्यो राजसिंहस्य संचितः कुशलैर्द्विजैः। गरुडो रुक्मपक्षो वै त्रिगुणोऽष्टादशात्मकः॥ नियुक्तास्तत्र पशवस्तत्तदुद्दिश्य दैवतम्। उरगाः पक्षिणश्चैव यथाशास्त्रं प्रचोदिताः॥ शामित्रे तु हयस्तत्र तथा जलचराश्च ये। ऋषिभिः सर्वमेवैतन्नियुक्तं शास्त्रतस्तदा॥
en: That fire-place of that lion among kings, set by skilful Brāhmaṇas, consisting on three sides of eighteen bricks, looked like the goldenwinged Garuda. And for the purpose of sacrificing them to the respective deities were collect beasts and reptiles, and birds, and horses, and aquatic animals. And the priests sacrificed all these in proper form.

sa_verse: पशूनां त्रिशतं तत्र यूपेषु नियतं तदा। अश्वरत्नोत्तमं तत्र राज्ञो दशरथस्य ह ॥
en: And to these Yūpas were bound, three hundred beasts, as well as the foremost of the best horses belonging to king Dasaratha.

sa_verse: कौसल्या तं हयं तत्र परिचर्य समन्ततः। कृपाणैर्विससारैनं त्रिभिः परमया मुदा॥
en: Then Kausalyā, having performed the preliminary rites, with three strokes slew that horse, experiencing great glee.

sa_verse: पतत्त्रिणा तथा सार्धं सुस्थितेन च चेतसा। अवसद् रजनीमेकां कौसल्या धर्मकाम्यया।॥
en: And with the view of reaping merit, Kausalyā, with an undisturbed heart passed one night with that horse furnished with wings.

sa_verse: होताध्वर्युस्तथोद्गाता हयेन समयोजयन्। महिष्या परिवृत्त्याथ वावातामपरां तथा॥ पतत्रिणस्तस्य वपामुद्धृत्य नियतेन्द्रियः। ऋत्विकपरमसम्पन्नः श्रपयामास शास्त्रतः॥
en: Hotás and Adhvaryus, and the Udgatás joined the king's Vāvātā along with his Mahișī and Parivrtti.* And priests of subdued senses, wellup in sacrificial rites, began to offer oblations with the fat of the winged-horse, according to the ordinance. *The Kșatriya kings could marry wives from among Kșatriyas, Vaişyas and Śūdras. The Kșatriya wife is called Mahiși, the Vaişya wife Vávātā and the Śūdra wife Parivrtti.

sa_verse: धूमगन्धं वपायास्तु जिघ्रति स्म नराधिपः। यथाकालं यथान्यायं निर्गुदन् पापमात्मनः॥
en: The lord of men, desirous of removing his sins, at the proper time smelt the odour of the smoke arising from the fat, agreeably to the scriptures.

sa_verse: हयस्य यानि चाङ्गानि तानि सर्वाणि ब्राह्मणाः। अग्नौ प्रास्यन्ति विधिवत् समस्ताः षोडशर्विजः॥
en: Then sixteen sacrificial priests in the prescribed form offered the various parts of the horse to the fire.

sa_verse: प्लक्षशाखासु यज्ञानामन्येषां क्रियते हविः। अश्वमेधस्य यज्ञस्य वैतसो भाग इष्यते॥ त्र्यहोऽश्वमेधः संख्यातः कल्पसूत्रेण ब्राह्मणैः। चतुष्टोममहस्तस्य प्रथमं परिकल्पितम्॥ उक्थ्यं द्वितीयं संख्यातमतिरात्रं तथोत्तरम्। कारितास्तत्र बहवो विहिताः शास्त्रदर्शनात् ॥
en: It is customary in other sacrifices to offer the oblations by means of a Plaksa bough; but in the horse-sacrifice a cane is used instead. The horsesacrifice, according to the Kalpa-Sūtras and the Brahmanas, extend over three days. Thereafter, on the first day was the Catuștoma celebrated; and on the second, the Uktha, and on the third the Atirâtra.

sa_verse: ज्योतिष्ठोमायुषी चैवमतिरात्रो च निर्मितौ। अभिजिद्विश्वजिच्चैवमाप्तोर्यामौ महाक्रतुः।॥
en: And then the jyotisloma, and then Āyuştoma, and the Atirātra, and the Abhijit, and the Visvajit, and the Āptoryāma, all these various great sacrifices were celebrated with due rites.

sa_verse: प्राचीं होने ददौ राजा दिशं स्वकुलवर्धनः। अध्वर्यवे प्रतीची तु ब्रह्मणे दक्षिणां दिशम्॥ उद्गात्रे तु तथोदीची दक्षिणैषा विनिर्मिता। अश्वमेधे महायज्ञे स्वयंभूविहिते पुरा ॥
en: And in this mighty horse-sacrifice founded of yore by Svayambhū, that perpetuator of his line, the king, bestowed the Eastern quarter on his chief sacrificial priest, the Western on his Adhvaryu, the Southern on Brahmă, and the Northern on the Udgātā, as Daksinās.

sa_verse: क्रतुं समाप्य तु तदा न्यायत: पुरुषर्षभः। ऋत्विग्भ्यो हि ददौ राजा धरां तां कुलवर्धनः॥ एवं दत्त्वा प्रहृष्टोऽभूच्छीमानिक्ष्वाकुनन्दनः। ऋत्विजस्त्वब्रुवन् सर्वे राजानं गतकिल्विषम्॥
en: Having completed that sacrifice, that perpetuator of his race, and foremost of men, the king, conferied on the priests the earth; and having conferred it, that auspicious descendant of Ikşvāku experienced high delight. And then the priests spoke to that monarch, who had all his sins purged off saying.

sa_verse: भवानेव महीं कृत्स्नामेको रक्षितुमर्हति। न भूम्या कार्यमस्माकं नहि शक्तां स्म पालने॥ रताः स्वाध्यायकरणे वयं नित्यं हि भूमिप। निष्क्रयं किञ्चिदेवेह प्रयच्छतु भवानिति॥
en: You alone are worthy to protect the entire world. We do not want the earth; nor can we rule it, being, O lord of Earth, constantly engaged in Vedic studies. Do you, therefore, confer upon us something instead, as the price thereof.

sa_verse: मणिरत्नं सुवर्णं वा गावो यद्वा समुद्यतम्। तत् प्रयच्छ नृपश्रेष्ठ धरण्या न प्रयोजनम्॥
en: Do you confer upon us gems, or gold, or kine, or anything else, for, O foremost of monarchs, we do not want earth.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो नरपतिर्ब्राह्मणैर्वेदपारगैः। गवां शतसहस्राणि दश तेभ्यो ददौ नृपः॥ दशकोटिं सुवर्णस्य रजतस्य चतुर्गुणम्।
en: Thus addressed by the Brāhmaṇas versed in the Veda, that best of kings bestowed upon them ten lace of kine, and ten Koțis of gold, and forty of silver.

sa_verse: ततः प्रसर्पकेभ्यस्तु हिरण्यं सुसमाहितः॥ जाम्बूनदं कोटिसंख्यं ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददौ तदा।
en: Then to those Brāhmaṇas that had come there, the king with due regard gave Koțis of gold.

sa_verse: दरिद्राय द्विजायाथ हस्ताभरणमुत्तमम्॥ कस्मैचिद् याचमानाय ददौ राघवनन्दनः।
en: Then to a certain poor twice-born one that asked for gifts, the descendant of Raghu gave an excellent ornament from his own arm.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतेषु विधिवत् द्विजेषु द्विजवत्सलः॥ प्रणाममकरोत् तेषां हर्षव्याकुलितेन्द्रियः।
en: When the regenerate ones were thus properly gratified, that one cherishing the Brāhmaṇas, with senses intoxicated by excess of joy, reverentially bowed to them.

sa_verse: तस्याशिषोऽथ विविधा ब्राह्मणैः समुदाहृताः॥ उदारस्य नृवीरस्य धरण्यां पतितस्य च।
en: Thereupon the Brāhmaṇas uttered various blessings upon that generous king, bending low to the earth.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतमना राजा प्राप्य यज्ञमनुत्तमम्॥ पापापहं स्वर्नयनं दुस्तरं पार्थिवर्षभैः। ततोऽब्रवीदृष्यशृङ्ग राजा दशरथस्तदा॥ कुलस्य वर्धनं तत् तु कर्तुमर्हसि सुव्रत।
en: Then having celebrated that excellent and sindestroying sacrifice, bringing heaven, and incapable of being celebrated by foremost monarchs, king Dasaratha, well pleased, spoke to Rsyasriga, saying, Oyou of excellent vows, it behove you to do that whereby my line may increase.

sa_verse: तथेति च स राजानमुवाच द्विजसत्तमः। भविष्यन्ति सुता राजश्चत्वारस्ते कुलोद्वहाः॥
en: Thereupon the best of Brāhmaṇas told the king that he would have four sons, born to him, as perpetuators of his race.

sa_verse: स तस्य वाक्यं मधुरं निशम्य प्रणम्य तस्मै प्रयतो नृपेन्द्रः। जगाम हर्ष परमं महात्मा तमृष्यशृङ्ग पुनरप्युवाच॥
en: Hearing these sweet words of his, that foremost of monarchs bended low to him with controlled faculties, and experienced the excess of joy. And then that high-souled one again spoke to Rşyaśțnga.

sa_verse: मेधावी तु ततो ध्यात्वा स किंञ्चिदिदमुत्तरम्। लब्धसंज्ञस्ततस्तं तु वेदज्ञो नृपमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then that one of capacious intelligence, versed in the Vedas, having pondered for a time, and regained his senses, returned to the king this excellent answer.

sa_verse: इष्टिं तेऽहं करिष्यामि पुत्रीयां पुत्रकारणात्। अथर्वशिरसि प्रोक्तैर्मन्त्रैः सिद्धां विधानतः॥
en: On your behalf, and with the view of obtaining sons for you, I will by help of mantras laid down in the Atharva Veda, duly celebrate the famous ceremony, capable of crowning you with offspring.

sa_verse: ततः प्राक्रमदिष्टिं तां पुत्रीयां पुत्रकारणात्। जुहावाग्नौ च तेजस्वी मन्त्रदृष्टेन कर्मणा॥
en: Then with the view of obtaining sons (for the king,) that effulgent one set about the sonconferring ceremony; and in accordance with the ordinance, and with mantras, offered oblations to the sacrificial fire.

sa_verse: ताः समेत्य यथान्यायं तस्मिन् सदसि देवताः। अब्रुवँल्लोककर्तारं ब्रह्माणं वचनं ततः॥
en: And having duly assembled there, the deities addressed these words to Brahmă the lord of creatures.

sa_verse: भगवंस्त्वत्प्रसादेन रावणो नाम राक्षसः। सर्वान् नो बाधते वीर्याच्छासितुं तं न शक्नुमः॥
en: O you possessed of the six attributes, through your grace, a Rākşasa named Rāvaņa oppresses us all by his prowess, nor can we baffle him.

sa_verse: त्वया तस्मै वरो दत्तः प्रीतेन भगवंस्तदा। मानयन्तश्च तं नित्यं सर्वं तस्य क्षमामहे ॥
en: O lord, as you have well-pleased conferred on him a boon, we always suffer him in deference to it.

sa_verse: उद्वेजयति लोकांस्त्रीनुच्छ्रितान् द्वेष्टि दुर्मतिः। शक्रं त्रिदशराजानं प्रधर्षयितुमिच्छति॥
en: The wicked-minded one harasses the three worlds furnished with prosperity, and bears illwill against them and seek o defeat Indra, the king of Tridaśa.

sa_verse: ऋषीन् यक्षान् सगन्धर्वान् ब्राह्मणानसुरांस्तदा। अतिक्रामति दुर्धर्षो वरदानेन मोहितः॥
en: And blinded by the boon he has received, that irrepressible one intends to bring down the lord himself of the celestials, and the Yakşas, and the Gandharvas, and the Brahmanas, and the Asuras.

sa_verse: नैनं सूर्यः प्रतपति पार्श्वे वाति न मारुतः। चलोर्मिमाली तं दृष्ट्वा समुद्रोऽपि न कम्पते॥
en: And the Sun does not burn him, or the Wind blow about him; and at sight of him, that one engarlanded with billows, the Ocean, dares not stir.

sa_verse: तन्महन्नोभयं तस्माद् राक्षसाद् घोरदर्शनात्। वधार्थं तस्य भगवन्नुपायं कर्तुमर्हसि॥
en: Therefore, great is the fear that afflict us, coming from that Rākşasa of dreadful appearance. And, O lord, it behoves you to devise some means for destroying him.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः सुरैः सर्वैश्चिन्तयित्वा ततोऽब्रवीत्। हन्तायं विदितस्तस्य वधोपायो दुरात्मनः॥ तेन गन्धर्वयक्षाणां देवतानां च रक्षसाम्। अवध्योऽस्मीति वागुक्ता तथेत्युक्तं च तन्मया॥
en: Thus addressed by the deities in a body, he said, Alas! I have, however, decided on the means of destroying that wicked-souled one. He had asked, 'May I be incapable of being slain by Gandharvas, and Yaksas, and gods, and Raksasas!,-whereat I said, 'Be it so!'

sa_verse: नाकीर्तयदवज्ञानात् तद् रक्षो मानुषांस्तदा। तस्मात्स मानुषाद्वध्यो मृत्युर्नान्योऽस्य विद्यते॥
en: Through disdain, the Rākşasa did not at that time mention men. Therefore, by men alone he is capable of being slain; nor can his end be compassed by any other means.

sa_verse: एतच्छ्रुत्वा प्रियं वाक्यं ब्रह्मणा समुदाहृतम्। देवा महर्षयः सर्वे प्रहृष्टास्तेऽभवंस्तदा॥
en: Hearing this welcome speech uttered by Brahmā, the deities and the Maharșis became exceedingly delighted.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे विष्णुरुपयातो महाद्युतिः। शङ्खचक्रगदापाणिः पीतवासा जगत्पतिः॥ वैनतेयं समारुह्य भास्करस्तोयदं यथा। तप्तहाटककेयूरो वन्द्यमानः सुरोत्तमैः॥ ब्रह्मणा च समागत्य तत्र तस्थौ समाहितः।
en: At this juncture, that lord of the universe, the highly-eifulgent Visnu, clad in yellow apparel, and bearing in his hands the conch, the discus, and the mace, and adorned with burnished Keyūras, (A bracelet worn on the upper arm.) arrived there, riding Vinatā's son; like to the Sun riding the clouds. And worshipped by the foremost of the celestials, he drew near Brahmā, and sat down with a collected mind.

sa_verse: तमब्रुवन् सुराः सर्वे समभिष्ट्रय संनताः॥ त्वां नियोक्ष्यामहे विष्णो लोकानां हितकाम्यया।
en: And bending low before him, the deities spoke to him saying, O Vişnu, for the benefit of the worlds, we shall appoint you to some work.

sa_verse: राज्ञो दशरथस्य त्वमयोध्याधिपतेर्विभो॥ धर्मज्ञस्य वदान्यस्य महार्षिसमतेजसः। अस्य भार्यासु तिसृषु ह्रीश्रीकीर्युपमासु च॥ विष्णो पुत्रत्वमागच्छ कृत्वाऽऽत्मानं चतुर्विधम्। तत्र त्वं मानुषो भूत्वा प्रवृद्धं लोककण्टकम्॥ अवध्यं दैवतैर्विष्णो समरे जहि रावणम्। स हि देवान् सगन्धर्वान् सिद्धांश्च ऋषिसत्तमान् ॥
en: Do you, O lord, dividing yourself into four, O Vişņu, become born a sons in the three wives, resembling Modesty, Auspiciousness, and Fame, of Ayodhyā's lord, king Dasaratha, cognizant of Duty, and munificent, and possessing energy, and like to a Maharsi. Do you, O Visnu, becoming man, slay in battle this thorn of the worlds, the pampered Răvaņa, incapable of being slain by the gods; for the foolish Rākşasa by virtue of sublimated prowess, baffles the deities, and the Gandharvas, and the Siddhas, and the foremost of saints.

sa_verse: राक्षसो रावणो मूल् वीर्योद्रेकेण बाधते। ऋषयश्च ततस्तेन गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा॥ क्रीडन्तो नन्दनवने रौद्रेण विनिपातिताः।
en: And by him bereft of the sense of right and wrong, have saints and Gandharvas and Apsaras sporting in the groves of Nandana, been wantonly slain.

sa_verse: वधार्थं वयमायातास्तस्य वै मुनिभिः सह ॥ सिद्धगन्धर्वयक्षाश्च ततस्त्वां शरणं गताः। त्वं गतिः परमा देव सर्वेषां नः परंतप ॥
en: It is to compass his death that accompanied by the ascetics, we have come here: it is for this that the Siddhas and the Gandharvas and the Yaksas have taken refuge in you! O God, you are the prime way of us all, O repressor of foes! Do you, for bringing destruction to the enemies of the gods, turn your thoughts to being born as man.

sa_verse: वधाय देवशत्रूणां नृणां लोके मनः कुरु। एवं स्तुतस्तु देवेशो विष्णुस्त्रिदशपुङ्गवः॥ पितामहपुरोगांस्तान् सर्वलोकनमस्कृतः। अब्रवीत् त्रिदशान् सर्वान् समेतान् धर्मसंहितान्॥
en: Thus besought, that foremost of gods and chief of celestials, Visnu, worshipped of all creatures, addressed the assembled deities, following Duty, with the grand-sire at their head, saying.

sa_verse: भयं त्यजत भद्रं वो हितार्थं युधि रावणम्। सपुत्रपौत्रं सामात्यं समन्त्रिज्ञातिबान्धवम्॥ हत्वा क्रूरं दुराधर्ष देवर्षीणां भयावहम्। दशवर्षसहस्राणि दशवर्षशतानि च॥
en: Do you renounce fear! For your behoof, slaying in battle the wily and irrepressible Răvaņa, dreadful to the saints and the celestials, together with his sons, and grandsons, and friends, and counsellors, and relatives, and acquaintances, I will abide among mortals, ruling this earth for ten thousand and as many hundred years.

sa_verse: वत्स्यामि मानुषे लोके पालयन् पृथिवीमिमाम्। एवं दत्त्वा वरं देवो देवानां विष्णुरात्मवान्॥
en: Having thus conferred a boon upon the gods, the lord Vişnu of subdued soul fell to thinking as to the place where he would be born among men.

sa_verse: मानुष्ये चिन्तयामास जन्मभूमिमथात्मनः। ततः पद्मपलाशाक्षः कृत्वाऽऽत्मानं चतुर्विधम्॥ पितरं रोचयामास तदा दशरथं नृपम्।
en: Then that one of eyes resembling lotus-petals, dividing self into four parts, chose even king Dasaratha for his father.

sa_verse: ततो देवर्षिगन्धर्वाः सरुद्राः साप्सरोगणाः। स्तुतिभिर्दिव्यरूपाभिस्तुष्टुवुर्मधुसूदनम्॥
en: Thereat the celestials and the saints and the Gandharvas and the Rudras and the Apsaras hymned the Slayer of Madhu in excellent hymns.

sa_verse: तमुद्धतं रावणमुग्रतेजसं प्रवृद्धदर्पं त्रिदशेश्वरद्विषम्। विरावणं साधुतपस्विकण्टकं तपस्विनामुद्धर तं भयावहम्॥
en: Do you utterly uproot the haughty Råvaņa of fierce prowess and enhanced insolence-that foe of the lord of celestials, who is the occasion of the tears of the three * Lit. the thorns of pious ascetics.

sa_verse: तमेव हत्वा सबलं सबान्धवं विरावणं रावणमुग्रपौरुषम्। स्वर्लोकमागच्छ गतज्वरश्चिरं सुरेन्द्रगुप्तं गतदोषकल्मषम्॥
en: Slaying that one of terrible prowess, who distresses the three worlds, with his forces and friends, do you, O foremost of gods, your fever of heart removed, repair to the celestial regions protected by you and purged of all its faults and sins.

sa_verse: ततो नारायणो विष्णुर्नियुक्तः सुरसत्तमैः। जानन्नपि सुरानेवं श्लक्ष्णं वचनमब्रवीत्॥ उपायः को वधे तस्य राक्षसाधिपतेः सुराः। यमहं तं समास्थाय निहन्यामृषिकण्टकम्॥
en: Thus besought by the foremost of the celestials, that searcher of hearts, Visnu, although cognizant of the means whereby Rāvana was to be destroyed, asked the gods these amiable words,-What, you gods, is the means of compassing which I could slay that thorn of the ascetics? Nara means a multitude, and Ayana, dwellingplace. He whose dwelling-place is a multitude, is Nārāyana. Metaphorically, the word means evidently the Scorcher of hearts, worlds, and dreadful to ascetics!

sa_verse: एवमुक्ताः सुराः सर्वे प्रत्यूचुर्विष्णुमव्ययम्। मानुषं रूपमास्थाय रावणं जहि संयुगे॥
en: Thus addressed, the deities answered Visnu, incapable of deterioration, saying,-Assuming the form of a human being, do you in battle slay Rāvana!

sa_verse: स हि तेपे तपस्तीवं दीर्घकालमरिंदमः। येन तुष्टोऽभवद् ब्रह्मा लोककृल्लोकपूर्वजः॥
en: He, O repressor of foes, had for a long course of time performed rigid austerities; and thereat, that creator of all, the first-create Brahmā, was well pleased.

sa_verse: संतुष्टः प्रददौ तस्मै राक्षसाय वरं प्रभुः। नानाविधेभ्यो भूतेभ्यो भयं नान्यत्र मानुषात्॥
en: And propitiated by his penances, the Master conferred a boon on the Raksasa to the effect, that, save man, no fear should come to him from the various beings.

sa_verse: अवज्ञाताः पुरा तेन वरदाने हि मानवाः। एवं पितामहात् तस्माद् वरदानेन गर्वितः॥
en: And in the matter of that boon-bestowing, man had formerly been disregarded (by Ravana). And puffed up with pride in consequence of the boon he received from the Grand-sire.

sa_verse: उत्सादयति लोकांस्त्रीन् स्त्रियश्चाप्युपकर्षति। तस्मात् तस्य वधो दृष्टो मानुषेभ्यः परंतप॥
en: He commits ravages upon the three worlds and carries away the fair sex by violence. Therefore, O subduer of enemies, we have even fixed upon man for bringing about his death.

sa_verse: इत्येतद् वचनं श्रुत्वा सुराणां विष्णुरात्मवान्। पितरं रोचयामास तदा दशरथं नृपम्॥
en: Hearing this speech of the celestials, Vişnu of subdued soul chose even king Dasaratha for his father.

sa_verse: स चाप्यपुत्रो नृपतिस्तस्मिन् काले महाद्युतिः। अयजद् पुत्रियामिष्टिं पुत्रेप्सुररिसूदनः॥
en: At that time, eagerly wishing to have sons, that destroyer of enemies, the effulgent king Dasaratha, who was sonless, was celebrating the sacrifice that confer male offspring.

sa_verse: स कृत्वा निश्चयं विष्णुरामन्त्र्य च पितामहम्। अन्तर्धानं गतो देवैः पूज्यमानो महर्षिभिः॥
en: Then, having ascertained the course to follow, Visnu, having greeted the Grand-sire, vanished there, worshipped by deities and the Maharsis.

sa_verse: ततो वै यजमानस्य पावकादतुलप्रभम्। प्रादुर्भूतं महद् भूतं महावीर्य महाबलम्॥ कृष्णं रक्ताम्बरधरं रक्तास्यं दुन्दुभिस्वनम्। स्निग्धहर्यक्षतनुजश्मश्रुप्रवरमूर्धजम्॥ शुभलक्षणसम्पन्नं दिव्याभरणभूषितम्। शैलशृङ्गसमुत्सेधं दृप्तशार्दूलविक्रमम्॥ दिवाकरसमाकारं दीप्तानलशिखोपमम्। तप्तजाम्बूनदमयों राजतान्तपरिच्छदाम्॥ दिव्यपायससम्पूर्णां पात्री पत्नीमिव प्रियाम्। प्रगृह्य विपुलां दोभ्यां स्वयं मायामयीमिव ॥
en: And then himself bearing in his hands a capacious vessel made of burnished gold, with a silver cover, dear like that of a spouse, and resembling the divine Creative energy, filled with celestial Payasa, (A preparation of milk, rice, and sugar). from out the sacrificial fire of Dasaratha initiated into the ceremony, there arose a mighty being, of unparalleled prowess, high energy, and huge strength, black, and wearing a crimson apparel, with a red face, uttering the blares of a trumpet, and having a body covered with leonine hair, having whiskers and an excellent head of hair, furnished with auspicious marks, and adorned with celestial ornaments, and resembling a mountain-peak, and bearing the prowess of a flaming tiger, and like to the Sun or tongues of flaming fire. And with his eyes fixed upon Dasaratha.

sa_verse: समवेक्ष्याब्रवीद् वाक्यमिदं दशरथं नृपम्। प्राजापत्यं नरं विद्धि मामिहाभ्यागतं नृप॥
en: He addressed the king, saying, O monarch, take me as a person commissioned by Prajāpati.

sa_verse: ततः परं तदा राजा प्रत्युवाच कृताञ्जलिः। भगवन् स्वागतं तेऽस्तु किमहं करवाणि ते॥
en: Hearing him speak thus, Dasaratha, with folded hands, said, Lord, you are welcomed? What can I do for you?

sa_verse: अथो पुनरिदं वाक्यं प्राजापत्यो नरोऽब्रवीत्। राजन्नर्चयता देवानद्य प्राप्तमिदं त्वया ॥
en: Thereupon, that person despatched by Prajapati again spoke thus, O king, having adored the deities, you have to-day obtained this.

sa_verse: इदं तु नृपशार्दूल पायसं देवनिर्मितम्। प्रजाकरं गृहाण त्वं धन्यमारोग्यवर्धनम्॥ भार्याणामनुरूपाणामश्नीतेति प्रयच्छ वै। तासु त्वं लप्स्यसे पुत्रान् यदर्थं यजसे नृप॥
en: O tiger of a king, accept this excellent and divinely-prepared Pāyasa, conferring sons, health, and affluence, which you are to give to your worthy consorts, saying, Partake it. Through them you will, O monarch, obtain sons, for obtaining whom you have performed this sacrifice.

sa_verse: तथेति नृपतिः प्रीतः शिरसा प्रतिगृह्य ताम्। पात्री देवानसम्पूर्णा देवदत्तां हिरण्मयीम्॥ अभिवाद्य च तद्भूतमद्भुतं प्रियदर्शनम्। मुदा परमया युक्तश्चकाराभिप्रदक्षिणम्॥
en: Thereupon, saying, So be it, the lord of men delightedly placed that divinely-bestowed golden vessel filled with the divine Payasa upon his head. And having saluted that wonderful being of gracious presence, he in excess of joy began to go round him again and again.

sa_verse: ततो दशरथः प्राप्य पायसं देवनिर्मितम्। बभूव परमप्रीतः प्राप्य वित्तमिवाधनः॥ ततस्तदद्भुतप्रख्यं भूतं परमभास्वरम्। संवर्तयित्वा तत् कर्म तत्रैवान्तरधीयत॥
en: Then Daśaratha, having received that divinely prepared Pāyasa, waxed exceeding glad; like that of a pauper attaining plenty. Then that highly effulgent being of a wonderful form, having performed that mission of his, vanished even there.

sa_verse: हर्षरश्मिभिरुद्योतं तस्यान्तः पुरमाबभौ। शारदस्याभिरामस्य चन्द्रस्येव नभोंऽशुभिः॥
en: And Dasaratha's inner apartment, being graced with the rays of joy, looked like the welkin flooded with the lovely beams of the autumnal moon.

sa_verse: सोऽन्तःपुरं प्रविश्यैव कौसल्यामिदमब्रीत्। पायसं प्रतिगृह्णीष्व पुत्रीयं त्विदमात्मनः॥
en: Then entering the inner apartment, he spoke to Kausalyā, saying, Take you this, Pāyasa; for this will make you bear a son.

sa_verse: कौसल्यायै नरपतिः पायसार्धं ददौ तदा। अर्धादर्धं ददौ चापि सुमित्रायै नराधिप२७ ।।
en: Having said this, the king offered to her a half portion of this Pāyasa.'Then he conferred upon Sumitrā a fourth of it.

sa_verse: कैकेय्यै चावशिष्टार्धं ददौ पुत्रार्थकारणात्। प्रददौ चावशिष्टार्धं पायसस्यामृतोपमम्॥ अनुचिन्त्य सुमित्रायै पुनरेव महामतिः। एवं तासां ददौ राजा भार्याणां पायसं पृथक् ॥
en: Then in order that she might have a son, king Dasaratha made over to Kaikeyi an equal portion of what remained. And then having reflected, the mighty-minded one gave to Sumitrā the remaining portion of the Pāyasa resembling ambrosia.

sa_verse: ताश्चैवं पायसं प्राप्य नरेन्द्रस्योत्तमस्त्रियः। सम्मानं मेनिरे सर्वाः प्रहर्षोदितचेतसः॥
en: Thus the king dispensed the Pāyasa to each and all of his wives. And those foremost wives of the king, having received that Pāyasa, became exceedingly delighted, and considered themselves as highly honoured.

sa_verse: ततस्तु ताः प्राश्य तमुत्तमस्त्रियो महीपतेरुत्तमपायसं पृथक्। ऽचिरेण गर्भान् प्रतिपेदिरे तदा॥
en: Then those excellent consorts of the lord of earth, having separately partaken of that choice Pāyasa, shortly bore offspring, resembling fire or the Sun.

sa_verse: ततस्तु राजा प्रतिवीक्ष्य ताः स्त्रियः प्ररूढगर्भाः प्रतिलब्धमानसः। बभूव हृष्टिस्त्रिदिवे यथा हरिः सुरेन्द्रसिद्धर्षिगणाभिपूजितः॥
en: The king, beholding those wives of his bearing children, obtained his desire and became delighted; Vişnu, while being worshipped by the Siddhas and the ascetics. even as

sa_verse: पुत्रत्वं तु गते विष्णौ राज्ञस्तस्य महात्मनः। उवाच देवताः सर्वाः स्वयंभूर्भगवानिदम्॥
en: When Vişņu had accepted the sonship of that high-souled king, the self-create Lord addressed the celestials, saying,

sa_verse: सत्यसंधस्य वीरस्य सर्वेषां नो हितैषिणः। तनूषु च। : सहायान् बलिनः सृजध्वं कामरूपिणः॥ मायाविदश्च शूरांश्च वायुवेगसमान् जवे। नयज्ञान् बुद्धिसम्पन्नान् विष्णुतुल्यपराक्रमान्॥ असंहार्यानुपायज्ञान् दिव्यसंहननान्वितान्। सर्वास्त्रगुणसम्पन्नानमृतप्राशनानिव॥
en: For assisting the heroic Vişnu firm in promise, always seeking the welfare of us all, do you create powerful beings, assuming shapes at will, cognizant of illusions, heroic, furnished with the celerity of the wind, versed in morality, possessing intelligence, like to Vişņu in prowess, unslayable, knowing the ways and means (of war and peace), gifted with excellent bodies, capable of resisting all weapons, and resembling immortals.

sa_verse: अप्सरःसु च मुख्यासु गन्धर्वीणां यक्षपन्नगकन्यासु ऋक्षविद्याधरीषु च ॥ किन्नरीषां च गात्रेषु वानरीणां तनूषु च। सृजध्वं हरिरूपेण पुत्रांस्तुल्यपराक्रमान्॥
en: And from forth the bodies of the foremost Apsaras, Gandharvis, Yaksis, and Pannagis, Rks is (She-bears) and Vidhyādharis, Kinnaris and Vānaris (She-monkeys) do you produce sons wearing the shapes of monkeys.

sa_verse: पूर्वमेव मया सृष्टो जाम्बवानृक्षपुङ्गवः। जृम्भमाणस्य सहसा मम वक्त्रादजायत।७।।
en: Formerly I had created that foremost of bears, Jāmbavān, who suddenly came out of my mouth as I was yawning.

sa_verse: ते तथोक्ता भगवता तत् प्रतिश्रुत्य शासनम्। जनयामासुरेवं ते पुत्रान् वानररूपिणः॥
en: Hearing this mandate of Him possessed of the six attributes, they began to produce sons endowed with monkey's forms.

sa_verse: ऋषयश्च महात्मानः सिद्धविद्याधरोरगाः। चारणाश्च सुतान् वीरान् ससृजुर्वनचारिणः॥
en: And high-souled ascetics, and Siddhas, and Vidyādharas, and Uragas, and Caranas, generated heroic sons, rangers of woods.

sa_verse: वानरेन्द्र महेन्द्राभमिन्द्रो वालिनमात्मजम्। सुग्रीवं जनयामास तपनस्तपतां वरः॥
en: And Indra beget as his son that foremost of monkeys, Vāli, resembling the Mahendra hill, and that best of those imparting heat, the Sun, Sugriva.

sa_verse: बृहस्पतिस्त्वजनयत् तारं नाम महाकपिम्। सर्ववानरमुख्यानां बुद्धिमन्तमनुत्तमम्॥
en: Brhaspati beget the mighty ape named Tāra, who was the most excellent and intelligent of the prime monkeys.

sa_verse: धनदस्य सुतः श्रीमान् वानरो गन्धमादनः। विश्वकर्मा त्वजनयन्त्रलं नाम महाकपिम्।।
en: The Bestower of riches beget as his son the graceful ape Gandhamādana. And Viśvakarman beget that mighty monkey named Nala.

sa_verse: पावकस्य सुतः श्रीमान् नीलोऽग्निसदृशप्रभः। तेजसा यशसा वीर्यादत्यरिच्यत वीर्यवान्॥
en: Agni beget as his son the powerful and graceful Nīla in effulgence like to the fire, who surpassed even his sire in energy, prowess, and renown.

sa_verse: रूपद्रविणसंपन्नावश्विनौ रूपसंमतौ। मैन्दं च द्विविदं चैव जनयामासतुः स्वयम्॥
en: And the beautiful Asvins, endowed with the wealth of loveliness, beget Mainda and Dvivida.

sa_verse: वरुणो जनयामास सुषेणं नाम वानरम्। शरभं जनयामास पर्जन्यस्तु महाबलः॥
en: Varuņa beget the monkey named Sușeņa; and Parjanya beget Sarabha, possessed of great strength.

sa_verse: मारुतस्यौरसः श्रीमान् हनूमान् नाम वानरः। वज्रसंहननोपेतो वैनतेयसमो जवे॥
en: And the Wind-god beget the graceful monkey named Hanumān, endued with a frame hard as adamant; in fleetness like to Vinatā's offspring.

sa_verse: सर्ववानरमुख्येषु बुद्धिमान् बलवानपि। ते सृष्टा बहुसाहस्रा दशग्रीववधोद्यताः॥ अप्रमेयबला वीरा विक्रान्ताः कामरूपिणः। ते गजाचलसंकाशा वपुष्मन्तो महाबलाः॥ ऋक्षवानरगोपुच्छाः क्षिप्रमेवाभिजज्ञिरे।
en: And the most intelligent as well as the most powerful amongst all the principal monkeys. Thus produced, there suddenly came into being by thousands, mighty bears, and monkeys, and Gopucchas, (Cow-tailed monkeys.) and of immeasurable strength, and heroic, and powerful, assuming shapes at will, endowed with bodies resembling elephants or hills, even those who would engage in compassing the destruction of the Ten-headed Rāvana.

sa_verse: यस्य देवस्य यद्रूपं वेषो यश्च पराक्रमः॥ अजायत समं तेन तस्य तस्य पृथक् पृथक् । गोलाङ्गुलेषु चोत्पन्नाः किंचिदुनतविक्रमाः॥
en: The sons of the deities retained distinctly the respective hues, forins, and prowess, that characterized their several sires. And those that sprang from the Golāngulas, possessed even more than the might of the gods.

sa_verse: ऋक्षीषु च तथा जाता वानराः किन्नरीषु च। देवा महर्षिगन्धर्वास्तार्क्ष्ययक्षा यशस्विनः॥ नागाः किंपुरुषाश्चैव सिद्धविद्याधरोरगाः। बहवो जनयामासुहृष्टास्तत्र सहस्रशः॥
en: Likewise, on Rksis and Kinnaris were gladly begot thousands upon thousands of monkeys, by the gods, Maharsis, Gandharvas, Tarksyas, and famous Yaksas, Nagas, and Kimpurusas (beings half-man and half-beast) Siddhas, Vidyadharas, and Uragas.

sa_verse: चारणाश्च सुतान् वीरान् ससृजुर्वनचारिणः। वानरान् सुमहाकायान् सर्वान् वै वनचारिणः॥ अप्सरःसु च मुख्यासु तथा विद्याधरीषु च। नागकन्यासु च तदा गन्धर्वीणां तनूषु च। कामरूपबलोपेता यथाकामविचारिणः॥
en: Upon the principal Apsaris, and the Vidyadharis, and the daughters of the Nāgas, and the Gandharvis were begot by the Cāraņas as sons, heroic monkeys of gigantic bodies, ranging the forests and living on fruits and roots.

sa_verse: सिंहशार्दूलसदृशा दर्पण च बलेन च। शिलाप्रहरणाः सर्वे सर्वे पर्वतयोधिनः॥
en: All these monkeys were endowed with strength; and could assume shapes and repair everywhere, at will. And they were like to lions and tigers, both in pride and in prowess. And they fought with crags and hurled hills.

sa_verse: मखदंष्ट्रायुधाः सर्वे सर्वे सर्वास्त्रकोविदाः। विचालयेयुः शैलेन्द्रान् भेदयेयुः स्थिरान् द्रुमान्॥
en: And they fought with nails and teeth, and were accomplished in all weapons. And they could move the largest hills, and crush the fixed trees,

sa_verse: क्षोभयेयुश्च वेगेन समुद्र सरितां पतिम्। दारयेयुः क्षितिं पद्भ्यामाप्लवेयुर्महार्णवान्॥
en: With their impetus, vex that lord of rivers, the Ocean. And they could with their kicks rend the Earth, and swim over the mighty main.

sa_verse: नभस्तलं विशेयुश्च गृह्णीयुरपि तोयदान्। गृह्णीयुरपि मातङ्गान् मत्तान् प्रव्रजतो वने॥
en: They could penetrate into the welkin, and capture the clouds. And they could subdue mad elephants ranging the forest.

sa_verse: नर्दमानांश्च नादेन पातयेयुर्विहङ्गमान्। ईदृशानां प्रसूतानि हरीणां कामरूपिणाम्॥ शतं शतसहस्राणि यूथपानां महात्मनाम्।
en: And with their roars, they could bring down birds singing. Thus came into being Koțis of high-souled leaders of monkey-herds, assuming forms at will.

sa_verse: ते प्रधानेषु यथेषु हरीणां हरियूथपाः॥ बभूवुर्मूथपश्रेष्ठान् वीरांश्चाजनयन् हरीन्।
en: And these became the leaders of the principal monkey-herds; and they, in their turn, generated heroic monkeys, the foremost of the leaders of herds.

sa_verse: अन्ये ऋक्षवतः प्रस्थानुपतस्थुः सहस्रशः॥ अन्ये नानाविधाञ्छैलान् काननानि च भेजिरे।
en: Some of these monkeys began to dwell on the top of the Rkşavān mountain; while others inhabited various other mountains and forests.

sa_verse: सूर्यपुत्रं च सुग्रीवं शक्रपुत्रं च वालिनम्॥ भ्रातरावुपतस्थुस्ते सर्वे च हरियूथपाः। नलं नीलं हनूमन्तमन्यांश्च हरियूथपान्॥ ते तार्क्ष्यबलसम्पन्नः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः। विचरन्तोऽर्दयन् सर्वान् सिंहव्याघ्रमहोरगान्॥
en: All the leaders of monkey-herds stayed with those brothers, Sugriva, the son of the Sun-god and Vali, that of Sakra, and also with Nala, and Nila, and Hanuman, and other leaders of monkey-herds. And endowed with the might of Garuda, and accomplished in fight, they ranged around, pounding lions, and tigers, and mighty Uragas.

sa_verse: महाबलो महाबाहुर्वाली विपुलविक्रमः। जुगोप भुजवीर्येण ऋक्षगोपुच्छवानरान् ॥
en: And the mighty-armed Vālī of great prowess and redoubtable strength protected by virtue of the energy of his arms Ķkşas, and Gopucchas, and monkeys.

sa_verse: तैरियं पृथिवी शूरैः सपर्वतवनार्णवा। कीर्णा विविधसंस्थानैर्नानाव्यञ्जनलक्षणैः॥ तैर्मेघवृन्दाचलकूटसंनिभैर्महाबलैर्वानरयूथपाधिपैः। बभूव भूर्भीमशरीररूपैः समावृता रामसहायहेतोः॥
en: This earth, furnished with mountains, and forests, and oceans, began to teem with those heroic lords of leaders of monkey-herds, inhabiting different places, bearing characteristic marks, resembling masses of clouds, mountain-peaks, possessed of mighty strength, and of terrible bodies and visages, in order that they might assist Rāma. or

sa_verse: समाप्तदीक्षानियमः पत्नीगणसमन्वितः। प्रविवेश पुरी राजा सभृत्यबलवाहनः॥
en: And the monarch, having observed all the rules of initiation, entered the palace with his equipage and retinue.

sa_verse: यथार्ह पूजितास्तेन राज्ञा च पृथिवीश्वराः। मुदिताः प्रययुर्देशान् प्रणम्य मुनिपुङ्गवम्॥
en: And the lords of the earth, having been received suitably by the king, with glad hearts set out for their own countries, saluting that foremost of ascetics (Rsyaśặnga).

sa_verse: श्रीमतां गच्छतां तेषां स्वगृहाणि पुरात्ततः। बलानि राज्ञां शुभ्राणि प्रहृष्टानि चकाशिरे॥
en: And clad in bright apparel, the delighted Own forces belonging to those graceful kings repairing to their homes, looked exceedingly beautiful.

sa_verse: गतेषु पृथिवीशेषु राजा दशरथः पुनः। प्रविवेश पुरीं श्रीमान् पुरस्कृत्य द्विजोत्तमान्॥
en: When the lords of the earth had gone away, the graceful king Dasaratha re-entered his palace, with the foremost of regenerate ones at his head.

sa_verse: शान्तया प्रययौ सार्धमृष्यशृङ्गः सुपूजितः। अनुगम्यमानो राज्ञा च सानुयात्रेण धीमता॥ एवं विसृज्य तान् सर्वान् राजा सम्पूर्णमानसः। उवास सुखितस्तत्र पुत्रोत्पत्तिं विचिन्तयन्।७।।
en: Followed by the intelligent monarch with his retinue, Rsyasrnga, having been duly honoured, set out with śāntā. Having thus dismissed them all, the king, his object attained, began to dwell there happily, expecting sons.

sa_verse: ततो यज्ञे समाप्ते तु ऋतूनां षट् समत्ययुः। ततश्च द्वादशे मासे चैत्रे नावमिके तिथौ॥ नक्षत्रेऽदितिदैवत्ये स्वोच्चसंस्थेषु पञ्चसु। ग्रहेषु कर्कटे लग्ने वाक्पताविन्दुना सह॥ प्रोद्यमाने जगन्नाथं सर्वलोकनमस्कृतम्। कौसल्याजनयद् रामं दिव्यलक्षणसंयुतम्॥ विष्णोरर्धं महाभागं पुत्रमैक्ष्वाकुनन्दनम्। लोहिताक्षं महाबाहुं रक्तोष्ठं दुन्दुभिस्वनम् ॥ कौसल्या शुशुभे तेन पुत्रेणामिततेजसा। यथा वरेण देवानामदितिर्वज्रपाणिना॥
en: And then when the six seasons had rolled away after the completion of the sacrifice, in the twelfth month, on the ninth lunar day, under the influence of the Punarvasu asterism presided by Aditi devatā, when the Sun, the Moon, Saturn, Jupiter, and Venus were at Arius, Capricorn, Libra, Cancer, And Pisces, and when Jupiter had arisen with the Moon at Cancer, Kausalyā gave birth to that lord of the universe, bowed to by all the worlds, Rāma, the descendant of Iksvaku, furnished with excellent marks, the one half of Vişnu,-exceedingly righteous, with rosy eyes, and mighty arms, and crimson nether lip, and endowed with a voice like the sound of a kettledrum. Then on having given birth to that son of immeasurable prowess, Kausalyā looked resplendent, like Aditi on having brought forth that foremost of celestials the wielder of the thunder-bolt.

sa_verse: भरतो नाम कैकेय्यां जज्ञे सत्यपराक्रमः। साक्षाद् विष्णोश्चतुर्भागः सर्वैः समुदितो गुणैः॥
en: Then was born of Kaikeyī, named Bharata, having truth for prowess, endowed with all the virtues, and representing the very fourth part of Visnu.

sa_verse: अथ लक्ष्मणशत्रुघ्नौ सुमित्राजनयत्सुतौ। वीरौ सर्वास्त्रकुशलौ विष्णोरर्धसमन्वितौ॥
en: Then Sumitrā gave birth to Lakşmaņa and Satrughna, heroic, and skilled in all weapons, and endowed with the half of Vişnu.

sa_verse: पुष्ये जातस्तु भरतो मीनलग्ने प्रसन्नधीः। सार्पे जातौ तु सौमित्री कुलीरेऽभ्युदिते रवौ॥
en: And Bharata of purged intelligence was born under the asterism Pușya, when the Sun had entered Pisces; while the two sons of Sumitrā were born when the Sun arose in Cancer, under the asterism of Aslesa. (The ninth lunar mansion)

sa_verse: राज्ञः पुत्रा महात्मानश्चत्वारो जज्ञिरे पृथक् । गुणवन्तोऽनुरूपाश्च रुच्या प्रोष्ठपदोपमाः॥ जगुः कलं च गन्धर्वा ननृतुश्चाप्सरोगणाः। देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुः पुष्पवृष्टिश्च खात् पतत्॥
en: Thus were separately born four high-souled sons to the king, crowned with qualities, and graceful, and in loveliness resembling the constellations Prosthapada.* Thereat the Gandharvas began to chaunt sweetly, and the Apsaras to dance. And the celestial kettledrums sounded; and there showered down blossoms from the sky. *Otherwise called Uttarabhadrapada and Purvabhādrapada.

sa_verse: उत्सवश्च महानासीदयोध्यायां जनाकुलः। रथ्याश्च जनसम्बाधा नटनर्तकसंकुलाः॥ गायनैश्च विराविण्यो वादनैश्च तथापरैः। विरेजुर्विपुलास्तत्र सर्वरत्नसमन्विनाम्॥ प्रदेयांश्च ददौ राजा सूतमागधबन्दिनाम्। ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददौ वित्तं गोधनानि सहस्रशः॥
en: And high festivities were commenced by the multitude in Ayodhyā. And the spacious highways became filled with players and dancers, glittering with all kinds of gems, and resounding with the music of singers and performers on instruments. And the king bestowed gifts upon bards and genealogists and panegyrists, and he also gave kine by thousands to Brāhmaṇas.

sa_verse: अतीत्यैकादशाहं तु नामकर्म तथाकरोत्। ज्येष्ठं रामं महात्मानं भरतं कैकयीसुतम्॥ सौमित्रिं लक्ष्मणमिति शत्रुघ्नमपरं तथा। वसिष्ठः परमप्रीतो नामानि कुरुते तदा॥
en: When the eleventh day had gone by, the king performed the Naming ceremony of his sons. And experiencing great delight, Vasistha conferred the names. And the high-souled eldest one was called Rāma; and Kaikeyi's son was called Bharata; and Sumitrā's son was called Laksmana, and the last was named Satrughna.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणान् भोजयामास पौरजानपदानपि। अददद् ब्राह्मणानां च रत्नौघममलं बहु॥
en: And the king fed the Brāhmaṇas as well as the inhabitants rural and urban; and he bestowed heaps of jewels upon Brāhmaṇas.

sa_verse: तेषां जन्मक्रियादीनि सर्वकर्माण्यकारयत्। तेषां केतुरिव ज्येष्ठो रामो रतिकरः पितुः॥ बभूव भूयो भूतानां स्वयम्भूरिव सम्मतः। सर्वे वेदविदः शूराः सर्वे लोकहिते रताः॥
en: Thus did he celebrate the natal rites of the princes. And among all those princes, the eldest, Rāma, like that of Ketu, (The ninth of the planets.) and the special delight of his father, became the object of general regard, even as the self-create Himself. And all of them were versed in the Vedas, and heroic, and intent upon the welfare of others.

sa_verse: सर्वे ज्ञानोपसम्पन्ना सर्वे समुदिता गुणैः। तेषामपि महातेजा रामः सत्यपराक्रमः॥ इष्टः सर्वस्य लोकस्य शशाङ्क इव निर्गलः। गजस्कन्धेऽश्वपृष्ठे च रथचर्यासु सम्मतः॥ धनुर्वेदे च निरतः पितुः शुश्रूषणे रतः।
en: And all were accomplished in knowledge; and endowed with virtues. And among them all, the exceedingly puissant Rāma, having true for prowess, was the desire of every one, and spotless like to the Moon himself. He could ride on elephants and horses, and was an adept in managing cars. And he was ever engaged in the study of arms, and aye occupied in ministering to his sire.

sa_verse: बाल्यात् प्रभृति सुस्निग्धो लक्ष्मणो लक्ष्मिवर्धनः॥ रामस्य लोकरामस्य भ्रातुर्येष्ठस्य नित्यशः। सर्वप्रियकरस्तस्य रामस्यापि शरीरतः॥ लक्ष्मणो लक्ष्मिसंपन्नो बहिःप्राण इवापरः। न च तेन विना निद्रां लभते पुरुषोत्तमः॥ मृष्टमन्त्रमुपानीतमश्नाति न हि तं विना।
en: And even from early youth, that enhancer of auspiciousness Laksmana was ever attached to his eldest brother Rāma, that delight of all. And like to another life of Rāma, Lakşmaņa furnished with auspiciousness was in everything attentive to Rāma's wishes, even at the neglect of his own person. And that foremost of persons did not even attain sleep without Rāma's company, nor did he partake any sweetmeat that was offered, unless Rāma partook it with him.

sa_verse: यदा हि हयमारूढो मृगयां याति राघवः॥ अथैनं पृष्ठतोऽभ्येति सधनुः परिपालयन्। भरतस्यापि शत्रुघ्नो लक्ष्मणावरजो हि सः॥ प्राणैः प्रियतरो नित्यं तस्य चासीत् तथा प्रियः।
en: When mounted on horse-back, Rāghava went a-hunting, Lakṣmaṇa went at his back bow in hand, protecting him. And that younger brother of Laksmana, Satrughna, likewise became ever dearer to Bharata than life itself.

sa_verse: स चतुर्भिर्महाभागैः पुत्रैर्दशरथः प्रियैः॥ बभूव परमप्रीतो देवैरिव पितामहः।
en: On account of those exalted and well-beloved sons of his, Dasaratha experienced the excess of joy, like to the Grand-sire on account of the gods.

sa_verse: ते यदा ज्ञानसम्पन्नाः सर्वे समुतदिता गुणैः॥ ह्रीमन्तः कीर्तिमन्तश्च सर्वज्ञा दीर्घदर्शिनः। तेषामेवंप्रभावाणां सर्वेषां दीप्ततेजसाम्॥ पिता दशरथो हृष्टो ब्रह्मा लोकाधिपो यथा।
en: And when they came to be furnished with knowledge, and crowned with virtues, and endowed with bashfulness and fame, and to attain wisdom in everything, and to be farsighted, Dasaratha, the father of such powerful and flamingly effulgent sons, became delighted even like that lord of worlds-Brahmā.

sa_verse: ते चापि मनुजव्याघ्रा वैदिकाध्ययने रताः॥ पितृशुश्रूषणरता धनुर्वेदे च निष्ठिताः।
en: Those tigers among men, ever engaged in the study of the Vedas, were accomplished in the art of archery, and always intent upon ministering to their father.

sa_verse: अथ राजा दशरथस्तेषां दारक्रियां प्रति॥ चिन्तयामास धर्मात्मा सोपाध्यायः सबान्धवः। तस्य चिन्तयमानस्य मन्त्रिमध्ये महात्मनः॥ अभ्यागच्छन्महातेजा विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः।
en: Once upon a time, when the virtuous king Dasaratha, surrounded by his priests and friends, was reflecting about the nuptials of his sons, to that high-souled one engaged in thought in the midst of his counsellors came the mighty ascetic Visvamitra.

sa_verse: स राज्ञो दर्शनाकानी द्वाराध्यक्षानुवाच ह॥ शीघ्रमाख्यात मां प्राप्तं कौशिकं गाधिनः सुतम्।
en: Desirous of seeing the king, he said to the warders,-Do you speedily announce that I, Gadhi's son, sprung in the Kusika line, have come!

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य राज्ञो वेश्म प्रदुद्रुवुः॥ सम्भ्रान्तमनसः सर्वे तेन वाक्येन चोदिताः। ते गत्वा राजभवनं विश्वामित्रमृषिं तदा॥ प्राप्तमावेदयामासुपायेक्ष्वाकवे तदा।
en: Hearing those words of his, they, urged on by them, all hurriedly began to run towards the royal chambers. And coming to the royal apartments, they communicated to Ikşváku's descendant the arrival of the ascetic Viśvāmitra.

sa_verse: तेषां तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा सपुरोधाः समाहितः।॥ प्रत्युज्जगाम संहृष्टो ब्रह्माणमिव वासवः।
en: Hearing those words of theirs, Dasaratha surrounded by his priests, went out delightedly to meet him, like Indra going out to meet Brhaspati. According to Śruti BỊhaspati is the Brahmă of the gods-Brhaspatir devānām Brahmā.

sa_verse: स दृष्ट्वा ज्वलितं दीप्त्या तापसं संशितव्रतम्॥ प्रहृष्टवदनो राजा ततोऽर्घ्यमुपहारयत्।
en: And having come to that ascetic observing vows and of flaming energy, the monarch with a cheerful countenance offered him the Arghya.

sa_verse: स राज्ञः प्रतिगृह्याऱ्या शास्त्रदृष्टेन कर्मणा॥ कुशलं चाव्ययं चैव पर्यपृच्छन्नराधिपम्।
en: There upon, having accepted the king's Arghya in accordance with the ordinance he enquired of the lord of men as to his continued prosperity and peace.

sa_verse: कोशे जनपदे बान्धवेषु सुहृत्सु च ॥ कुशलं कौशिको राज्ञः पर्यपृच्छत् सुधार्मिकः।
en: And the exceedingly virtuous descendant of Kusika asked the king concerning the welfare of the exchequer, and the provinces; and the peace of his friends and acquaintances.

sa_verse: अपि ते संनताः सर्वे सामन्तरिपवो जिताः॥ दैवं च मानुषं चैव कर्म ते साध्वनुष्ठितम्।
en: And are they captains submissive: and have you vanquished your foes? And have you performed well the human and the divine rites?

sa_verse: वसिष्ठं च समागम्य कुशलं मुनिपुङ्गवः॥ ऋषींश्च तान् यथान्यायं महाभाग उवाच ह।
en: And approaching Vasiştha and the other anchorites, that foremost of ascetics of exalted piety duly asked them regarding their welfare.

sa_verse: ते सर्वे हृष्टमनसस्तस्य राज्ञो निवेशनम्॥ विविशुः पूजितास्तेन निषेदुश्च यथार्हतः।
en: And having been properly received by the monarch, they with glad hearts entered the royal residence, and sat them down according to precedence.

sa_verse: अथ हृष्टमना राजा विश्वामित्रं महामुनिम्॥ उवाच परमोदारो हृष्टस्तमभिपूजयन्।
en: Then gladly worshipping the mighty ascetic, Visvāmitra, the exceedingly generous king, wellpleased, addressed him saying.

sa_verse: यथामृतस्य सम्प्राप्तिर्यथा वर्षमनूदके॥ वै यथा सदृशदारेषु पुत्रजन्माप्रजस्य प्रणष्टस्य यथा लाभो यथा हर्षों महोदयः॥ तथैवागमनं मन्ये स्वागतं ते महामुने। कं च ते परमं कामं करोमि किमु हर्षितः॥
en: Like that of the obtaining of ambrosia, like to a shower in a land suffering from drought, like to the birth of sons of worthy wives to him without issue, like that of the recovery of a lost thing, yea, like to the dawning of a mighty joy, I consider this your arrival. O illustrious ascetic, are you well come? What is even that which is nearest to your heart?

sa_verse: पात्रभूतोऽसि मे ब्रह्मन् दिष्ट्या प्राप्तोऽसि मानद। अद्य मे सफलं जन्म जीवितं च सुजीवितम्॥
en: You, O Brahmana, are worthy of my best services. By luck it is that, O bestower of honour, I have gained you. To-day my birth has been crowned with fruit to-day has my life attained its object.

sa_verse: यस्माद् विप्रेन्द्रमद्राक्षं सुप्रभाता निशा मम। पूर्वं राजर्षिशब्देन तपसा द्योतितप्रभः॥ ब्रह्मर्षित्वमनुप्राप्तः पूज्योऽसि बहुधा मया। तदद्भुतमभूद्विप्र पवित्रं परमं मम॥
en: And truly yesterday night has been succeeded by an auspicious morning, since I have beheld you. Having first attained exceeding effulgence by virtue of austerities performed for obtaining the title of Rājarşi, you have afterwards obtained the status of a Brahmarși. You are worthy of manifold homage from me. This your exceedingly holy arrival appear wonderful.

sa_verse: शुभक्षेत्रगतश्चाहं तव संदर्शनात् प्रभो। ब्रूहि यत् प्रार्थितं तुभ्यं कार्यमागमनं प्रति ॥
en: O lord, by beholding you, surely my body has been rendered pure. Tell me, what is it that you would have, and what is the purpose of your coming?

sa_verse: इच्छाम्यनुगृहीतोऽहं त्वदर्थं परिवृद्धये। कार्यस्य न विमर्श च गन्तुमर्हसि सुव्रत॥ कर्ता चाहमशेषेण दैवतं हि भवान् मम। मम चायमनुप्राप्तो महानभ्युदयो द्विज। तवागमनजः कृत्स्नो धर्मश्चानुत्तमो द्विज ॥
en: I wish that I may be obliged by doing your will. And, ( you of excellent vows, you ought not to hesitate. I will every way accomplish your will; for you are my god. O regenerate one, surely great prosperity come to me in consequence of your coming, inasmuch as it shall be the means of bringing me entire and excellent merit, O Brāhmaṇa!

sa_verse: इति हृदयसुखं निशम्य वाक्यं श्रुतिसुखमात्मवता विनीतमुक्तम्। प्रथितगुणयशा गुणैर्विशिष्टः परमऋषिः परमं जगाम हर्षम्॥
en: Hearing this soul-soothing, ear-charming, and free-humble speech that was uttered, the illustrious prime of ascetics crowned with virtues, and furnished with all perfections, experienced exceeding delight.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा राजसिंहस्य वाक्यमद्भुतविस्तरम्। हृष्टरोमा महातेजा विश्वामित्रोऽभ्यभाषत ॥
en: Hearing those astonishing words of that lionlike king, the highly-energetic Viśvāmitra with his down standing on end, said.

sa_verse: सदृशं राजशार्दूल तवैव भुवि नान्यतः। महावंशप्रसूतस्य वसिष्ठव्यपदेशिनः॥
en: O tiger of a king, sprung from an illustrious line, and having Vasistha, for your guide, these words become you alone on earth and no one else.

sa_verse: यत् तु मे हृद्गतं वाक्यं तस्य कार्यस्य निश्चयम्। कुरुष्व राजशार्दूल भव सत्यव्रतिश्रवः॥
en: Do you, O tiger of a king, ascertain your course in respect of the matter I bear in my heart; and do you prove firm in promise!

sa_verse: अहं नियममातिष्ठे सिद्ध्यर्थं पुरुषर्षभ। तस्य विघ्नकरौ द्वौ तु राक्षसौ कामरूपिणौ॥
en: For celebrating a sacrifice, I, O foremost of men, abide by some prescribed course. And it comes to pass that two Rākşasas assuming shapes at will, have become bent upon disturbing the ceremony.

sa_verse: व्रते तु बहुशश्चीर्णे समाप्त्यां राक्षसाविमौ। मारीचश्च सुबाहुश्च वीर्यवन्तौ सुशिक्षितौ ॥ तौ मांसरुधिरौघेण वेदि तामभ्यवर्षताम्। अवधूते तथाभूते तस्मिन् नियमनिश्चये॥ कृतश्रमो निरुत्साहस्तस्माद् देशादपाक्रमे। न च मक्रोधमुत्स्रष्टुं बुद्धिर्भवति पार्थिव॥
en: And in that sacrifice which I have determined to bring to a completion, and which is on the eve of being completed, both these Rākşasas, Mārica and Subāhu, accomplished in arms and possessed of prowess, shower flesh and gore upon the altar. And on that ceremony being thus disturbed and by purpose thus frustrated, I considered my labours as all lost, and, therefore, have left my country in dejection.
