sa_verse: नान्यां गतिं गमिष्यामि नान्यच्छरणमस्ति मे। दैवं पुरुषकारेण निवर्तयितुमर्हसि ॥
en: Other way will I wend none; nor is there any other refuge for me. It behove you to meet Density with exertion.

sa_verse: उक्तवाक्यं तु राजानं कृपया कुशिकात्मजः। अब्रवीन्मधुरं वाक्यं साक्षाच्चण्डालतां गतम्॥
en: When the king had spoken thus, Kausika's son, moved with pity, said these sweet words to the king who had undergone Candāla-hood.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाको स्वागतं वत्स जानामि त्वां सुधार्मिकम्। शरणं ते प्रदास्यामि मा भैषीपपुङ्गव॥
en: O descendant of Ikşvāku, have you had a pleasant journey? I know you well, O highly virtuous one. Refuge will I grant you, so fear not, O best of monarchs.

sa_verse: अहमामन्त्रये सर्वान् महर्षीन् पुण्यकर्मणः। यज्ञसाह्यकरान् राजंस्ततो यक्ष्यसि निर्वृतः॥ गुरुशापकृतं रूपं यदिदं त्वयि वर्तते। अनेन सह रूपेण सशरीरो गमिष्यसि॥ हस्तप्राप्तमहं मन्ये स्वर्ग तव नराधिप। यस्त्वं कौशिकमागम्य शरण्यं शरणागतः॥
en: I shall summon all the pious Maharsis, who shall assist at the sacrifice. O king, and then you will be able to accomplish your purpose easily. And should the guise you have come by in virtue of your preceptor's curse, cling to you yet, you will bodily repair to heaven in this form. And since appearing before Kuśika's son, you have taken his refuge, I consider heaven, O lord of men, as if within they grasp.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजाः : पुत्रान् परमधार्मिकान्। व्यादिदेश महाप्राज्ञान् यज्ञसम्भारकारणात्॥
en: Having said this, that exceedingly energetic one ordered his highly virtuous and profoundly wise sons to provide the sacrificial necessaries.

sa_verse: सर्वाशिष्यान् समाहूय वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह। सर्वानृषीन् सवासिष्ठानानयध्वं ममाज्ञया॥ सशिष्यान् सुहृदश्चैव सर्विजः सुबहुश्रुतान्।
en: Summoning his disciples, he said, 'Do you by my command bring hither all the saints together with Vasistha's sons; and our friends and their disciples an the family priests variously versed in lore.'

sa_verse: यदन्यो वचनं ब्रूयान्मद्वाक्यबलचोदितः॥ तत् सर्वमखिलेनोक्तं ममाख्येयमनादृतम्।
en: Should any summoned by my mandate, say aught, 'do you fully represent to me the expression of slight.'

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा दिशो जग्मुस्तदाज्ञया॥ आजग्मुरथ देशेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यो ब्रह्मवादिनः। ते च शिष्याः समागम्य मुनि ज्वलिततेजसम्॥ ऊचुश्च ते वचनं सर्वे सर्वेषां ब्रह्मवादिनाम्।
en: Hearing his speech, they set out in different directions; and Brahmavādis* began to pour in from various countries. And the disciples (of Viśvāmitra) returning, fully communicated to that ascetic of flaming energy the words of the Brahmavadis, saying. can * A Brahmavādi is one who maintains that all things are Spirit.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा ते वचनं सर्वे समायान्ति द्विजातयः॥ सर्वदेशेषु चागच्छन् वर्जयित्वा महोदयम्।
en: Hearing your message, the regenerate ones resident in every part will come here, and some have already arrived, all save Mahodaya and the hundred sons of Vasiştha.

sa_verse: वासिष्ठं यच्छतं सर्वं क्रोधपर्याकुलाक्षरम्॥ यथाह वचनं सर्वं शृणु त्वं मुनिपुङ्गव।
en: Do you, O foremost of ascetics, listen to the words that they said with accents tremulous with emotion.

sa_verse: क्षत्रियो याजको यस्य चण्डालस्य विशेषतः॥ कथं सदसि भोक्तारो हविस्तस्य सुरर्षयः। ब्रह्मणा वा महात्मानो भुक्त्वा चाण्डालभोजनम्॥ कथं स्वर्गं गमियन्ति विश्वामित्रेण पालिताः।
en: How can celestials and saints partake of offerings in the court of him that in addition to being a Chandala, has for this priest a Kşatriya? And how high-souled Brahmanas, patronised by Visvāmitra, attain to heaven, having partaken of a Candāla's fare?

sa_verse: एतद् वचननैष्ठुर्यमूचुः संरक्तलोचनाः॥ वासिष्ठा मुनिशार्दूल सर्वे सहमहोदयाः।
en: These cruel words, O powerful ascetic, did Vasiştha's sons together with Mahodaya, utter with reddened eyes.'

sa_verse: तेषां तद् श्रुत्वा सर्वेषां मुनिपुङ्गवः॥ क्रोधसंरक्तनयनः सरोषमिदमब्रवीत्।
en: Hearing those words of theirs, that foremost of ascetics, with eyes reddened in anger, wrathfully cried.

sa_verse: यद् दूषयन्त्यदुष्टं मां तप उग्रं समास्थितम्॥ भस्मीभूता दुरात्मानो भविष्यन्ति न संशयः।
en: Since blameless , as I am, those wicked-minded ones censure me practising fierce austerities, they shall, without doubt, be reduced to ashes.

sa_verse: अद्य ते कालपाशे नीता वैवस्वतक्षयम्॥ सप्तजातिशतान्येव मृतपाः सम्भवन्तु ते। श्वमांसनियताहारा मुष्टिका नाम निघृणाः॥ वचनं विकृताश्च विरूपाश्च लोकाननुचरन्त्विमान्। महोदयश्च दुर्बुद्धिर्मामदूष्यं ह्यदूषयत्॥ दूषितः सर्वलोकेषु निषादत्वं गमिष्यति।
en: And this very day bound by the noose of Kala, meeting with destruction at the hands of Vivaśvata's son, (Yama) they shall for seven hundred firths range these worlds, wearing dead men's clothes, always feeding on dogs' flesh, going by the name of Mahodaya also has blamed me, although undeserving of blame; therefore, reproved of all, he shall undergo Nişadahood. And becoming cruel, and engaged in taking life, he shall through my ire fare wretchedly for a long lapse of time.'

sa_verse: प्राणातिपातनिरतो निरनुक्रोशतां गतः॥ दीर्घकालं मम क्रोधाद् दुर्गतिं वर्तयिष्यति। एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं विश्वामित्रो महातपाः। विरराम महातेजा ऋषिमध्ये महामुनिः॥
en: Having uttered this in the assembly of saints, that mighty ascetic, the highly powerful Viśvāmitra of fierce asceticism, paused.

sa_verse: तपोबलहताज्ञात्वा वासिष्ठान् समहोदयान्। ऋषिमध्येमहातेजा विश्वामित्रोऽभ्यभाषत॥
en: And knowing (by virtue of his Yoga power) Vasiştha's sons together with Mahodaya as destroyed in consequence of his ascetic energy, the highly powerful Viśvāmitra said in the midst of the saints.

sa_verse: अयमिक्ष्वाकुदायादस्त्रिशङ्कुरिति विश्रुतः। धर्मिष्ठश्च वदान्यश्च मां चैव शरणं गतः॥ स्वेनानेन शरीरेण देवलोकजिगीषया। यथायं स्वशरीरेण देवलोकं गमिष्यति॥ तथा प्रवर्त्यतां यज्ञो भवद्भिश्च मया सह।
en: This descendant of Ikşvāku, the famous Trišanku, is virtuous and munificent, and has taken refuge in me, with the view of attaining the celestial regions in his own person. Therefore do you engage with me in the sacrifice, so that he may bodily repair to heaven.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रवचः श्रुत्वा सर्व एव महर्षयः॥ ऊचुः समेता सहसा धर्मा धर्मसंहितम्। अयं कुशिकदायादो मुनिः परमकोपनः॥ यदाह वचनं सम्यगेतत् कार्यं न संशयः।
en: Hearing Viśvāmitra's words, the pious Maharsis readily spoke in harmony with duty, saying, “This descendant of Kuśika is a highly irascible ascetic, therefore what he said should, without doubt, he performed.'

sa_verse: अग्निकल्पो हि भगवान् शापं दास्यति रोषतः॥ तस्मात् प्रवर्त्यतां यज्ञः सशरीरो यथा दिवि। गच्छेदिक्ष्वाकुदायादो विश्वामित्रस्य तेजसा॥
en: The worshipful one is like to fire, and, if angered, may curse us. Therefore, let us engage in this sacrifice, so that Ikşvāku's descendant through the potency of Viśvāmitra way repair to heaven in person.

sa_verse: यतः प्रवर्त्यतां यज्ञः सर्वे समधितिष्ठतः। एवमुक्त्वा महर्षयः संजह्वस्ताः क्रियास्तदा॥
en: Then let us engage in this sacrifice. Saying this, sages entered the upon the ceremony.

sa_verse: याजकश्च महातेजा विश्वामित्रोऽभवत् क्रतौ। ऋत्विजश्चानुपूर्येण मन्त्रवन्मन्त्रकोविदाः॥ चक्रुः सर्वाणि कर्माणि यथाकल्पं यथाविधि।
en: And in that sacrifice the highly energetic Viśvāmitra acted as the priest. And Ritwijas versed in mantras performed every thing in order with mantras, in accordance with scripture and prescription.

sa_verse: ततः कोपसमाविष्टो विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। स्रुवमुद्यम्य सक्रोधस्त्रिशङ्कुमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon, getting into a wrath, the mighty ascetic Viśvāmitra, lifting up a garland, wrathfully exclaimed.

sa_verse: पश्य मे तपसो वीर्यं स्वार्जितस्य नरेश्वर। एष त्वां स्वशरीरेण नयामि स्वर्गमोजसा॥
en: O lord of men, do you witness the prowess of my self-earned asceticism. And, O king, do you in person repair to heaven hard to attain.

sa_verse: दुष्प्रापं स्वशरीरेण स्वर्गं गच्छ नरेश्वर। स्वार्जितं किंचिदप्यस्ति मया हि तपसा फलम्॥ राजंस्त्वं तेजसा तस्य सशरीरो दिवं व्रज।
en: Something yet remains in me of the selfearned fruit of my asceticism; and, O king, through the energy of that asceticism, do you repair to heaven in person.

sa_verse: उक्तवाक्ये मुनौ तस्मिन् सशरीरो नरेश्वरः॥ दिवं जगाम काकुत्स्थ मुनीनां पश्यतां तदा।
en: And on the ascetic saying this, O Kākutstha, that lord of men, bodily ascended heaven in the very sight of the anchorets.

sa_verse: स्वर्गलोकं गतं दृष्ट्वा त्रिशङ्ख पाकशासनः॥ सह सर्वैः सुरगणैरिदं वचनमब्रबीत्।
en: And beholding Trisanku risen to heaven, the subduer of Indra, together with all the celestial said.

sa_verse: त्रिशङ्को गच्छ भूयस्त्वं नासि स्वर्गकृतालयः॥ गुरुशापहतो मूढ पत भूमिमवाक्शिराः।
en: O Trisanku, turn you back. You have not earned abode in heaven. O fool, you have been blighted by the curse of your spiritual guide. Do you therefore drop down headlong.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो महेन्द्रेण त्रिशङ्कुरपतत् पुनः॥ विक्रोशमानस्त्राहीति विश्वामित्रं तपोधनम्।
en: The great Indra having spoken thus, Trisanku fell down, crying to the ascetic Viśvāmitra, 'Save me, save me.'

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य क्रोशमानस्य कौशिकः॥ रोषमाहारयत् तीव्र तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत्।
en: Thereupon, hearing his distressful cries, Kausika waxed mightily wroth, and exclaimed, 'Stay, stay.'

sa_verse: ऋषिमध्ये स तेजस्वी प्रजापतिरिवापरः॥ सृजन् दक्षिणमार्गस्थान् सप्तर्षीनपरान् पुनः। नक्षत्रवंशमपरमसृजत् क्रोधमूर्च्छितः॥
en: In the midst of the ascetics, like to another Prajapati, he created other seven Rsis* on the Southern way: and also, overwhelined with wrath created another set of stars. *Ursa major.

sa_verse: दक्षिणां दिशमास्थाय ऋषिमध्ये महायशाः। सृष्ट्वा सक्षत्रवंशं च क्रोधेन कलुषीकृतः॥ अन्यमिन्द्रं करिष्यामि लोको वा स्यादनिन्द्रकः। दैवतान्यपि स क्रोधात् स्रष्टुं समुपचक्रमे॥
en: And collied* with passion, that illustrious one in the midst of the ascetics created another system of stars in the southern direction. And, saying, 'I will create another Indra, or the world (that I create) shall be without an Indra.' And in anger, he went the length of creating celestials. *And passion having my best judgement collied.

sa_verse: ततः परमसम्भ्रान्ताः सर्षिसङ्घाः सुरासुराः। विश्वामित्रं महात्मानमूचुः सानुनयं वचः॥
en: Thereupon, in trepidation, the saints and the celestials and the Asuras humbly addressed the high souled Viśvāmitra thus.

sa_verse: अयं राजा महाभाग गुरुशापपरिक्षतः। सशरीरो दिवं यातु नार्हत्येव तपोधन॥
en: This king, O highly exalted one, has been visited with the curse of his preceptor, therefore, O ascetic; he deserves not to ascend heaven in person.

sa_verse: तेषां तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा देवानां मुनिपुङ्गवः। अब्रवीत् सुमहद् वाक्यं कौशिकः सर्वदेवताः।.२६ ।।
en: Hearing those words of theirs, that best of anchorets, Kausika, in company with the celestials, said these pregnant words.

sa_verse: सशरीरस्य भद्रं वस्त्रिशङ्कोरस्य भूपतेः। आरोहणं प्रतिज्ञातं नानृतं कर्तुमुत्सहे॥
en: Good betide you, I have vowed to this king, Trišanku's bodily ascension unto heaven, therefore, I dare not falsify my vow.

sa_verse: स्वर्गोऽस्तु सशरीरस्य त्रिशङ्कोरस्य शाश्वतः। नक्षत्राणि च सर्वाणि मामकानि ध्रुवाण्यथ ॥ यावल्लोका धरिष्यन्ति तिष्ठन्त्वेतानि सर्वशः। यत् कृतानि सुराः सर्वे तदनुज्ञातुमर्हथ॥
en: Let Trisanku evermore dwell in heaven in person, and let these stars created by me verily endure as long as the worlds. This it behove you, you gods, to ordain.

sa_verse: एवमुक्ताः सुराः सर्वे प्रत्यूचुर्मुनिपुङ्गवम्। एवं भवतु भद्रं ते तिष्ठन्त्वेतानि सर्वशः॥ गगने तान्यनेकानि वैश्वानरपथाद् बहिः। नक्षत्राणि मुनिश्रेष्ठ तेषु ज्योतिःषु जाज्वलन्॥ अवाक्शिरास्त्रिंशङ्कुश्च श्च तिष्ठत्वमरसंनिभः। अनुयास्यन्ति चैतानि ज्योतींषि नृपसत्तमम्॥ कृतार्थं कीर्तिमन्तं च स्वर्गलोकगतं यथा। विश्वामित्रस्तु धर्मात्मा सर्वदेवैरभिष्टुतः॥
en: Thus addressed, the deities answered that best of ascetics saying, 'So be it, good betide you! All these innumerable stars, O foremost of anchorets, shall remain in the firmament outside the path of Vaiśvānara (Zodiac); and shining in their splendour, Trisanku shall dwell with bended head, like to an immortal. And all these luminous bodies shall follow that best of kings, illustrious and successful, as if he had attained heaven itself.' And the virtuous and exceedingly energetic Viśvāmitra, thus assured by the celestials, said in the midst of the saints, 'You gods, excellent well.'

sa_verse: ऋषिमध्ये महातेजा बाढमित्येव देवताः। ततो देवा महात्मानो ऋषयश्च तपोधनाः। जग्मुर्यथागतं सर्वे यज्ञस्यान्ते नरोत्तम॥
en: Then, after the sacrifice had concluded, the high-souled celestials and the saints of ascetic wealth went to their respective regions, o foremost of men.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रो महातेजाः प्रस्थितान् वीक्ष्य तानृषीन्। अब्रवीत्ररशार्दूल सर्वांस्तान् वनवासिनः॥
en: And, O puissant one, seeing those saints gone, the highly energetic Viśvāmitra addressed those inhabitants of the forest.

sa_verse: महाविघ्नः प्रवृत्तोऽयं दक्षिणामास्थितो दिशम्। दिशमन्यां प्रपत्स्यामस्तत्र तप्स्यामहे तपः॥
en: A mighty disturbance has happened in regard to the southern quarter: let us therefore repairing to another region, carry on austerities.

sa_verse: पश्चिमायां विशालायां पुष्करेषु महात्मनः। सुखं तपश्चरिष्यामः सुखं तद्धि तपोवनम्॥
en: You high-souled ones, in the west there are extensive tracts; and there in Puşkara will we peaceably carry on our austerities. That asylum is really pleasant.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजाः पुष्करेषु महामुनिः। तप उग्रं दुराधर्षं तेपे मूलफलाशनः॥
en: Having said this, that exceedingly energetic and mighty Muni (Ascetic) began to perform terrible austerities, subsisting on fruits and roots.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु अयोध्याधिपतिर्महान्। अम्बरीष इति ख्यातो यष्टुं समुपचक्रमे॥
en: And it came to pass that at this time that mighty lord of Ayodhyā, Ambarışa, prepared for celebrating a sacrifice.

sa_verse: तस्य वै यजमानस्य पशुमिन्द्रो जहार ह। प्रनष्टे तु पशौ विप्रो राजानमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: And as he was sacrificing, Indra stole away his sacrificial beast. And on the beast being stolen, the priest said unto the king.

sa_verse: पशुरभ्याहृतो राजन् प्रणयस्तव दुर्नयात्। अरक्षितारं राजानं घ्नन्ति दोषा नरेश्वर॥
en: O king, the beast has been stolen (away); and it has been lost through your dereliction. And, O lord of men, his own fault destroy the king that fail to protect (the beast).7

sa_verse: प्रायश्चित्तं महद्येतनरं वा पुरुषर्षभ। आनयस्व पशुं शीघ्रं यावत् कर्म प्रवर्तते॥
en: O best of men, even this is the expiation: do you, while the ceremony lasts, speedily bring back the beast, or bring a man in its stead.

sa_verse: उपाध्यायवचः श्रुत्वा स राजा पुरुषर्षभः। अन्वियेष महाबुद्धिः पशुं गोभिः सहस्रशः॥
en: Hearing the priest's words, that highly intelligent king began to range various countries and provinces, cities, forests, and holy asylums, searching for the beast, with a thousand king (as the price thereof.)

sa_verse: देशाञ्जलपदांस्तांस्तान् नगराणि वनानि च। आश्रमाणि च पुण्यानि मार्गमाणो महीपतिः॥ स पुत्रसहितं तात सभार्यं रघुनन्दन। भृगुतुङ्गे समासीनमृचीकं संददर्श ह॥
en: And, O child, it came to pass that arriving at Bhrgutunga, (A mountain peak) he beheld Řcīka seated there in company with his wife and sons.

sa_verse: तमुवाच महातेजाः प्रणम्याभिप्रसाद्य च। महर्षि तपसा दीप्तं राजर्षिरमितप्रभः॥ पृष्ट्वा सर्वत्र कुशलम्चीकं तमिदं वचः। गवां शतसहस्रेण विक्रीणीषे सुतं यदि॥ पशोरर्थे महाभाग कृतकृत्योऽस्मि भार्गव।
en: O descendant of Raghu. And bowing to that Brahmarşi flaming in asceticism, and propitiating him, the exceedingly energetic royal saint of unparalleled effulgence having enquired as to his complete welfare, addressed Rcika, saying, 'O highly pious one, O Bhrgu's son, if in order that I may have a substitute for my sacrificial beast, you sell your son, my desire I shall atain.'

sa_verse: सर्वे परिगता देशा यज्ञियं न लभे पशुम्॥ दातुमर्हसि मूल्येन सुतमेकमितो मम।
en: I have ranged every country; but the beast I do not find. Therefore, it behove you to part with one of your sons for price.'

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो महातेजा ऋचीकस्त्वब्रवीद् वचः॥ नाहं ज्येष्ठं नरश्रेष्ठ विक्रीणीयां कथंचन।
en: Thus addressed the exceedingly energetic Rcīka replied, “O best of men, my first-born I will in no wise dispose of.'

sa_verse: ऋचीकस्य वचः श्रुत्वा तेषां माता महात्मनाम्॥ उवाच नरशार्दूलमम्बरीषमिदं वचः।
en: Hearing the words of the high-souled Rcīka, their mother spoke to that foremost of men, Amvarisha, saying,

sa_verse: अविक्रेयं सुतं ज्येष्ठं भगवानाह भार्गवः॥ ममापि दयितं विद्धि कनिष्ठं शुनकं प्रभो। तस्मात् कनीयसं पुत्रं न दास्ये तव पार्थिव॥
en: The worshipful son of Bhțgu has said that his first-born cannot be disposed of-do you, O lord, also know that the youngest, Sunaka, is my favourite. Therefore my youngest son will I not give to you.

sa_verse: प्रायेण हि नरश्रेष्ठ ज्येष्ठाः पितृषु वल्लभाः। मातृणां च कनीयांसस्तस्माद् रक्ष्ये कनीयसम्॥
en: O foremost of men, the eldest sons are generally the best beloved of their fathers; and the youngest, of their mother, therefore the youngest I would retain.

sa_verse: उक्तवाक्ये मुनौ तस्मिन् मुनिपन्यां तथैव च। शुन:शेपः स्वयं राम मध्यमो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: And when the ascetic as well as his wife had spoken thus, the second son, Sunahsepa, O Rāma, himself said.

sa_verse: पिता ज्येष्ठमविक्रेयं माता चाह कनीयसम्। विक्रेयं मध्यमं मन्ये राजपुत्र नयस्व माम्॥
en: My father would not sell the eldest; nor my mother the youngest,—therefore, I consider even the second as disposable. Do you then, O prince, take me.

sa_verse: अथ राजा महाबाहो वाक्यान्ते ब्रह्मवादिनः। हिरण्यस्य सुवर्णस्य कोटिभी रत्नराशिभिः॥ गवां शतसहस्रेण शुनःशेपं नरेश्वरः। गृहीत्वा परमप्रीतो जगाम रघुनन्दन॥
en: When that one versed in the Veda had ended, that lord of men, O mighty-armed descendant of Raghu, taking possession of Sunaḥsepa, by paying koțis of kine, and heaps of jewels, and gold and silver by hundreds and thousands, went away exceedingly delighted.

sa_verse: अम्बरीषस्तु राजर्षी रथमारोप्य सत्वरः। शुनःशेपं महातेजा जगामाशु महापशाः॥
en: That royal saint, the exceedingly energetic and highly famous Ambarīşa, placing Sunaḥsepa on his car, speedily set out.

sa_verse: शुनःशेपं नरश्रेष्ठ गृहीत्वा तु महायशाः। व्यश्रमत् पुष्करे राजा मध्याह्ने रघुनन्दन ॥
en: O foremost of men, taking Sunaḥsepa, that illustrious king at noon rested in Puşkara, O descendant of Raghu.

sa_verse: तस्य विश्रममाणस्य शुन:शेपो महायशाः। पुष्करं ज्येष्ठमागम्य विश्वामित्रं ददर्श ह॥ तप्यन्तमृषिभिः सार्धं मातुलं परमातुरः। विषण्णवदनो दीनस्तृष्णया च श्रमेण च ॥
en: And having arrived at the excellent Puskara, as the king was resting, the famous Sunaḥsepa with an aggrieved heart saw his maternal uncle Viśvāmitra in company with some saints engaged in asceticism. Thereupon, with a woebegone countenance, and sore afflicted with fatigue and thirst, he, O Rāma, flung himself into (Viśvāmitra's) lap, and said.

sa_verse: पपाताङ्के मुने राम वाक्यं चेदमुवाच ह। न मेऽस्ति माता न पिता ज्ञातयो बान्धवाः कुतः॥ त्रातुमर्हसि मां सौम्य धर्मेण मुनिपुङ्गव ।
en: I have neither father, nor mother, nor relatives, nor friends anywhere. It therefore behoves you, O gentle one, to save me in the interests of virtue, O foremost of ascetics.

sa_verse: त्रातां त्वं हि नरश्रेष्ठ सर्वेषां त्वं हि भावनः॥ राजा च कृतकार्यः स्यादहं दीर्घायुरव्ययः। स्वर्गलोकमुपाश्नीयां तपस्तप्त्वा ह्यनुत्तमम्॥
en: And, O best of men, you are the protector of all, and their refuge. May the king have his desire and may I at the same time, attaining long life, and undeteriorating, gain heaven, having performed meritorious austerities!

sa_verse: स मे नाथो ह्यनाथस्य भव भव्येन चेतसा। पितेव पुत्रं धर्मात्मंस्त्रातुमर्हसि किल्विषात्॥
en: Do you with a delighted heart become a lord to me that am without one. And, O righteous one, even as a father rescue a son, do you deliver me from this peril.

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा विश्वामित्रो महातपः। सान्त्वयित्वा बहुविधं पुत्रानिदमुवाच ह॥
en: Hearing his words, Viśvāmitra of mighty austerities, pacifying him by every means, spoke to this sons, saying,

sa_verse: यत्कृते पितरः पुत्राञ्जनयन्ति शुभार्थिनः। परलोकहितार्थाय तस्य कालोऽयमागतः॥
en: That in view of which fathers beget wellwishing sons, the compassing of other's welfare is at hand.

sa_verse: अयं मुनिसुतो बालो मत्तः शरणमिच्छति। अस्य जीवितमात्रेण प्रियं कुरुत पुत्रकाः॥
en: This youthful son. of the ascetic crave my protection. O you sons, do accomplish my desire by saving his life.

sa_verse: सर्वे सुकृतकर्माणः सर्वे धर्मपरायणाः। पशुभूता नरेन्द्रस्य तृप्तिमग्नेः प्रयच्छत॥
en: You are all of virtuous deeds, you are all engaged in the observance of righteousness, do you confer satisfaction upon Agni by one of you becoming the (sacrificial) beast of the lord of men.

sa_verse: नाथवांश्च शुन:शेपो यज्ञस्याविघ्नतो भवेत्। देवतास्तर्पिताश्च स्युर्मम चापि कृतं वचः॥
en: Thus Sunaḥsepa will obtain protection, the sacrifice will be freed from hindrance the deities will be propitiated, and finally my word will be made good.

sa_verse: मुनेस्तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा मधुच्छन्दादयः सुताः। साभिमानं नरश्रेष्ठ सलीलमिदमब्रुवन्॥
en: Hearing the ascetic's words, his sons, Madhucchanda and others, O foremost of me, haughtily and tauntingly answered.

sa_verse: कथमात्मसुतान् हित्वा त्रायसेऽन्यसुतं विभो। अकार्यमिव पश्यामः श्वमांसमिव भोजने॥
en: O lord, how, neglecting your own sons, you desire to deliver that of another? This we consider as heinous, even like to eating one's own flesh.

sa_verse: तेषां तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा पुत्राणां मुनिपुङ्गवः। क्रोधसंरक्तनयनो व्याहर्तुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: Hearing this speech of his sons, that best of anchorets, with eyes reddened with anger, said.

sa_verse: निःसाध्वसमिदं प्रोक्तं धर्मादपि विगर्हितम्। अतिक्रम्य तु मद्वाक्यं दारुणं रोमहर्षणम्॥ श्वमांसभोजिनः सर्वे वासिष्ठा इव जातिषु । पूर्णं वर्षसहस्रं तु पृथिव्यामनुवत्स्यथ ॥
en: Disregarding my words, you have uttered this audacious and shocking speech, disclaimed by morality, and capable of causing one's hair to stand on end. Therefore, becoming Mustikas, and living on dogs' flesh, do you all, even like Vasiştha's sons, inhabit the earth for a thousand years.

sa_verse: कृत्वा शापसमायुक्तान् पुत्रान् मुनिवरस्तदा। शुनःशेपमुवाचार्तं कृत्वा रक्षां निरामयाम्॥
en: Having cursed his sons, that best of ascetics then, by all means cheering up the distressed Śunaḥsepa as to his protection, addressed him.

sa_verse: पवित्रपाशैराबद्धो रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनः। वैष्णवं यूपमासाद्य वाग्भिरग्निमुदाहर॥ इमे च गाथे द्वे दिव्ये गायेथा मुनिपुत्रक। अम्बरीषस्य यज्ञेऽस्मिस्ततः सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि॥
en: Do you donning on a sacred zone, and wearing a garland of red flowers, and bedaubing your person with red sand paste, hymn Agni with mantras, approaching the Vaisnava sacrificial stake; and, O ascetic's son, (at the same time) chant these two verses in that sacrifice of Amvarisha. Then you will attain success.

sa_verse: शुनःशेपो गृहीत्वा ते द्वे गाथे सुसमाहितः। त्वरया राजसिंहं तमम्बरीषमुवाच ह॥
en: Thereupon, with a concentrated mind securing those two verses, Sunaḥsepa speedily presented himself before that leonine monarch saying,

sa_verse: राजसिंह महाबुद्धे शीघ्रं गच्छावहे वयम्। निवर्तयस्व राजेन्द्र दीक्षां च समुदाहर॥
en: O lion of a king, O you endued with high intelligence, let us without delay repair hence. And, O foremost of monarchs, do you engage in the sacrifice and commence upon the initiation.

sa_verse: तद् वाक्यमृषिपुत्रस्य श्रुत्वा हर्षसमन्वितः जगाम नृपतिः शीघ्रं यज्ञवाटमतन्द्रितः॥
en: Hearing those words of the ascetic's son, the king, filled with delight, readily at once repaired to the sacrificial ground.

sa_verse: सदस्यानुमते राजा पवित्रकृतलक्षणम्। पशुं रक्ताम्बरं कृत्वा यूपे तं समबन्धयत्॥
en: With the consent of his court, the king fastened Sunaħsepa with a Kuśa cord, and investing him with a crimson apparel tethered him to the stake as the (sacrificial) beast.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतः सहस्राक्षो रहस्यस्तुतितोषितः। दीर्घमायुस्तदा प्रादाच्छुनःशेपाय वासवः॥
en: Thereupon, gratified with the excellent eulogy, the thousand eyed Väsava conferred upon Sunaḥsepa long life.

sa_verse: स च राजा नरश्रेष्ठ यज्ञस्य च समाप्तवान्। फलं बहुगुणं राम सहस्राक्षप्रसादजम्॥
en: And, O foremost of men, that king also, having completed the sacrifice, obtained the manifold fruit thereof through the grace of the thousand-eyed deity,

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रोऽपि धर्मात्मा भूयस्तेपे महातपाः। पुष्करेषु नरश्रेष्ठ दशवर्षशतानि च॥
en: O Rāma, And, O best of men, the righteous Viśvāmitra of mighty asceticism again carried on austerities at Puskara for ten hundred years.

sa_verse: पूर्णे वर्षसहस्रे तु व्रतस्नातं महामुनिम्। अभ्यगच्छन् सुराः सर्वे तप:फलचिकीर्षवः॥
en: When the thousand years had been completed and the mighty ascetic had accomplished his vow, the celestials in a body desirous of conferring upon him the fruit thereof, appeared before him.

sa_verse: अब्रवीत् सुमहातेजा ब्रह्मा सुरुचिरं वचः। ऋषिस्त्वमसि भद्रं ते स्वार्चितैः कर्मभिः शुभैः॥
en: The exceedingly effulgent Brahmă addressed him in soothing words, saying, “You are henceforth a saint, good to you, and (this. eminence) you have attained through your own laudable exertions.'

sa_verse: तमेवमुक्वा देवेशस्त्रिदिवं पुनरभ्यगात्। विश्वामित्रो महातेजा भूयस्तेपे महत् तपः॥
en: Having spoken thus to him, the lord of celestials returned to heaven. And Viśvāmitra of mighty energy became again engaged in rigid austerities.

sa_verse: ततः कालेन महता मेनका परमाप्सराः। पुष्करेषु नरश्रेष्ठ स्नातुं समुपचक्रमे॥ तां ददर्श महातेजा मेनकां कुशिकात्मजः। रूपेणाप्रतिमां तत्र विद्युतं जलदे यथा ॥ कन्दर्पदर्पवशगो मुनिस्तामिदमब्रवीत्। अप्सरः स्वागतं तेऽस्तु वस चेह ममाश्रमे॥
en: And, O foremost of men, it came to pass that after a long lapse of time that prime of Apsaras, Menaka, was at that time perforining her ablutions in Puskara, and she was observed by Kusika's son like to lighting among clouds. And coming under the control of Kandarpa, (Cupid) the anchorite spoke to her, saying, 'O Apsari, has your journey been a pleasant one? Do you abide in my asylum.'

sa_verse: अनुगृह्णीष्व भद्रं ते मदनेन विमोहितम्। इत्युक्ता सा वरारोहा तत्र वासमथाकरोत् ॥
en: Do you favour me; for, good betide you, I have been rendered senseless by Madana. Thus addressed, that one of shapely hips began to dwell there.

sa_verse: तपसो हि महाविघ्नो विश्वामित्रमुपागमत्। तस्यां वसन्त्यां वर्षाणि पञ्च पञ्च च राघव॥ विश्वामित्राश्रमे सौम्ये सुखेन व्यतिचक्रमुः।
en: And mighty was the hindrance that befell Viśvāmitra as regarded his asceticism, as she, O Rāghava, staying in that asylum of his, pleasantly spent five and five years, O gentle one.

sa_verse: अथ काले गते तस्मिन् विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः॥ सव्रीड इव संवृत्तश्चिन्ताशोकपरायणः।
en: And after this period had gone by, overwhelmed with shame and afflicted with anxiety and grief, the mighty ascetic Viśvāmitra impatiently thought,

sa_verse: बुद्धिर्मुनेः समुत्पन्ना सामर्षा रघुनन्दन॥ सर्वं सुराणां कर्मैतत् तपोऽपहरणं महत्।
en: O son of Raghu, that all this mighty loss of austerities was the work of the celestials.

sa_verse: अहोरात्रापदेशेन गताः संवत्सरा दश ॥ काममोहाभिभूतस्य विघ्नोऽयं प्रत्युपस्थितः।
en: And deprived of his senses by lust, the decade had passed away imperceptibly as if it were one day and night; and this impediment stood in the way of his austerities.

sa_verse: स निःश्वसन्मुनिवरः पश्चात्तापेन दुःखितः॥ भीतामप्सरसं दृष्ट्वा वेपन्तीं प्राञ्जलिं स्थिताम्। मेनकां मधुरैर्वाक्यैर्विसृज्य कुशिकात्मजः॥ उत्तरं पर्वतं राम विश्वामित्रो जगाम ह।
en: Having a sigh, that best of ascetics burned in repentance. And with sweet words renouncing the terrified and trembling Menaka standing with clasped hands, Kusika's son, Visvamitra, O Rāma, went to the northern mountains.

sa_verse: स कृत्वा नैष्ठिकी बुद्धिं जेतुकामो महायशाः॥ कौशिकीतीरमासाद्य तपस्तेपे दुरासदम्।
en: And practising the Brahmacarya mode of life with the intention of subduing lust, that highly famous one engaged in arduous austerities on the banks of the Kausiki.

sa_verse: तस्य वर्षसहस्राणि घोरं तप उपासतः॥ उत्तरे पर्वते राम देवतानामभूद् भयम्।
en: And as he was thus engaged in profound austerities on the northern mountain, a thousand years, O Rāma passed away.

sa_verse: आमन्त्रयन् समागम्य सर्वे सर्षिगणाः सुराः॥ महर्षिशब्दं लभतां साध्वयं कुशिकात्मजः।
en: Then taking counsel together, the celestials and the saints appeared before (Brahma), saying, 'Let Kusika's son obtain the title of Maharsi.'

sa_verse: देवतानां वचः श्रुत्वा सर्वलोकपितामहः॥ अब्रवीन्मधुरं वाक्यं विश्वामित्रं तपोधनम्। महर्षे स्वागतं वत्स तपसोग्रेण तोषितः॥
en: Hearing the words of the celestials, the Grand sire of all addressed the ascetic Viśvāmitra, in these sweet words, 'O mighty saint, hast you had a pleasant journey? Satisfied with your fierce austerities, O Kausika, I confer upon you the eminence of the foremost saintship.'

sa_verse: महत्त्वमृषिमुख्यत्वं ददामि तव कौशिक। ब्रह्मणस्तु वचः श्रुत्वा विश्वामित्रस्तपोधनः॥ प्राञ्जलिः प्रणतो भूत्वा प्रत्युवाच पितामहम्। ब्रह्मर्षिशब्दमतुलं स्वार्चितैः कर्मभिः शुभैः॥ यदि मे भगवन्नाह ततोऽहं विजितेन्द्रियः।
en: Hearing Brahma's speech, the anchorite Viśvāmitra bowing down thus answered the Grandsire with clasped hands, The incomparable title of Brahmarși is to be won by one by performing sterling works. And since you have not addressed me (by that title), it appears that I have not yet succeeded in subduing my senses. *The text is very faulty. The literal meaning would be, since you have not.....I have subdued my senses, which would be absurd. I have therefore rendered the passage freely.

sa_verse: तमुवाच ततो ब्रह्मा न तावत् त्वं जितेन्द्रियः॥ यतस्व मुनिशार्दूल इत्युक्त्वा त्रिदिवं गतः।
en: Thereupon Brahmā said to him, 'Do you exert thyself until you conquer your senses?' Saying this, Brahmā went to heaven.

sa_verse: विप्रस्थितेषु देवेषु विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः॥ ऊर्ध्वबाहुनिरालम्बो वायुभक्षस्तपश्चरन्।
en: And when the celestials had gone, the mighty ascetic, Viśvāmitra, with upraised arms, and without any support, and subsisting on air, began to perform penances.

sa_verse: धर्मे पञ्चतपा भूत्वा वर्षास्वाकाशसंश्रयः॥ शिशिरे सलिलेशायी रात्र्यहानि तपोधनः। एवं वर्षसहस्रं हि तपो घोरमुपागमत्॥
en: And in summer, the ascetic surrounded himself with five fires, and in rains remained in an uncovered place, and in winter day and night stood submerged in water. Thus passed by a thousand years of terrible penances.

sa_verse: तस्मिन् संतप्यमाने तु विश्वामित्रे महामुनौ। संतापः सुमहानासीत् सुराणां वासवस्य च ॥
en: And on the mighty ascetic Viśvāmitra being engaged in austerities, great was the agitation that exercised the celestials and Indra, in particular.

sa_verse: रम्भामप्सरसं शक्रः सर्वैः सह मरुद्गणैः। उवाचात्महितं वाक्यमहितं कौशिकस्य च॥
en: Śakra together with the Maruts spoke to Rambha these words, fraught with weal to himself, and woe to Kausika.'

sa_verse: सुरकार्यमिदं रम्भे कर्तव्यं सुमहत् त्वया। लोभनं कौशिकस्येह काममोहसमन्वितम्॥
en: O Rambha, this mighty service you will have to perform in the interest of the celestials, even to take Kausika with the witchery of love.'

sa_verse: तथोक्ता साप्सरा राम सहस्राक्षेण धीमता। व्रीडिता प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं प्रत्युवाच सुरेश्वरम्॥
en: Thus addressed by the intelligent thousandeyed deity, the Apsarā, O Rāma, with clasped palms, thus bashfully answered the chief of the celestials,

sa_verse: अयं सुरपते घोरो विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। क्रोधमुत्स्रक्ष्यते घोरं मयि देव न संशयः॥
en: O lord of the celestials, this mighty ascetic, Viśvāmitra, is a terrible person; and, without doubt, he will, O divine one, waxing wroth, curse me.

sa_verse: ततो हि मे भयं देव प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि। एवमुक्तस्तया राम सभयं भीतया तदा॥ तामुवाच सहस्राक्षो वेपमानां कृताञ्जलिम्। मा भैषी रम्भे भद्रं ते कुरुष्व मम शासनम्॥
en: And O god, even this is my fear, and therefore it behove you to favour me. Thus apprehensively addressed by her in fear, the thousand-eyed one answered that damsel trembling and staying with clasped hands, 'Never fear, O Rambhā, good to you! Do you perform my bidding.'

sa_verse: कोकिलो हृदयग्राही माधवे रुचिरदुमे। अहं कन्दर्पसहितः स्थास्यामि तव पार्वतः॥
en: Assuming the form of a Cole, captivating the heart, I will in this spring crowned with graceful trees, stay by your side in company with Kandarpa.

sa_verse: त्वं हि रूपं बहुगुणं कृत्वा परमभास्वरम्। तमृषि कौशिकं भद्रे भेदयस्व तपस्विनम्॥
en: Do you adding to your beauty, diverse blandishments bewitch this ascetic, Kusika's son, O gentle one?'

sa_verse: सा श्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कृत्वा रूपमनुत्तमम्। लोभयामास ललिता विश्वामित्रं शुचिस्मिता॥
en: Hearing Indra's words, that comely damsel of luminous smiles, heightening her charms exceedingly inspired Viśvāmitra with desire.

sa_verse: कोकिलस्य तु शुश्राव वल्गु व्याहरतः स्वनम्। सम्प्रहृष्टेन मनसा स चैनामन्ववैक्षत ॥
en: He listened to the mellifluous strains of the Cole; and with a delighted heart, he beheld the fair one.

sa_verse: अथ तस्य च शब्देन गीतेनाप्रतिमेन च। दर्शनेन च रम्भाया मुनिः संदेहमागतः॥
en: Anon, listening to the warbling of the Cole and her own incomparable singing, as well as beholding Rambha, the ascetic began to entertain doubts.

sa_verse: सहस्राक्षस्य तत्सर्वं विज्ञाय मुनिपुङ्गवः। रम्भां क्रोधसमाविष्टः शशाप कुशिकात्मजः॥
en: And knowing for certain that it was the thousand-eyed deity who had devised all that, that foremost of anchorets, Kusika's son, overwhelmed with anger, cursed Rambha, saying,

sa_verse: यन्मां लोभयसे रम्भे कामक्रोधजयैषिणम्। दशवर्षसहस्राणि शैली स्थास्यसि दुर्भगे॥
en: Since, O Rambhā, you endeavour to seduce me who is bent upon subduing his anger and lust, you shall, O luckless one, remain as a stone for ten thousand years.

sa_verse: ब्रह्मणः सुमहातेजास्तपोबलसमन्वितः। उद्धरिष्यति रम्भे त्वां मत्क्रोधकलुषीकृताम्॥
en: And a highly energetic Brāhmaṇa equipped with ascetic energy, will, O Rambha, deliver you, stained because of my ire.'

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। अशक्नुवन् धारयितुं कोपं संतापमात्मनः॥
en: Thus said that exceedingly energetic and mighty ascetic Viśvāmitra, unable to contain his anger and indignation of heart.

sa_verse: तस्य शापेन महता रम्भा शैली तदाभवत्। वचः श्रुत्वा च कन्दर्पो महर्षेः स च निर्गतः॥
en: And in consequence of his mighty curse, Rambha was turned into a stone. Hearing the curse of the mighty saint, both Kandarpa and Indra left the place.

sa_verse: कोपेन च महातेजास्तपोऽपहरणे कृते। इन्द्रियैरजितै राम न लेभे शान्तिमात्मनः॥ बभूवास्य मनश्चिन्ता तपोऽपहरणे कृते। नैवं क्रोधं गमिष्यामि न च वक्ष्ये कथंचन ॥
en: O Rāma, on account of his anger, and his sense remaining still unsubdued he found no rest from deterioration of ascetic merit. And coming by decrease of ascetic merit, he thought within himself, ‘No more shall I suffer anger to exercise me, nor will I ever say anything to any.'

sa_verse: अथवा नोच्छ्वसिष्यामि संवत्सरशतान्यपि। अहं हि शोषयिष्यामि आत्मानं विजितेन्द्रियः॥
en: And I shall not breathe for an hundred years; and controlling my sense, I shall dry up my body.

sa_verse: तावद् यावद्धि मे प्राप्तं ब्राह्मण्यं तपसार्जितम्। अनुच्छ्वसन्नभुञ्जानास्तिष्ठेयं शाश्वतीः समाः॥
en: And so long as I do not attain Brāhmaṇa-hood as earned by my austerities, I shall suspending ny breath and abstaining from food, stay for a long lapse of time.

sa_verse: न हि मे तप्यमानस्य क्षयं यास्यन्ति मूर्तयः। एवं वर्षसहस्रस्य दीक्षां स मुनिपुङ्गवः। चकाराप्रतिमां लोके प्रतिज्ञां रघुनन्दन॥
en: And engaged in austerities, my form will not undergo any deterioration. That foremost of ascetics bound himself by this unparalleled vow to lead a life of such self-denial.

sa_verse: अथ हैमवतीं राम दिशं त्यक्त्वा महामुनिः। पूर्वां दिशमनुप्राप्य तपस्तेपे सुदारुणम्॥
en: Forsaking the northern direction the mighty Muni, O Rāma, betaking himself to the Eastern quarter, became engaged in dreadful austerities.

sa_verse: मौनं वर्षसहस्रस्य कृत्वा व्रतमनुत्तमम्। चकाराप्रतिमं राम तपः परमदुष्करम्॥
en: Adopting the high vow of taciturnity for a thousand years, he, O Rāma began to perform the most signal and arduous austerities.

sa_verse: पूर्णे वर्षसहस्रे तु काष्ठभूतं महामुनिम्। विघ्नैर्बहुभिराधूतं क्रोधो नान्तरमाविशत्॥
en: When the thousand years had been complete, many an impediment tried the mighty Muni staying like the trunk of a tree, yet could not anger enter his heart.

sa_verse: स कृत्वा निश्चयं राम तप आतष्ठिताव्ययम्। तस्य वर्षसहस्रस्य वर्ते पूर्णे महाव्रतः॥ भोक्तुमारब्धवानन्नं तस्मिन् काले रघूत्तम। इन्द्रो द्विजातिभूत्वा तं सिद्धमन्नमयाचत ॥
en: And firmly deter mined to shut out anger, he, O Rāma, kept his asceticism from deterioration. And, O foremost of the Raghus, when his vow of a thousand years had been observed, that one of mighty vows became desirous of feeding on boiled rice. And it came to pass ( best of the Raghus that at this time Indra assuming the guise of a regenerate one, asked for the rice.

sa_verse: तस्मै दत्त्वा तदा सिद्धं सर्वं विप्राय निश्चितः। निःशेषितेऽने भगवानभुक्त्वैव महातपाः॥
en: Thereupon he gave it away to the Vipra; and when the rice had been thus exhausted, that worshipful one of mighty austerities went without foot.

sa_verse: न किंचिदवदद् विप्रं मौनव्रतमुपास्थितः। तथैवासीत् पुनर्मोनमनुच्छ्वासं चकार ह॥
en: Nor, abiding by the vow of reticence, did he say aught to the Vipra. And he then again resumed his dumb guise, restraining his breath at the same time.

sa_verse: अथ वर्षसहस्रं च नोच्छ्वसन् मुनिपुङ्गवः। तस्यानुच्छ्वसमानस्य मूर्ध्नि धूमो व्यजायत॥
en: And that puissant ascetic did not breathe for a you sand years. And as he restrained his breath, vapours began to issue out of his head.

sa_verse: त्रैलोक्यं येन सम्भ्रान्तमातापितमिवाभवत्। ततो देवर्षिगन्धर्वाः पन्नगोरगराक्षसाः॥ मोहितास्तपसा तस्य तेजसा मन्दरश्मयः। कश्मलोपहताः सर्वे पितामहमथाब्रुवन्॥
en: And at this, the three worlds became afflicted with fear. And bewildered on account of the energy of his asceticisin, and shorn of their brightness, and afflicted with anguish, the Devarsis and the Gandharvas and the Pannagas and the Uragas and the Rakşasas in a body addressed the Pitāmaha, (Brahmā).

sa_verse: बहुभिः कारणैर्देव विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। लोभितः क्रोधितश्चैव तपसा चाभिवर्धते॥
en: O divine one, various were the means by which we endeavoured to affect the mighty Muni Viśvāmitra with covetousness and lust; but for all that he increase in asceticism.

sa_verse: नह्यस्य वृजिनं किंचिद् दृश्यते सूक्ष्ममप्युत। न दीयते यदि त्वस्य मनसा यदभीप्सितम्॥ विनाशयति त्रैलोक्यं तपसा सचराचरम्। व्याकुलाश्च दिशः सर्वा न च किंचित् प्रकाशते॥
en: Nor do we perceive in him ever so little of anger or lust. And if you do not confer upon him what his mind desire to have, he will annihilate the three worlds with all that is mobile and immobile in them. And the ten cardinal points are disconsolate: and nothing can be discovered therein.

sa_verse: सागराः क्षुभिताः सर्वे विशीर्यन्ते व पर्वताः। प्रकम्पते च वसुधा वायुर्वातीह संकुल॥
en: And the seas are vexed, and the mountains, riven. And the earth shake, and the winds keep steadily blowing.

sa_verse: ब्रह्मन् न प्रतिजानीमो नास्तिको जायते जनः। सम्मूढमिव त्रैलोक्यं सम्प्रक्षुभितमानसम्॥
en: O Brahmana, we do not know how to remedy, this. And every one is inactive like an infidel. And the three worlds look as if stupefied, with their minds exceedingly exercised.

sa_verse: भास्करो निष्प्रभश्चैव महर्षेस्तस्य तेजसा। बुद्धिं न कुरुते यावत्राशे देव महामुनिः॥ तावत् प्रसाद्यो भगवत्रग्निरूपो महाद्युतिः।
en: By virtue of that mighty saint's energy, the sun itself has been deprived of his splendour.

sa_verse: कालाग्निना यथा पूर्वं त्रैलोक्यं दह्यतेऽखिलम्॥ देवराज्यं चिकीर्षेत दीयतामस्य यन्मनः।
en: Therefore, O god, against the mighty Muni bending his mind upon destruction, and consuming the three entire worlds like to the fire raging at the universal dissolution, that exalted one of exceeding effulgence resembling a flame, should be pacified.

sa_verse: ततः सुरगणाः सर्वे पितामहपुरोगमाः॥ विश्वामित्रं महात्मानं वाक्यं मधुरमब्रुवन्।
en: Even should he desires the dominion of the celestial regions, do you grant him his wish.

sa_verse: ब्रह्मर्षे स्वागतं तेऽस्तु तपसा स्म सुतोषिताः॥ ब्राह्मण्यं तपसोग्रेण प्राप्तवानसि कौशिक।
en: Then the celestials with Pitamaha at their head, addressed the high-souled Viśvāmitra in sweet-words, saying, "Welcome, O Brahmarşi! Well pleased have we been with your penances. And, O son of Kusika, in consideration of your fiery asceticism, you have obtained Brāhmaṇahood.'

sa_verse: दीर्घमायुश्च ते ब्रह्मन् ददामि समरुद्गणः॥ स्वस्ति प्राप्नुहि भद्रं ते गच्छ सौम्य यथासुखम्। पितामहवचः श्रुत्वा सर्वेषां त्रिदिवौकसाम्॥ कृत्वा प्रणामं मुदितो व्याजहार महामुनिः।
en: O Brāhmaṇa, I will in company with the Maruts confer on you long life. hail to you! Do you accept this, good betide you. Go you, O gentle ascetic, bowing down to the celestials, said in delight,

sa_verse: ब्राह्मण्यं यदि मे प्राप्तं दीर्घमायुस्तथैव च॥ ॐकारोऽथ वषट्कारो देवाश्च वरयन्तु माम्। क्षत्रवेदविदां श्रेष्ठो ब्रह्मवेदविदामपि ॥ ब्रह्मपुत्रो वसिष्ठो मामेवं वदतु देवताः। यद्येवं परमः कामः कृतो यान्तु सुरर्षभाः॥
en: If Brāhmaṇa-hood has really been obtained by e together with length of days, let Omkâra and Vaşaļkāra and the Vedas crown me; and let, you gods, that foremost of those versed in Kștra Veda ad well as of those cognizant of the Brahmaveda, even Brahma's son, Vasistha, recognise me. Having granted this prime desire of mine, do you go away, you gods.

sa_verse: ततः प्रसादिते देवैर्वसिष्ठो जपतां वरः। सख्यं चकार ब्रह्मर्षिरेवमस्त्विति चाब्रवीत्॥
en: Then pacified by the celestials, that best of reciters, the Brahmarsi Vasistha, made friends (with Viśvāmitra), saying,

sa_verse: ब्रह्मर्षिस्त्वं न संदेहः सर्वं सम्पद्यते तव। इत्युक्त्वा देवताश्चापि सर्वा जग्मुर्यथागतम्॥
en: So be it.' You are a Brahmarsi. There is no doubt about this. And everything has been compassed in your behalf, having said this, the deities went to their respective regions.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रोऽपि धर्मात्मा लब्ध्वा ब्राह्मण्यमुत्तमम्। पूजयामास ब्रह्मर्षि वसिष्ठं जपतां वरम्॥
en: And that Brahmarși, the righteous Viśvāmitra also, having attained excellent Brāhmaṇa-hood, paid his homage to that best of reciters, Vasistha.

sa_verse: कृतकामो महीं सर्वां चचार तपसि स्थितः। एवं त्वनेन ब्राह्मण्यं प्राप्तं राम महात्मना॥
en: And, having secured his end, began to range the entire world, staying in asceticism. In this wise, O Rāma, was Brāhmanya actually obtained by the high-souled one.

sa_verse: एष राम मुनिश्रेष्ठ एष विग्रहवांस्तपः। एष धर्मः परो नित्यं वीर्यस्यैष परायणम्॥
en: This, O Rāma, is the foremost of ascetics, this one is Asceticism incarnate. This one ever abide by duty; and he is the stay of ascetic energy.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजा विरराम द्विजोत्तमः। शतानन्दवचः श्रुत्वा रामलक्ष्मणसंनिधौ॥ जनकः प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यमुवाच कुशिकात्मजम्।
en: Having said this, that best of regenerate persons paused. Hearing Satānanda's narration delivered in the presence of Rāma and Laks maņa, Janaka with clasped hands addressed the son of Kusika.

sa_verse: धन्योऽस्म्यनुगृहीतोऽस्मि यस्य मे मुनिपुङ्गव॥ यज्ञं काकुत्स्थसहितः प्राप्तवानसि कौशिक। पावितोऽहं त्वया ब्रह्मन् दर्शनेन महामुने ॥
en: Blessed and beholden am I, that you, O Kausika, accompanied with Kākutstha, hast arrived at my sacrifice, O puissant anchorite. Purified am I, O Brāhmaṇa, by your very sight.

sa_verse: अप्रमेयं तपस्तुभ्यमप्रमेयं च ते बलम्। अप्रमेया गुणाश्चैव नित्यं ते कुशिकात्मज ॥
en: Immeasurable is your asceticism; and ever immeasurable your power; and immeasurable your qualities,

sa_verse: तृप्तिराश्चर्यभूतानां कथानां नास्ति मे विभो। कर्मकालो मुनिश्रेष्ठ लम्बते रविमण्डलम्॥
en: O Kusika's son. I never, O lord, am tired of listening to that wonderful narration. Now, O foremost of ascetics, the hour for performing the daily devotions has arrived, and the solar disc hangs aslope.

sa_verse: श्वः प्रभाते महातेजो द्रष्टुमर्हसि मां पुनः। स्वागतं जपतां श्रेष्ठ मामनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥
en: To-morrow morning, O highly energetic one, you will see me again. Welcome, best of reciters. It behove you to favour me.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो मुनिवरः प्रशस्य पुरुषर्षभम्। विससर्जाशु जनकं प्रीतं प्रीतमनास्तदा॥
en: Thus addressed, that best of ascetics, extolling that powerful one, well pleased, dismissed the delighted Janaka.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा मुनिश्रेष्ठं वैदेहो मिथिलाधिपः। प्रदक्षिणां चकाराशु सोपाध्यायः सबान्धवः॥
en: Thus accosted, Mithila's lord, Vaideha, in company with his priests and friends, went round that foremost of ascetics,

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रोऽपि धर्मात्मा सहरामः सलक्ष्मणः। स्ववासमभिचक्राम पूज्यमानो महात्मभिः॥
en: And the righteous Viśvāmitra also together with Rāma and Lakşmaņa, having been honoured by the high-souled ones, took up their quarters there.

sa_verse: भगवन् स्वागतं तेऽस्तु किं करोमि तवानघ। भवानाज्ञापयतु मामाज्ञाप्यो भवता ह्यहम्॥
en: Hail, O worshipful sir! What shall I do to you, O sinless one? Do you command. Surely, I deserve to be commanded by you.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः स धर्मात्मा जनकेन महात्मना। प्रत्युवाच मुनिश्रेष्ठो वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः॥
en: Thus addressed by the high-souled Janaka, that first of ascetics endowed with a righteous soul, well versed in speech, answered,

sa_verse: पुत्रौ दशरथस्येमौ क्षत्रियौ लोकविश्रुतौ। द्रष्टुकामौ धनुःश्रेष्ठं यदेतत्त्वयि तिष्ठति ॥
en: These sons of Dasaratha-Ksatriyas, famed among men, are eager to behold than best of bows.

sa_verse: एतद् दर्शय भद्रं ते कृतकामौ नृपात्मजौ। दर्शनादस्य धनुषो यथेष्ठं प्रतियास्यतः।६।।
en: If it be with you, do you show it to them, may it be well with you! having obtained a sight of that bow, the king's sons, their desires crowned with success, will success, will return as they list.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु जनकः प्रत्युवाच महामुनिम्। श्रूयतामस्य धनुषो यदर्थमिह तिष्ठति॥
en: Thus accosted, Janaka replied to the mighty Muni, saying, Lisen to why the bow lie here.

sa_verse: देवरात इति ख्यातो निमेयेष्ठो महीपतिः। न्यासोऽयं तस्य भगवन् हस्ते दत्तो महात्मनः॥
en: There was a king known by the name of Devarata. He was the elder brother of Nimi. And, Oworshipful one, this bow was consigned to the hands of that high-souled one, this bow was consigned to the hands of that high-souled one as a trust.

sa_verse: ततो विमनसः सर्वे देवा वै मुनिपुङ्गव। प्रसादयन्त देवेशं तेषां प्रीतोऽभवद् भवः॥
en: Thereat, O powerful ascetic, with agitated hearts, the deities fell to propitiating that lord of the celestials, and Bhava was pleased with them.

sa_verse: प्रीतियुक्तस्तु सर्वेषां ददौ तेषां महात्मनाम्। तदेतद् देवदेवस्य धनूरत्नं महात्मनः॥ न्यासभूतं तदा न्यस्तमस्माकं पूर्वजे विभौ।
en: And well-pleased with them, he conferred this upon those high-souled ones. And even this is that jewel of a bow belonging to the high-souled god of gods, and which was ultimately, O lord, consigned as a trust to our ancestor.

sa_verse: अथ मे कृषतः क्षेत्र लाङ्गलादुत्थिता ततः॥ क्षेत्रं शोधयता लब्धा नाम्ना सीतेति विश्रुता। भूतलादुत्थितां सा तु व्यवर्धत ममात्मजा ॥
en: And as I was ploughing the mead, arose a damsel, and as I obtained her while hallowing the field (for sacrifice), she has come to be known by the name of Sītā. And arising from the earth, she grew as my daughter.

sa_verse: वीर्यशुल्केति मे कन्या स्थापितेयमयोनिजा। भूतलादुत्थितां तां तु वर्धमानां ममात्मजाम्॥ वरयामासुरागत्य राजानो मुनिपुङ्गव। तेषां वरयतां कन्यां सर्वेषां पृथिवीक्षिताम्॥ वीर्यशुल्केति भगवन् न ददामि सुतामहम्। ततः सर्वे नृपतयः समेत्य मुनिपुङ्गव॥ मिथिलामप्युपागम्य वीर्यं जिज्ञासवस्तदा।
en: Unsprung from the usual source, she was then established here as my daughter, whose hand must be obtained by bending the bow. And, O foremost of ascetics, many a king, coming here, had saught to obtain my growing daughter arisen from the earth. But, O worshipful one, in consideration of her being one whose dower must be prowess in bending the bow, I would not bestow my daughter upon those lords of earth seeking for the kings in a body began to flock to Mithila, desirous of being acquainted with the strength of the bow.

sa_verse: तेषां जिज्ञासमानानां शैवं धनुरूपाहृतम्॥ न शेकुर्ग्रहणे तस्य धनुषस्तोलनेऽपि वा।
en: And on their being curious (as to the bow), I showed it to them; but they could neither hold nor wield it.

sa_verse: तेषां वीर्यवतां वीर्यमल्पं ज्ञात्वा महामुने॥ प्रत्याख्याता नृपतयस्तन्निबोध तपोधन । ततः परमकोपेन राजानो मुनिपुङ्गव॥ अरुन्धन् मिथिलां सर्वे वीर्यसंदेहमागताः। आत्मानमवधूतं मे विज्ञाय नृपपुङ्गवाः॥ रोषेण महताविष्टाः पीडयन् मिथिलां पुरीम्।
en: And, O mighty Muni, finding those powerful kings to be but endowed with small prowess, I passed them by. Hear what then befell, O you of ascetic wealth. Then, O powerful anchorite, in high ire, the kings, doubtful as to their strength in stringing the bow, laid siege to Mithila. And those potent princes, conceiving themselves as frustrated by me, began to harass the city of Mithila, waxing wondrous wroth.

sa_verse: ततः संवत्सरे पूर्णे क्षयं यातानि सर्वशः॥ साधनानि मुनिश्रेष्ठ ततोऽहं भृशदुःखितः।
en: And when a year had been completed, my defensive resources were entirely exhausted, and at this, I was exceedingly aggrieved.

sa_verse: ततो देवगणान् सर्वांस्तपसाहं प्रसादयम्॥ ददुश्च परमप्रीताश्चतुरङ्गबलं सुराः।
en: Then I sought to propitiate the deities; and well-pleased, the celestials granted me a Caturanga army.

sa_verse: ततो भग्ना नृपतयो हन्यमाना दिशो ययुः॥ अवीर्या वीर्यसंदिग्धाः सामात्याः पापकारिणः।
en: At length those wicked kings, meeting with slaughter, broke and fled in all directions, together with their adherents, bereft of vigour, and confidence in their strength.

sa_verse: तदेतन्मुनिशार्दूल धनुः परमभास्वरम्॥ रामलक्ष्मणयोश्चापि दर्शयिष्यामि सुव्रत।
en: And, O puissant ascetic, this highly effulgent bow will I show to Rāma and Laksmana.

sa_verse: यद्यस्य धनुषो रामः कुर्यादारोपणं मुने। सुतामयोनिजां सीतां दद्यां दाशरथेरहम्॥
en: O you of excellent vows. And, if, Ascetic, Rāma succeeds in fixing string to it, I will confer upon Daſaratha's son my daughter unsprung from the usual source. Sītā,

sa_verse: जनकस्य वचः श्रुत्वा विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। धनुर्दर्शय रामाय इति होवाच पार्थिवम्॥
en: Hearing Janaka's words, the mighty Muni Viśvāmitra said to the king, Do you show the bow to Rāma.

sa_verse: ततः स राजा जनकः सचिवान् व्यादिदेश ह
धनुरानीयतां दिव्यं गन्धमाल्यानुलेपितम्॥
en: Thereupon the king Janaka commanded his ministers, saying, Do you bring the wonderful bow furnished with unguents and garlands.

sa_verse: जनकेन समादिष्टाः सचिवाः प्राविशन् पुरम्। तद्धनुः पुरतः कृत्वा निर्जग्मुरमितौजसः॥
en: Commanded by Janaka, the ministers entered the city; and placing the bow in their front, those, endowed with immeasurable energy, came out.

sa_verse: नृणां शतानि पञ्चाशद् व्यायतानां महात्मनाम्। मञ्जूषामष्टचक्रां तां समूहुस्ते कथंचन॥
en: And deposited in a case on a cart borne upon eight wheels, it was with difficulty drawn along by five thousand stalwart persons of welldeveloped frames.

sa_verse: तामादाय सुमञ्जूषामायसीं यत्र तद्धनुः। सुरोपमं ते जनकमूचुर्नृपतिमन्त्रिणः॥
en: And having brought that bow plaited with iron, placed in its case, the royal counsellors spoke to Janaka resembling an immortal, saying,

sa_verse: इदं धनुर्वरं राजन् पूजितं सर्वराजभिः। मिथिलाधिप राजेन्द्र दर्शनीयं यदीच्छसि॥
en: Here is the best of bow, O king, worshipped of all sovereigns. O foremost of kings, O lord of Mithilā, if it please you, behold the same.

sa_verse: तेषां नृपो वचः श्रुत्वा कृताञ्जलिरभाषत। विश्वामित्रं महात्मानं ताबुभौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Hearing their speech, the king with ciasped palms said to the high-souled Viśvāmitra as well as Rāma and Laksmana,

sa_verse: इदं धनुर्वरं ब्रह्मञ्जनकैरभिपूजितम्। राजभिश्च महावीरशक्तैः पूरितं तदा॥
en: This best of bows, O Brāhmaṇa, has always been worshipped by the Janakas: as also by mighty kings incapable of wielding and stringing it.)

sa_verse: नैतत् सुरगणाः सर्वे सासुरा न च राक्षसाः। गन्धर्वयक्षप्रवराः सकिन्नरमहोरगाः॥ क्व गतिर्मानुषाणां च धनुषोऽस्य प्रपूरणे। आरोपणे समायोगे वेपने तोलने तथा॥
en: And neither the celestials, nor the Asuras, nor the Rākşasas, nor the Gandharvas, nor the Yakş as, nor the Kinnaras, nor the mighty Uragas, how shall men fare have succeeded in stringing or stretching it, or fixing the arrow to it, or pulling its string, or wielding it.

sa_verse: तदेतद् धनुषां श्रेष्ठमानीतं मुनिपुङ्गव। दर्शयैतन्महाभाग अनयो राजपुत्रयोः॥
en: This foremost of bows has been brought here, O chief of ascetics. Do you, O exalted one, show it to these sons of the king.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रः सरामस्तु श्रुत्वा जनकभाषितम्। वत्स राम धनुः पश्य इति राघवमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing Janaka's words, the righteous Viśvāmitra said to Rāghava, O Rāma, do you, my child, behold the bow.

sa_verse: महर्षेर्वचनाद् रामो यत्र तिष्ठति तद्धनुः। मञ्जूषां तामपावृत्य दृष्ट्वा धनुरथाब्रवीत्॥
en: At the words of the Maharsi, Rama, opening the case, took a sight of the bow, and said,

sa_verse: इदं धनुर्वरं दिव्यं संस्पृशामीह पाणिना। यत्नवांश्च भविष्यामि तोलने पूरणेऽपि वा॥
en: This divine bow will I touch with my hand, and shall I also strive to wield and draw it?

sa_verse: बादमित्यब्रवीद् र.ना मुनिश्च समभाषत। लीलया स धनुर्मध्ये जग्राह वचनान्मुनेः॥ पश्यतां नृसहस्राणां बहूनां रघुनन्दनः। आरोपयत् स धर्मात्मा सलीलमिव तद्धनुः॥ आरोपयित्वा मौर्वी च पूरयामास तद्धनुः। तद् बभञ्ज धनुर्मध्ये नरश्रेष्ठो महायशाः॥ तस्य शब्दो महानासीनिर्घातसमनिःस्वनः। भूमिकम्पश्च सुमहान् पर्वतस्येव दीर्यतः॥ निपेतुश्च नराः सर्वे तेन शब्देन मोहिताः। वर्जयित्वा मुनिवरं राजानं तौ च राघवौ॥
en: Thereat both the king and the ascetic said, Excellent well. At the words of the anchorite, in the sight of countless thousands of spectators, the righteous son of Raghu with exceeding ease took hold of the bow by the middle, and fixed the string upon it, and having fixed the string, drew it. And that foremost of men enjoying high fame, snapped the bow in the middle. And mighty was the sound that was heard on the occasion, like to the bursting of a thunder-clap: and the earth trembled terribly, as it does in the vicinity of a mountain splitting; and overwhelmed by the noise, all rolled head over heels, with the exception of that best of ascetics, the king, and the two Rāghavas.

sa_verse: प्रत्याश्वस्ते जने तस्मिन् राजा विगतसाध्वसः। उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं वाक्यज्ञो मुनिपुङ्गवम्॥
en: On the people being reassured, the king conversant with speech, his apprehension removed, with folded hands addressed that puissant ascetic, saying.

sa_verse: भगवन् दृष्टवीर्यो मे रामो दशरथात्मजः। अत्यद्भुतमचिन्त्यं च अतर्कितमिदं मया॥
en: O worshipful one, I have beheld the prowess of Dasaratha's son. This is verily wonderful and inconceivable; and I did not think this was possible.

sa_verse: जनकानां कुले कीर्तिमाहरिष्यति मे सुता। सीता भर्तारमासाद्य रामं दशरथात्मजम्॥
en: My daughter, Sītā, being united with her lord, Dasaratha's son, Rama, will shed lustre on Janaka's line.

sa_verse: मम सत्या प्रतिज्ञा सा वीर्यशुल्केति कौशिक। सीता प्राणैर्बहुमता देया रामाय मे सुता ॥
en: My promise viz., that I will confer Sītā upon him that will bend the bow, has been fulfilled, O son of Kusika. And this Sītā, this my daughter, dearer to me than life will I confer upon Rāma.

sa_verse: भवतोऽनुमते ब्रह्मशीघ्रं गच्छन्तु मन्त्रिणः। मम कौशिक भद्रं ते अयोध्यां त्वरिता रथैः. :.२४॥ राजानं प्रश्रितैर्वाक्यैरानयन्तु पुरं मम। प्रदानं वीर्यशुल्कायाः कथयन्तु च सर्वशः॥
en: O Brāhmaṇa, by your permission let my counsellors speedily post hence, O Kausika, to Ayodhyā, in cars; and with humble speech bring the king to my capital. And let them communicate to him all about the bestowal of Sītå upon Răma, in consequence of his having bent the bow.

sa_verse: मुनिगुप्तौ च काकुत्स्थौ कथयन्तु नृपाय वै। प्रीतियुक्तं तु राजानमानयन्तु सुशीघ्रगाः॥
en: Let them acquaint the monarch with the welfare of the Kākutsthas protected by the ascetic; and let them, speedily posting hence, bring the delighted king.

sa_verse: कौशिकस्तु तथेत्याह राजा चाभाष्य मन्त्रिणः। अयोध्यां प्रेषयामास धर्मात्मा कृतशासनान्। यतावृत्तं समाख्यातुमानेतुं च नृपं तथा॥
en: And thereupon Kusika's son said,-So be it. And the righteous king, summoning his counsellors, despatched them to Ayodhyā with his letter, to communicate all duly to the king, and bring him thither.

sa_verse: जनकेन समादिष्टा दूतास्ते क्लान्तवाहनाः। त्रिरात्रमुषिता मार्गे तेऽयोध्यां प्राविशन्पुरीम्॥
en: Thus commissioned by Janaka, the envoys, having spent three nights on the way, entered the city of Ayodhyā, with their conveyances afflicted with fatigue.

sa_verse: ते राजवचनाद् गत्वा राजवेश्म प्रवेशिताः। ददृशुर्देवसंकाशं वृद्धं दशरथं नृपम्॥
en: In accordance with the royal commission, entering the king's residence, they saw the aged king Dasaratha, resembling a celestial.

sa_verse: बद्धाञ्जलिपुटाः सर्वे दूता विगतसाध्वसाः। राजानं प्रश्रितं वाक्यमब्रुवन् मधुराक्षरम्॥ मैथिलो जनको राजा साग्निहोत्रपुरस्कृतः। मुहुर्मुहुर्मधुरया स्नेहसंरक्तया गिरा॥ कुशलं चाव्ययं चैव सोपाध्यायपुरोहितम्। जनकस्त्वां महाराज पृच्छते सपुरःसरम्॥
en: Freed from apprehension the envoys with clasped hands addressed the monarch in sweetly humble accents, saying, O mighty monarch, Mithilā's lord, king Janaka, in company with this priests, in sweet and affectionate words, repeatedly enquires after the complete welfare of thyself along with your priests and servants.

sa_verse: पृष्ट्वा कुशलमव्यग्रं वैदेही मिथिलाधिपः। कौशिकानुमते वाक्यं भवन्तमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: After having enquired after your complete welfare, Mithila's lord, Vaideha, by permission of Kausika, addresses you thus,

sa_verse: पूर्वं प्रतिज्ञा विदिता वीर्यशुल्का ममात्मजा। राजानश्च कृतामर्षा निर्वीर्या विमुखीकृताः॥
en: You know the vow I had made formerlyviz., to confer my daughter upon him that would bend the bow,-and the kings, in consequence of their having been deprived of prowess, and being baffled, have come to entertain spite against me.

sa_verse: सेयं मम सुता राजन् विश्वामित्रपुरस्कृतैः। यदृच्छयागतै राजन् निर्जिता तव पुत्रकैः॥ तच्च रत्नं धनुर्दिव्यं मध्ये भग्नं महात्मना। रामेण हि महाबाहो महत्यां जनसंसदि॥
en: And that daughter of mine, O king, has been won by your son, arrived one, that divine, jewelled bow has been snapped in the middle by the high-souled Rāma in the midst of a large assembly.

sa_verse: अस्मै देया मया सीता वीर्यशुल्का महात्मने। प्रतिज्ञां तर्तुमिच्छामि तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥
en: Upon that high-souled one should I confer Sītā, having prowess assigned for her dower. And in this wise will I free myself from my vow; and this you should permit. 10

sa_verse: सोपाध्यायो महाराज पुरोहितपुरस्कृतः। शीघ्रमागच्छ भद्रं ते द्रष्टुमर्हसि राघवौ॥
en: O mighty king, do you, good betide you, come speedily headed by your priests. It behove you to see the Rāghavas;

sa_verse: प्रतिज्ञां मम राजेन्द्र निर्वर्तयितुमर्हसि। पुत्रयोरुभयोरेव प्रीतिं त्वमुपलप्स्यसे॥
en: And, O foremost of kings, to see me delivered from this vow. And do you attain the joy incident to the nuptials of both your sons,

sa_verse: एवं विदेहाधिपतिर्मधुरं वाक्यमब्रवीत्। विश्वामित्राभ्यनुज्ञातः शतानन्दमते स्थितः॥
en: Thus spoke sweetly the lord of Videha, permitted by Viśvāmitra and staying by the opinions of Satānanda.'

sa_verse: दूतवाक्यं तु तच्छ्रुत्वा राजा परमहर्षितः। वसिष्ठं वामदेवं च मन्त्रिणश्चैवमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing the words of the envoys, the king, exceedingly rejoiced, addressed Vasiştha and Vamadeva, as well as his counsellors, saying,

sa_verse: गुप्तः कुशिकपुत्रेण कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा विदेहेषु वसत्यसौ॥
en: Protected by Kusika's son, that exhancer of Kausalyā's joy stay in Videha in company with his brother, Laksmana.15

sa_verse: दृष्टवीर्यस्तु काकुत्स्थो जनकेन महात्मना। सम्प्रदानं सुतायास्तु राघवे कर्तुमिच्छति॥
en: And the high-souled Janaka has witnessed the prowess of Kākutstha; and he wish to bestow his daughter upon Rāghava.

sa_verse: यदि वो रोचते वृत्तं जनकस्य महात्मनः। पुरीं गच्छामहे शीघ्रं मा भूत् कालस्य पर्ययः॥
en: If this alliance with the high-souled Janaka is relished by you, we shall speedily repair to his capital. Let there be no waste of time.

sa_verse: मन्त्रिणो बाढमित्याहुः सह सर्वैर्महर्षिभिः। सुप्रीतश्चाब्रवीद् राजा श्वो यात्रेति च मन्त्रिणः॥
en: Thereupon, the counsellors along with the Maharsis said, Excellent! And the king highly delighted, said to the counsellors, Our journey commence on the morrow.

sa_verse: त्रिणस्तु नरेन्द्रस्य रात्रिं परमसत्कृताः। ऊषुः प्रमुदिताः सर्वे गुणैः सर्वैः समन्विताः॥
en: And excellently ministered to, the counsellors of that foremost of monarchs (Janaka), endowed with every excellent quality, spent that night in joy.

sa_verse: ततो रात्र्यां व्यतीतायां सोपाध्यायः सबान्धवः। राजा दशरथो हृष्टः सुमन्त्रमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then when the night had been spent, king Dasaratha accompanied with his priests and adherents, well pleased spoke to Sumantra, saying,

sa_verse: अद्य सर्वे धनाध्यक्षा धनमादाय पुष्कलम्। व्रजन्त्वग्रे सुविहिता नानारत्नसमन्विताः॥
en: To-day let the officers in charge of the treasury, taking plenty of excellent wealth, and furnished with various gems, go in advance under proper escort.

sa_verse: चतुरङ्गबलं चापि शीघ्रं निर्यातु सर्वशः। ममाज्ञासमकालं च यानं युग्यमनुत्तमम्॥
en: And let the fore-fold forces sally out with speed. And at my command let horses and conveyances and elegant vehicles march out

sa_verse: वसिष्ठो वामदेवश्च जाबालिरथ कश्यपः। मार्कण्डेयस्तु दीर्घायुर्ऋषिः कात्यायनस्तथा॥ एते द्विजाः प्रयान्त्वने स्यन्दनं योजयस्व मे। यथा कालात्ययो न स्याद् दूता हि त्वरयन्ति माम्।।५।
en: And let Vasiştha and Vamadeva and Jābāli and Kasyapa and Mārkandeya endowed with long life and the saint Katyāyana-let these regenerate ones go forward. And do you also yoke my car. Let not the proper time pass away; for the envoys urge speed upon me.

sa_verse: वचनाच्च नरेन्द्रस्य सेना च चतुरङ्गिणी। राजानमृषिभिः सार्धं व्रजन्तं पृष्ठतोऽन्वयात्॥
en: At these words of the king, the four-fold forces together with the saints went in the wake of the monarch.

sa_verse: गत्वा चतुरहं मार्ग विदेहानभ्युपेयिवान्। राजा च जनकः श्रीमाऽश्रुत्वा पूजामकल्पयत्॥ ततो राजानमासाद्य वृद्धं दशरथं नृपम्। मुदितो जनको राजा प्रहर्षं परमं ययौ॥
en: After having passed four days on the way, they arrived at Videha. And bearing of Dasaratha's arrival, the auspicious king Janaka experienced great delight, and having obtained the aged king Dasaratha, he honoured him duly.

sa_verse: उवाच वचनं श्रेष्ठो नरश्रेष्ठं मुदान्वितम्। स्वागतं ते नरश्रेष्ठ दिष्ट्या प्राप्तोऽसि राघव॥
en: That chief of men well pleased, spoke these words, Have your journey been a pleasant one, O best of men? By luck have I obtained you, O descendant of Raghu.

sa_verse: पुत्रयोरुभयोः प्रीतिं लप्स्यसे वीर्यनिर्जिताम्। दिष्ट्या प्राप्तो महातेजा वसिष्ठो भगवानृषिः॥ सह सर्वैर्द्विजश्रेष्ठैर्देवैरिव शतक्रतुः।
en: Do you experience the joy earned by the prowess of your sons. And by luck it is that I have obtained the highly energetic and worshipful saint Vasistha accompanied by the foremost regenerate ones, like him of an hundred sacrifices, by the celestials.

sa_verse: दिष्ट्या मे निर्जिता विघ्ना दिष्ट्या मे पूजितं कुलम्।।११ राघवैः सह सम्बन्धाद् वीर्यश्रेष्ठर्महाबलैः।
en: By luck it is that I have overcome the obstacle; by luck it is that my race has attained renown, in consequence of alliance with those endowed with prowess, the exceedingly potent Rāghavas.

sa_verse: श्वः प्रभाते नरेन्द्र त्वं संवर्तयितुमर्हसि ॥ यज्ञस्यान्ते नरश्रेष्ठ विवाहमृषिसत्तमैः।
en: O lord of men, to-morrow morning after the completion of the sacrifice, do you perform the nuptials, in company with the foremost of the saints.

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा ऋषिमध्ये नराधिपः॥ वाक्यं वाक्यविदां श्रेष्ठः प्रत्युवाच महीपतिम्।
en: Hearing his speech in the midst of the saints, that best of those conversant with words, the lord of men, replied to the monarch, saying,

sa_verse: प्रतिग्रहो दातृवशः श्रुतमेतन्मया पुरा॥ यथा वक्ष्यसि धर्मज्ञ तत् करिष्यामहे वयम्।
en: A gift should be accepted, this I heard formerly. And what you say, O you cognizant of duty, will we accomplish.

sa_verse: तद् धर्मिष्ठं यशस्यं च वचनं सत्यवादिनः॥ श्रुत्वा विदेहाधिपतिः परं विस्मयमागतः।
en: Hearing these words of the truthful (king), chiming in with morality and conducive to fame, the lord of Videha was filled with admiration.

sa_verse: ततः सर्वे मुनिगणाः परस्परसमागमे॥ हर्षेण महता युक्तास्तां रात्रिमवसन् सुखम्।
en: Then the ascetics experiencing great delight, passed the night in each other's company.

sa_verse: राजा च राघवौ पुत्रौ निशाम्य परिहर्षितः॥ उवास परमप्रीतो जनकेनाभिपूजितः।
en: And the king, overjoyed on beholding his sons, the Rāghavas passed (the night) in exceeding delight, splendidly entertained of Janaka.

sa_verse: जनकोऽपि महातेजाः क्रिया धर्मेण तत्त्ववित्। यज्ञस्य च सुताभ्यां च कृत्वा रात्रिमुवास ह॥
en: And the exceedingly energetic Janaka also, versed in men and things, having in accordance with the ordinance completed the sacrifice and performed all the preliminary rites relative to the nuptials of his daughters, reposed for the night.

sa_verse: भ्राता मम महातेजा वीर्यवानतिधार्मिकः। कुशध्वज इति ख्यातः पुरीमध्यवसच्छुभाम्॥ वार्याफलकपर्यन्तां पिबन्निक्षुमती नदीम्। सांकाश्यां पुण्यसंकाशां विमानमिव पुष्पकम्॥
en: My highly energetic, puissant and eminently righteous brother known by the name of Kusadhvaja dwell in the auspicious city, Sāņkāśyā, whose ramparts are ranged round with pointed weapons, and which is laved by the river Ikşumatī, and which resembles the celestial regions or the aerial car, Pușpaka.

sa_verse: तमहं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि यज्ञगोप्ता स मे मतः। प्रीतिं सोऽपि महातेजा इमां भोक्ता मया सह ॥
en: I wish to see him, and he is in charge of my sacrifice. And that highly energetic one will partake with me the joy of this occasion.

sa_verse: एवमुक्ते तु वचने शतानन्दस्य संनिधौ। आगताः केचिदव्यग्रा जनकस्तान् समादिशत्॥
en: This having been said to Satānanda, some competent persons presented themselves; and Janaka commanded them (to set up) for Sāņkāśyā.

sa_verse: शासनात् तु नरेन्द्रस्य प्रययुः शीघ्रवाजिभिः। समानेतुं नरव्याघ्रं विष्णुमिन्द्राज्ञया यथा॥
en: And commanded by the monarch, off they want, mounting on fleet coursers, with the view of bringing over that tiger among men, like Viş nu at the mandate of Indra.

sa_verse: सांकाश्यां ते समागम्य ददृशुश्च कुशध्वजम्। न्यवेदयन् यथावृत्तं जनकस्य च चिन्तितम्।७।।
en: And arriving at Sāņkāśyā, they presented themselves before Kušadhvaja, and faithfully delivered to him the intention of Janaka.

sa_verse: तद्वृत्तं नृपतिः श्रुत्वा दूतश्रेष्ठैर्महाजवैः। आज्ञया तु नरेन्द्रस्य आजगाम कुशध्वजः॥
en: And hearing the tidings conveyed by those foremost of envoys endowed with great fleetness, Kusadhvaja set out at the mandate of the monarch.

sa_verse: स ददर्श महात्मानं जनकं धर्मवत्सलम्। सोऽभिवाद्य शतानन्दं जनकं चातिधार्मिकम्॥ राजाहं परमं दिव्यमासनं सोऽध्यरोहत।
en: And on coming to Videha, he appeared before the high-souled Janaka addicted to righteousness. And saluting Satānanda as well as the eminently virtuous Janaka, he sat down on an excellent and superb seat worthy of a King.

sa_verse: उपविष्टाबुभौ तौ तु भ्रातरावमितद्युती॥ प्रेषयामासतुर्वीरौ मन्त्रि श्रेष्ठं सुदामनम्। गच्छ मन्त्रिपते शीघ्रमिक्ष्वाकुममितप्रभम्॥ आत्मजैः सह दुर्धर्षमानयस्व समन्त्रिणम्।
en: Having been seated, both the heroic brothers of immeasurable splendour addressed that foremost of counsellors, Sudáman, saying, 'Go, foremost of counsellors, and speedily bring over the irrepressible Ikşvāku of immeasurable splendour along with his sons and ministers.

sa_verse: औपकार्यां स गत्वा तु रघूणां कुलवर्धनम् ॥ ददर्श शिरसा चैनमभिवाद्येदमब्रवीत्।
en: Thereupon, repairing to the camp of that enhancer of the race of the Raghus, he saw Dasaratha, and saluting him with beaded head, addressed him.

sa_verse: अयोध्याधिपते वीर वैदेही मिथिलाधिपः॥ स त्वां द्रष्टुं व्यवसितः सोपाध्यायपुरोहितम्।
en: O heroic lord of Ayodhyā, Vaideha, the master of Mithila, has wished to see you along with your priests.

sa_verse: मन्त्रि श्रेष्ठवचः श्रुत्वा राजा सर्षिगणस्तथा॥ सबन्धुरगमत् तत्र जनको यत्र वर्तते।
en: Hearing the words of that best of counsellors, the king accompanied by the saints and his adherents, came to Janaka.

sa_verse: राजा च मन्त्रिसहितः सोपाध्यायः सबान्धवः॥ वाक्यं वाक्यविदां श्रेष्ठो वैदेहमिदमब्रवीत्।
en: And in company with his counsellors with his counsellors and priests and adherents, the king, foremost of those skilled in speech, spoke to Vaideha.

sa_verse: विदितं ते महाराज इक्ष्वाकुकुलदैवतम्॥ वक्ता सर्वेषु कृत्येषु वसिष्ठो भगवानृषिः।
en: O mighty king, you know that the worshipful saint Vasiştha is the spiritual guide of our race; and in every ceremony that we undertake, he it is who serves the function of a spokesman.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्राभ्यनुज्ञातः सह सर्वैर्महर्षिभिः॥ एष वक्ष्यति धर्मात्मा वसिष्ठो में यथाक्रमम्।
en: And permitted by Viśvāmitra along with all the Maharsis, even this one of a righteous soul will relate my genealogy.

sa_verse: तूष्णीभूते दशरथे वसिष्ठो भगवानृपिः॥ उवाच वाक्यं वाक्यज्ञो वैदेहं सपुरोधसम्।
en: And on Dasaratha resuming silence, the worshipful saint Vasistha, versed in speech, spoke to Vaideha in company with his priests, saying,

sa_verse: अव्यक्तप्रभवो ब्रह्मा शाश्वतो नित्य अव्ययः॥ तस्मान्मरीचिः संजज्ञे मरीचे: कश्यपः सुतः। विवस्वान् कश्यपाज्जज्ञे मनुर्वैवस्वतः स्मृतः॥
en: The perpetual, everlasting, and undeteriorating Brahmā sprang from the Unmanifest (Brahmā). From him sprang Marīci; and Kasyapa is son to Marīci. And from Kaśyapa sprung Vivasvat; and Manu is son to Vivasvat.

sa_verse: मनुः प्रजापतिः पूर्वमिक्ष्वाकुश्च मनोः सुतः। तमिक्ष्वाकुमयोध्यायां राजानं विद्धि पूर्वकम्॥
en: This Manu is otherwise called Prajäpati; and Ikşvāku is Manu's son. And this Ikşvāku you must understand, was the first king of Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकोस्तु सुतः श्रीमान् कुक्षिरित्येव विश्रुतः। कुक्षेरथात्मजः श्रीमान् विकुक्षिरुदपद्यत॥
en: And Ikşvāku's son, it is well known, was the graceful Kuksi. And Kuksi's son was the graceful Vikuksi.

sa_verse: विकुक्षेस्तु महातेजा बाणः पुत्रः प्रतापवान्। बाणस्य तु महातेजा अनरण्यः प्रतापवान् ॥
en: And Vikuksi's son was the exceedingly energetic and powerful Bāņa. And Bāna's son was the highly energetic and powerful Anaranya.

sa_verse: अनरण्यात् पृथुर्जज्ञे त्रिशङ्कुस्तु पृथोरपि। त्रिशङ्कोरभवत् पुत्रो धुन्धुमारो महायशाः॥
en: From Anaranya sprang Prithu; and from Prithu, Trisanku. And Trisanku's son was the highly famous Dhundhumāra.

sa_verse: धुन्धुमारान्महातेजा युवनाश्वो महारथः। युवनाश्वसुतश्चासीन्मान्धाता पृथिवीपतिः॥
en: And from Dhundhumāra sprung the Mahāratha, Yuvanāśva. And from Yuvanāśva sprung Māndhātā, lord of earth.

sa_verse: मान्धातुस्तु सुतः श्रीमान् सुसंधिरुदपद्यत। सुसंधेरपि पुत्रौ द्वौ ध्रुवसंधिः प्रसेनजित्॥
en: And Māndhātā's son was the graceful Susandhi. And Susandhi's two sons were Dhruvasandhi and Prasenajit.

sa_verse: यशस्वी ध्रुवसंधेस्तु भरतो नाम नामतः। भरतात् तु महातेजा असितो नाम जायत॥ यस्यैते प्रतिराजानं उदपद्यन्त शत्रवः। हैहयास्तालजङ्घाश्च शूराश्च शशबिन्दवः॥
en: And from Dhruvasandhi sprung the famous Bharata. And from Bharata sprung Asita; to fight whom were born as hostile kings, those heroes, the Haihayas, the Talajanghas, and the Sasabindus.

sa_verse: तांश्च स प्रतियुध्यन् वै युद्धे राजा प्रवासितः। हिमवन्तमुपागम्य भार्याभ्यां सहितस्तदा॥
en: And engaged in conflict with them, that king fled (from his kingdom); and repairing to the Himavat in company with his two consorts, the feeble Asita there paid his debt to nature.

sa_verse: असितोऽल्पबलो राजा कालधर्ममुपेयिवान्। द्वे चास्य भार्ये गर्भिण्यौ बभूवतुरिति श्रुतिः॥ एका गर्भविनाशार्थं सपत्न्यै सगरं ददौ।
en: The story runs that his two wives were in the family way; and that with the intention of destroying the embryo of the other, one of them administered poison to the former mixed in her food.

sa_verse: ततः शैलवरे रम्ये बभूवाभिरतो मुनिः॥ भार्गवश्च्यवनो नाम हिमवन्तमुपाश्रितः। तत्र चैका महाभागा भार्गवं देववर्चसम्॥ ववन्दे पद्मपत्राक्षी काङ्क्षन्ती सुतमुत्तमम्। तमृषि साभ्युपागम्य कालिन्दी चाभ्यवादयत्॥
en: And it came to pass that at this time, Bhrgu's son, the ascetic Cyavana, had become addicted to the romantic Himavat, foremost of mountains. And here one of those exalted dames with eyes resembling lotus-petals, saluting Bhrigu's son born shining like a celestial, desired of him an excellent. son. And drawing near to that sage, Kalindi saluted him.

sa_verse: स तामभ्यवदद् विप्रः पुत्रेप्सुं पुत्रजन्मनि । तव कुक्षौ महाभागे सुपुत्रः सुमहाबलः॥ महावीर्यो महातेजा अचिरात् संजनिष्यति। गरेण सहितः श्रीमान् मा शुचः कमलेक्षणे॥
en: And that Vipra said to her, who was desiring of having a son born of her,-In your womb, O exalted one, will be speedily born an excellent son mixed up with poison, highly powerful, and exceedingly energetic, and possessed of mighty strength, and graceful.

sa_verse: च्यवनं च नमस्कृत्य राजपुत्री पतिव्रता। पत्या विरहिता तस्मात् पुत्रं देवी व्यजायत॥
en: Therefore, do you not grieve, O you of lotuseyes. And having paid reverence to Cyavana, that chaste and worshipful princess, although bereft of her husband, gave birth to a son.

sa_verse: सपत्न्या तु गरस्तस्यै दत्तो गर्भजिघांसया। सह तेन गरेणैव संजातः सगरोऽभवत्॥
en: And since intending to destroy her foetus she that was co-wife with her had administered poison to her, Sagara* was born together with the poison. *Gara-poison. Sagara means, with poison, i.e. here, one born with poison.

sa_verse: सगरस्यासमञ्जस्तु असमञ्जादथांशुमान्। दिलीपोंऽशुमतः पुत्रो दिलीपस्य भगीरथः॥
en: And Sagara's son was Asamanja, and Ansumat. And Dilīpa was son to Ansumat, and Bhagiratha to Dilīpa.

sa_verse: भगीरथात् ककुत्स्थश्च ककुत्स्थाच्च रघुस्तथा। रघोस्तु पुत्रस्तेजस्वी प्रवृद्धः पुरुषादकः॥
en: And from Bhagiratha sprang Kākutstha, and from Kākutstha, Raghu. And Raghu's son was the puissant Pravşdha, feeding on human flesh.

sa_verse: कल्माषपादोऽप्यभवत् तस्माज्जातस्तु शङ्खणः। सुदर्शनः शङ्खणस्य अग्निवर्णः सुदर्शनात्॥
en: And he came finally to be known by the name of Kalmāşapāda.* And from him sprung Śarkhana. And Sudarśana was Sankhana's and Agnivarna was Sudaśana's son. He incurred Vasiştha's curse, and was turned into a Rākşasa. He took up water, intending to clear scores with Vasistha; but at the request of his wife, desisted, pouring down the water at his own feet. Hence the name of Kalmāşapāda.

sa_verse: शीघ्रगस्त्वग्निवर्णस्य शीघ्रगस्य मरुः सुतः। मरोः प्रशुश्रुकस्त्वासीदम्बरीषः प्रशुश्रुकात्॥
en: Śīghraga was Agnivarņa’s, and Maru was Śīghraga's son. And Maru's son was Prasuśruka, and from Praśuśruka sprung Ambarīşa.

sa_verse: अम्बरीषस्य पुत्रोऽभूनहुषश्च महीपतिः। नहुषस्य ययातिस्तु नाभागस्तु ययातिजः॥ नाभागस्य बभूवाज अजाद् दशरथोऽभवत्। अस्माद् दशरथाज्जातौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Ambarisa's son was Nahusa, lord of earth. And Nahusa's son was Yayāti, was Nābhāga. And Nābhāga's son was Aja, and from Aja sprung Dasaratha. And from this Dasaratha have come the brothers Rāma and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: आदिवंशविशुद्धानां राज्ञां परमधर्मिणाम्। इक्ष्वाकुकुलजातानां वीराणां सत्यवादिनाम्॥ रामलक्ष्मणयोरर्थे त्वत्सुते वरये नृप। सदृशाभ्यां नरश्रेष्ठ सदृशे दातुमर्हसि॥
en: It is in the interests of Rāma and Lakşmaņa belonging to the heroic and truthful and pious sovereigns sprung in the Ikşvāku line, and possessing purity of race even from the time of their founder, that, Oking, we solicit the hands of your daughters. And, O foremost of men, it be-hove you to confer like brides upon like bridegrooms.

sa_verse: राजाभूत् त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतः स्वेन कर्मणा। निमिः परमधर्मात्मा सर्वसत्त्ववतां वरः॥
en: There was a king famed over the three worlds by his own acts, Nimi-eminently picus and the foremost of those endowed with strength.

sa_verse: तस्य पुत्रो मिथिर्नाम जनको मिथिपुत्रकः। प्रथमो जनको राजा जनकादप्युदावसुः॥
en: His son was named Mithi, and Mithi's son was Janaka. And from this king Janaka have we son was derived that word as applied to every one of us. And from Janaka sprang Udāvasu;

sa_verse: उदावसोस्तु धर्मात्मा जातो वै नन्दिवर्धनः। नन्दिवर्धसुतः शूरः सुकेतुर्नाम नामतः॥
en: Udāvasu's the pious-souled Nandivardhana. And Nandivardha's son was the heroic Suketu.

sa_verse: सुकेतोरपि धर्मात्मा देवरातो महाबलः। देवरातस्य राजर्षेङ्घहद्रथ इति स्मृतः॥
en: Suketu's son was the mighty and righteous Devarata. And the Rājarși Devarāta's son was Brhadratha.

sa_verse: बृहद्रथस्य शूरोऽभून्महावीरः प्रतापवान्। महावीरस्य धृतिमान् सुधृतिः सत्यविक्रमः॥
en: Bịhadratha's son was the heroic and puissant Mahāvīra. And Mahāvīra's son was Sudhști, endowed with fortitude and having truth for prowess.

sa_verse: सुधृतेरति धर्मात्मा धृष्टकेतुः सुधार्मिकः। धृष्टकेतोश्च राजर्षेर्हर्यश्व इति विश्रुतः॥
en: Sudhrtia's son was the pious-spirited and eminently righteous Dhīșțaketu. And the Rājarși Dhistaketu's son was Haryasva.

sa_verse: हर्यश्वस्य मरुः पुत्रो मरोः पुत्रः प्रतीन्धकः। प्रतीन्धकस्य धर्मात्मा राजा कीर्तिरथः सुतः॥
en: Haryaśva's son was Maru; and Maru's son was Pratīndhaka. And Pratīndhaka's son was the righteous king Kirtiratha.

sa_verse: पुत्रः कीर्तिरथस्यापि देवमीढ इति स्मृतः। देवमीढस्य विबुधो विबुधस्य महीध्रकः॥
en: Kirtiratha's Devamidha, and Devamidha's Vibudha, and Vibudha's Mahidhraka.

sa_verse: महीध्रकसुतो राजा कीर्तिरातो महाबलः। कीर्तिरातस्य राजर्षेर्महारोमा व्यजायत॥
en: Mahīdhraka's son was king Kirtirāta endowed with great strength. And the Rājarşi Kirtirāta had Mahāroman born to him.

sa_verse: महारोम्णस्तु धर्मात्मा स्वर्णरोमा व्यजायत। स्वर्णरोम्णस्तु राजर्षेर्हस्वरोमा व्यजायत॥
en: Mahāroman, the virtuous Svarnaroman. And the Rājarşi Svarnaroman had Hrasvaroman born to him. son was

sa_verse: तस्य पुत्रद्वयं राज्ञो धर्मज्ञस्य महात्मनः। ज्येष्ठोऽहमनुजो भ्राता मम वीरः कुशध्वजः॥ मांत ज्येष्ठं पिता राज्ये सोऽभिषिच्य पिता मम। कुशध्वजं समावेश्य भारं मयि वनं गतः॥
en: This high-souled king conversant with morality had two sons: the elder, myself the younger, even my brother, who was the elder son, and consigning to my care Kusadhvaja, our father sought the forest.

sa_verse: वृद्धे पितरि स्वर्याते धर्मेण धुरमावहम्। भ्रातरं देवसंकाशं स्नेहात् पश्यन् कुशध्वजम्॥
en: And on my aged sire ascending heaven, I righteously ruled the kingdom and cherished my brother Kusadvaja resembling a celestial, with the eye of affection.

sa_verse: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य सांकाश्यादागतः पुरात्। सुधन्वा वीर्यवान् राजा मिथिलामवरोधकः॥
en: It came to pass that on one occasion a certain powerful king named Sudhanvan came before Mithilā intending to lay siege to it.

sa_verse: स च मे प्रेषयामास शैवं धनुरनुत्तमम्। सीता च कन्या पद्माक्षी मह्यं वै दीयतामिति॥
en: He sent word to me, saying, 'Do you give me the all-excellent bow of Śiva, as well as your daughter, the lotus-even Sītā.'

sa_verse: तस्याप्रदानान्महर्षे युद्धमासीन्मया सह । स हतोऽभिमुखो राजा सुधन्वा तु मया रणे॥
en: And is consequence of my not granting him either, king Sudhanvan, O Brahmarsi, entered into hostilities with me; but he was both defeated and slain by me in the encounter.

sa_verse: निहत्य तं मुनिश्रेष्ठ सुधन्वानं नराधिपम्। सांकाश्ये भ्रातरं शूरमभ्यषिञ्च कुशध्वजम्॥
en: O foremost of ascetics, slaying king Sudhanvan. I installed in Sānkāśyā my heroic brother Kuśadhvaja.

sa_verse: कनीयानेष मे भ्राता अहं ज्येष्ठो महामुने। ददामि परमप्रीतो वध्वौ ते मुनिपुङ्गव॥
en: Thus one, O mighty anchorite, is my younger brother, and I am his elder. O powerful ascetic, well pleased will I confer on you these as your daughter-in-law,

sa_verse: सीतां रामाय भद्रं ते ऊर्मिलां लक्ष्मणाय वै। वीर्यशुल्कां मम सुतां सीतां सुरसुतोपमाम्॥ द्वितीयामुर्मिलां चैव त्रिर्वदामि न संशयः। ददामि परमप्रीतो वध्वौ ते मुनिपुङ्गव॥
en: Sītā on Rāma, good betide you, and Urmilā on Laksmana. And, I take oath thrice that, without doubt, I will with a glad heart confer upon you, O potent ascetic, as your daughters-in-law my second daughter Urmilā and also Sītā resembling the daughter of a celestial, having prowess assigned for her dower.

sa_verse: रामलक्ष्मणयो राजन् गोदानं कारयस्व ह। पितृकार्यं च भद्रं ते ततो वैवाहिकं कुरु॥
en: do you now, O king, give away kine on behalf of the nuptials of Rāma and Lakşmaņa; and also perform their ancestral rites, good to you; and then complete the marriage ceremony.

sa_verse: मघा ह्यद्य महाबाहो तृतीयदिवो प्रभो। फल्गुन्यामुत्तरे राजेस्तस्मिन् वैवाहिकं कुरु। रामलक्ष्मणयोरर्थे दानं कार्यं सुखोदयम्॥
en: To-day the star Magha is on the ascendant. On the third day, my master, when the Phalguna will be on the north, do you, O monarch, perform the marriage ceremony. Do you now, however, dispense gifts for invoking blessings upon Rāma and Laksmana.24

sa_verse: तमुक्तवन्तं वैदेहं विश्वामित्रो महामुनिः। उवाच वचनं वीरं वसिष्ठसहितो नृपम्॥
en: When Vaideha had spoken thus, the mighty ascetic Viśvāmitra is company with Vasiștha addressed that heroic king, saying.

sa_verse: अचिन्त्यान्यप्रमेयाणि कुलानि नरपुङ्गव। इक्ष्वाकूणां विदेहानां नैषां तुल्योऽस्ति कश्चन॥
en: O puissant one, the lines of the Ikşvākus and the Vaidehas are exceedingly noble and incomparable. No other race can by any means compare with them.

sa_verse: सदृशो धर्मसम्बन्धः सदृशो रूपसम्पदा। रामलक्ष्मणयो राजन् सीता चोर्मिलया सह॥
